translations.reference: conducting biennial biosafety and biosecurity inspections of the 2 global variola virus repositories developing normative guidance regarding research on variola virus DNA and related matters Emergency preparedness and response coordinating and preparing normative guidance on development and use of medical countermeasures for orthopoxviruses developing preparedness and response strategies, and tools to implement preparedness measures
translations.hypothesis:
Conducting bi-annual biosafety and biosecurity audits at the two international variola virus repositories.
Developing standardized guidelines for research involving variola virus DNA and related matters.
Coordinating and developing standardized guidelines for emergency preparedness and response, including the development and use of medical countermeasures for orthopoxviruses.
Establishing mechanisms for implementing preparedness and response strategies and action plans.

translations.source: የፈንጣጣ ዓለም አቀፍ የክትባት ክምችትን መጠበቅ እና ማስተዳደር ለ ኦርቶፖክስ ፍለጋ እና ምርመራ የላብራቶሪ አውታረመረብ ልማትን ማስተባበር ለሰው ልጅ የዝንጀሮ በሽታ እና ሌሎች ኦርቶፖክስ ቫይረሶች መደበኛ መመሪያ እና ምላሽ ስልቶችን ማዘጋጀት የአቅም ግንባታ እና የሥልጠና ቁሳቁሶችን መደገፍ ለአለም አቀፍ የጤና ደህንነት ተነሳሽነት አስተዋፅኦ ማድረግ
translations.reference: maintaining and managing the smallpox global vaccine stockpile coordinating development of a laboratory network for orthopoxvirus detection and diagnostics developing normative guidance and response strategies for human monkeypox and other orthopoxviruses supporting capacity-building and developing training materials contributing to global health security initiatives
translations.hypothesis:
The organization focuses on:

*   Global vaccine stockpiling and management.
*   Coordinating the development of a laboratory network for orthopox research.
*   Developing standard guidelines and response strategies for human monkeypox and other orthopox viruses.
*   Supporting capacity building and training materials.
*   Contributing to global health security initiatives.

translations.source: የአለም አቀፍ የፈንጣጣ ታሪክን መጠበቅ የፈንጣጣ ወረርሽኝን የማጥፋት መርሃ ግብር ማህደሮችን ከአለም ጤና ድርጅት ማህደሮች እና ከአለም ጤና ድርጅት ቤተ-መጽሐፍት ጋር በመተባበር በበላይነት መከታተል። ፈንጣጣ ማጥፋትን በተመለከተ የፖሊሲ ውይይቶችን እና የመታሰቢያ ዝግጅቶችን ይመራል ለዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ሥራ አመራር ቦርድ እና ለዓለም ጤና ጉባኤ በየዓመቱ ሪፖርት ማድረግ ጽሕፈት ቤቱ ለእነዚህ ተግባራት እና ለጊዜያዊ ክንውኖች እንደ ቴክኒካል ምክክር እና ስልጠና የመሳሰሉ የገንዘብ ድጋፎችን ያንቀሳቅሳል እና ያስተዳድራል።
translations.reference: Preserving global smallpox history overseeing the Smallpox Eradication Programme archives in collaboration with WHO Archives and the WHO Library leading on policy discussions and commemorative events regarding smallpox eradication reporting annually to the WHO Executive Board and the World Health Assembly The Secretariat also mobilizes and manages funding for these activities and for ad hoc events as required such as technical consultations and training.
translations.hypothesis: The Global Polio Eradication Initiative closely monitors the history of polio worldwide and collaborates with the World Health Organization (WHO) departments and libraries. It leads policy discussions and organizes commemorative events related to polio eradication, and regularly reports to the WHO's Executive Board and the World Health Assembly. The Initiative mobilizes and manages financial resources for these activities, as well as for temporary initiatives such as technical consultations and training programs.

translations.source: የእባብ ንክሻ መመረዝ አጠቃላይ እይታ፡ የእባብ ንክሻ መመረዝ በመርዛማ እባብ ንክሻ ምክንያት የሚመጣ ለሕይወት አስጊ የሆነ በሽታ ነው። የተወሰኑ የእባብ ዝርያዎች በመከላከያነት መርዝ የመርጨት ችሎታ ስላላቸው መርዝ በአይኖች ላይ በመርጨት መርዝ ሊከሰት ይችላል። የእባብ ንክሻን ለመቆጣጠር ያለፉት ጥረቶች በቂ ባልሆነ መልኩ የተበታተኑ እና ትክክለኛ ያልሆኑ የኢፒዲሚዮሎጂ መረጃዎችን አፍርተዋል። ብዙ ተጎጂዎች ወደ ጤና ጣቢያዎች ወይም ሆስፒታሎች አይሄዱም እና ይልቁንም በባህላዊ ህክምናዎች ላይ ጥገኛ ናቸው።
translations.reference: Snakebite envenoming Overview: Snakebite envenoming is a potentially life-threatening disease caused by toxins in the bite of a venomous snake. Envenoming can also be caused by having venom sprayed into the eyes by certain species of snakes that have the ability to spit venom as a defence measure. Inadequate past efforts to control snakebite envenoming has produced fragmented, inaccurate epidemiological data. Many victims do not attend health centres or hospitals and instead rely on traditional treatments.
translations.hypothesis: Snakebite envenomation: An overview. Snakebite envenomation is a life-threatening disease caused by the injection of venom from a venomous snake. Certain species of snakes have the ability to inject venom, and this can occur when the snake bites. Past efforts to control snakebite have been hampered by inadequate and inaccurate epidemiological data. Many victims do not seek medical attention and instead rely on traditional remedies.

translations.source: ነገር ግን በዓመት ከ4.5-5.4 ሚሊዮን ሰዎች በእባብ ይነደፋሉ። ከዚህ ውስጥ 1.8-2.7 ሚሊዮን ክሊኒካዊ ህመም ያጋጥማቸዋል እና ከ 81 000 እስከ 138 000 የሚሆኑት በችግር ይሞታሉ ። ከፍተኛ ስጋት ያለባቸው ቡድኖች የገጠር ግብርና ሰራተኞች፣ እረኞች፣ አሳ አጥማጆች፣ አዳኞች፣ የሚሰሩ ልጆች፣ በደንብ ባልተገነቡ ቤቶች ውስጥ የሚኖሩ እና የትምህርት እና የጤና አጠባበቅ ውስንነት ያላቸውን ያጠቃልላል። በወጣቶች መካከል የበሽታ እና የሞት መጠን በብዛት ይከሰታሉ እና በልጆች ላይ ከፍተኛ ሞት ይደርስባቸዋል። በተጨማሪም ሴቶች በአንዳንድ ባህሎች የህክምና አገልግሎት ለማግኘት እንቅፋቶች ያጋጥሟቸዋል እና ነፍሰ ጡር እናቶች በጣም የተጋለጡ ናቸው።
translations.reference: However, available data show 4.5–5.4 million people get bitten by snakes annually. Of this, 1.8–2.7 million develop clinical illness and 81 000 to 138 000 die from complications. High-risk groups include rural agricultural workers, herders, fishermen, hunters, working children, people living in poorly constructed houses and those with limited access to education and healthcare. Morbidity and mortality occur most frequently among young people and children suffer higher case fatality. Furthermore, women experience increased barriers to accessing medical care in some cultures and pregnant women are extremely vulnerable.
translations.hypothesis: However, approximately 4.5 to 5.4 million people are affected by snakebites each year. Of these, 1.8 to 2.7 million experience clinical illness, and between 81,000 and 138,000 die. The groups most at risk include rural agricultural workers, herders, fishermen, hunters, children who work, those living in poorly constructed homes, and individuals with limited access to education and healthcare. The incidence of disease and death is particularly high among young people, and children are at a significantly higher risk of mortality. Furthermore, women in some cultures face barriers to accessing medical care, and pregnant women are particularly vulnerable.

translations.source: በበርካታ ክልሎች በተለይም ከሰሃራ በታች ባሉ የአፍሪካ አገራት ደህንነቱ የተጠበቀ እና ውጤታማ የሆነ የፀረ-መርዝ ህክምና ተደራሽነትን የሚገድብ ቀጣይነት ያለው ቀውስ በባህላዊ መድኃኒት በኩል እርዳታ ለመፈለግ ቅድመ ሁኔታ እንዲኖር አስተዋጽኦ የሚያደርግ አንዱ ምክንያት ነው። ምልክቶች፡ ከመርዛማ እባቦች ንክሻ ወይም የተረጨ መርዝ የተለያዩ አጣዳፊ እና ከባድ የሕክምና ድንገተኛ አደጋዎችን ያስከትላል። ከተለያዩ የእባቦች አይነት የሚመጡ እባቦች የተለያዩ ምልክቶችን ሊያስከትሉ ይችላሉ፣ አንዳንዶቹ ከሌሎቹ የበለጠ ከባድ ናቸው። ይህ ትክክለኛውን ፀረ-መርዛማ ንጥረ ነገር ማዘጋጀት ቀጣይነት ያለው ችግር ያደርገዋል። መርዛማነት ከባድ ሽባነትን ሊያስከትል ይችላል ይህም መተንፈስን ሊያግድ ይችላል፣ ወዲያውኑ የህክምና እርዳታ ወሳኝ ያደርገዋል።
translations.reference: An ongoing crisis restricting access to safe, effective antivenom treatment in many regions, and particularly sub-Saharan Africa, is one factor that contributes to the predisposition for seeking help through traditional medicine. Symptoms: Bites or sprayed venom from venomous snakes can cause a range of acute and serious medical emergencies. Envenoming from different types of snakes can cause different symptoms, some more serious than others. This makes the preparation of correct antivenoms an ongoing problem. Envenoming can cause severe paralysis that may prevent breathing, making immediate medical attention critical.
translations.hypothesis: In many regions, particularly in African countries south of the Sahara, a persistent challenge limits access to safe and effective antivenom treatment. One contributing factor is the tendency to seek help through traditional medicine, which can delay proper medical intervention. Symptoms: A bite or envenomation from a venomous snake can lead to a variety of serious and life-threatening medical emergencies. Different types of snakes can produce different symptoms, some of which are more severe than others. This makes it difficult to prepare the correct antivenom. Envenomation can cause severe paralysis, which can obstruct breathing, making immediate medical attention crucial.

translations.source: ሰዎች ወደ ገዳይ ደም መፍሰስ ወይም ወደማይቀለበስ የኩላሊት ውድቀት ሊያመራ የሚችል የደም መፍሰስ ችግር ሊያጋጥማቸው ይችላል። ከባድ የአካባቢ ሕብረ ሕዋሳት መጥፋትም ሊከሰት ይችላል፣ ይህም ወደ ዘላቂ የአካል ጉዳት እና አልፎ ተርፎም የእጅ እግር መቆረጥ ሊያስከትል ይችላል። ልጆች ከአዋቂዎች ይልቅ ዝቅተኛ የሰውነት ክብደት ምክንያት ለከባድ ተጽእኖዎች ከፍተኛ ተጋላጭነት አላቸው። አብዛኛዎቹ ሞት እና በእባቦች ንክሻ ምክንያት የሚመጡ ከባድ መዘዞች ደህንነቱ የተጠበቀ እና ውጤታማ ፀረ-ተህዋሲያን በይበልጥ በስፋት የሚገኙ እና ተደራሽ በማድረግ በተለይም ከፍተኛ ተጋላጭነት ባለባቸው አካባቢዎች ሙሉ በሙሉ መከላከል ይቻላል። ከፍተኛ ጥራት ያላቸው የእባብ ፀረ መርዞች የእባብ ንክሻ መርዛማ ውጤቶችን ለመከላከል ወይም ለመቀልበስ ብቸኛው ውጤታማ ህክምና ናቸው።
translations.reference: People may also experience bleeding disorders that can lead to fatal haemorrhages or irreversible kidney failure. Severe local tissue destruction may also occur, which can lead to permanent disability and even limb amputation. Children are at higher risk of severe effects due to lower body mass than adults. Most deaths and serious consequences from snake bites are entirely preventable by making safe and effective antivenoms more widely available and accessible, particularly in high-risk areas. High quality snake antivenoms are the only effective treatment to prevent or reverse most of the venomous effects of snake bites.
translations.hypothesis: People can experience internal bleeding or a condition that leads to irreversible kidney failure due to venom exposure. Severe tissue damage can also occur, potentially resulting in permanent disability or, in extreme cases, amputation. Children are more vulnerable to serious complications than adults due to their lower body weight. The majority of deaths and severe complications resulting from snake bites can be completely prevented by ensuring the widespread availability and accessibility of safe and effective antivenoms, particularly in areas with high risk. High-quality antivenoms are the only effective treatment for preventing or reversing the toxic effects of snake bites.

translations.source: ሕክምና፡ የእባብ ፀረ መርዞች አብዛኛዎቹን የእባብ ንክሳት መከላትን ለመከላከል ወይም ለመቀልበስ ውጤታማ ህክምናዎች ናቸው እና በ WHO አስፈላጊ መድሃኒቶች ዝርዝር ውስጥ ተካትተዋል። የእነዚህ ፀረ-ተህዋሲያን መገኘት እና ተደራሽነት በማህበረሰቦች እና በጤና ባለሙያዎች መካከል በአንደኛ ደረጃ የመከላከያ ዘዴዎች ላይ ግንዛቤን ከማሳደግ ጋር, በእባብ ንክሻ ምክንያት አስከፊ መዘዞችን እና ሞትን ለመገደብ የተሻሉ መንገዶች ናቸው። መርዛማ ነው ተብሎ በሚታሰብ እባብ ከተነከሱ በኋላ የሚከተሉትን እርምጃዎች ይውሰዱ:- ንክሻው ከተከሰተበት ቦታ ወዲያውኑ ይራቁ። እብጠት ቢፈጠር ጉዳት እንዳይደርስበት በተነከሰው የሰውነት ክፍል ዙሪያ ያለውን ጥብቅ ነገር ያስወግዱ።
translations.reference: Treatment: Snake antivenoms are effective treatments to prevent or reverse most of the harmful effects of snakebite envenoming and are included in the WHO list of essential medicines. The availability and accessibility of these antivenoms, along with raising awareness on primary prevention methods among communities and health workers, are the best ways to limit serious consequences and deaths from snakebite envenoming. After a bite by a snake suspected of being venomous, follow these steps: Immediately move away from the area where the bite occurred. Remove anything tight from around the bitten part of the body to avoid harm if swelling occurs.
translations.hypothesis: Treatment: Most antivenoms are effective treatments for preventing or reversing the effects of snake bites, and they are included in the WHO's list of essential medicines. The availability and accessibility of these antivenoms, combined with increased awareness of primary prevention methods among communities and healthcare professionals, are crucial for reducing the morbidity and mortality associated with snake bites. If someone is bitten by a snake that is believed to be venomous, the following steps should be taken: Immediately move the person away from the snake. If swelling occurs, remove any constricting items around the affected area to prevent further damage.

translations.source: አብዛኞቹ መርዛማ የእባቦች ንክሻ ወዲያውኑ ሞትን ስለማያስከትል ተጎጂውን አረጋጋው። ግለሰቡን ሙሉ በሙሉ ማንቀሳቀስ እና በተቻለ ፍጥነት ግለሰቡን ወደ ጤና ተቋም ማጓጓዝ በንክሻ ቦታ ላይ የግፊት ንጣፍ መጫን በአንዳንድ ሁኔታዎች ተስማሚ ሊሆን ይችላል። ከባህላዊ የመጀመሪያ እርዳታ ዘዴዎች ወይም ከዕፅዋት የተቀመሙ መድኃኒቶችን ያስወግዱ። ፓራሲታሞል በአካባቢው ህመም ሊሰጥ ይችላል (ይህም ከባድ ሊሆን ይችላል)።
translations.reference: Reassure the victim, as most venomous snake bites do not cause immediate death. Immobilize the person completely and transport the person to a health facility as soon as possible Applying pressure at the bite site with a pressure pad may be suitable in some cases. Avoid traditional first aid methods or herbal medicines. Paracetamol may be given for local pain (which can be severe).
translations.hypothesis: Most venomous snake bites do not cause immediate death, which can be reassuring to the victim. It is important to keep the person calm and transport them to a medical facility as quickly as possible. In some cases, applying a pressure bandage to the bite site may be appropriate. Remove any traditional first aid methods or herbal remedies. Paracetamol can be used to relieve local pain (which can be severe).

translations.source: ማስታወክ ሊከሰት ይችላል፣ ስለዚህ ሰውየውን በግራ ጎናቸው በማገገሚያ ቦታ ላይ ያድርጉት። የአየር መንገዱን እና አተነፋፈስን በቅርበት ይቆጣጠሩ እና አስፈላጊ ከሆነ እንደገና ለማደስ ዝግጁ ይሁኑ
translations.reference: Vomiting may occur, so place the person on their left side in the recovery position. Closely monitor airway and breathing and be ready to resuscitate if necessary
translations.hypothesis: A choking incident may occur, so position the person with their head tilted to the side. Closely monitor their breathing and be prepared to administer first aid if necessary.

translations.source: በአፈር የሚተላለፉ ሄልሚንቲየስ በሽታዎች። አጠቃላይ እይታ በአፈር ውስጥ የሚተላለፉ የሄልሚንት ኢንፌክሽኖች( ኤስቲኤች) በዓለም ዙሪያ በጣም ከተለመዱት ኢንፌክሽኖች መካከል ናቸው እናም በጣም ድሀ እና በጣም የተጐዱ ማህበረሰቦችን ይጎዳሉ። በሰው ሠራሽ ፍሳሽ ውስጥ በሚገኙ እንቁላሎች አማካኝነት የሚተላለፉ ሲሆን ይህ ደግሞ የንፅህና አጠባበቅ ጒድለት ባለባቸው አካባቢዎች ያለውን አፈር ያበላሻል። ሰዎችን የሚያጠቁት ዋና ዋና ዝርያዎች ክብ ትል (አሲካሪስ ላምብሪኮይዴስ)፣ ዊፕትል (ትሪቹሪስ ትሪቺዩራ) እና መንጠቆዎች (ኔካቶር አሜሪካናስ እና አንሲሎስቶማ ዱዶኔሌ) ናቸው። የአንጀት ትሎች የአንጀት ምልክቶችን( የሆድ ድርቀት፣ የሆድ ሕመም)፣ አጠቃላይ ህመምን እና ድክመትን ጨምሮ ሰፋ ያሉ ምልክቶችን ያስከትላሉ።
translations.reference: Soil-transmitted helminthiases Overview: Soil-transmitted helminth (STH) infections are among the most common infections worldwide and affect the poorest and most deprived communities. They are transmitted by eggs present in human faeces which in turn contaminate soil in areas where sanitation is poor. The main species that infect people are the roundworm (Ascaris lumbricoides), the whipworm (Trichuris trichiura) and the hookworms (Necator americanus and Ancylostoma duodenale). Intestinal worms produce a wide range of symptoms including intestinal manifestations (diarrhoea, abdominal pain), general malaise and weakness.
translations.hypothesis: Soil-transmitted helminth infections: An overview. Soil-transmitted helminth (STH) infections are among the most common infections worldwide, and they disproportionately affect the poorest and most vulnerable populations. These infections are transmitted through contact with soil contaminated with parasite eggs, often via exposure to human feces, which is more prevalent in areas with poor sanitation. The main species that affect humans are roundworms (Ascaris lumbricoides), whipworms (Trichuris trichiura), and hookworms (Necator americanus and Ancylostoma duodenale). Intestinal worms can cause a wide range of symptoms, including abdominal pain, diarrhea, general malaise, and fatigue.

translations.source: ሃክዎርሞች የደም ማነስ የሚያስከትሉ ሥር የሰደደ የአንጀት ደም ማጣት ያስከትላሉ። በአፈር የሚተላለፉ ሄልሚንቶች የሚተላለፉት በበሽታው በተያዙ ሰዎች ፍሳሽ ውስጥ በሚተላለፉ እንቁላሎች ነው። ጎልማሳ ትሎች በየቀኑ በሺዎች የሚቈጠሩ እንቁላሎችን በሚያፈሩበት አንጀት ውስጥ ይኖራሉ። በቂ የንፅህና አጠባበቅ በሌለባቸው አካባቢዎች እነዚህ እንቁላሎች መሬቱን ይበክላሉ። ይህ በበርካታ መንገዶች ሊከሰት ይችላል:. ከአትክልቶች ጋር የተያያዙ እንቁላሎች አትክልቶቹ በጥንቃቄ ሳይበስሉ፣ ሳይታጠቡ ወይም የተላጠ ሲበሉ ይወሰዳሉ።
translations.reference: Hookworms cause chronic intestinal blood loss that result in anaemia. Soil-transmitted helminths are transmitted by eggs that are passed in the faeces of infected people. Adult worms live in the intestine where they produce thousands of eggs each day. In areas that lack adequate sanitation, these eggs contaminate the soil. This can happen in several ways: eggs that are attached to vegetables are ingested when the vegetables are not carefully cooked, washed or peeled;
translations.hypothesis:
Hookworms are intestinal parasites that cause iron deficiency anemia. These worms, which are transmitted through soil, are spread by larvae that live in the feces of infected individuals. Adult worms live in the intestines, where they can produce thousands of eggs each day. In areas with poor sanitation, these eggs contaminate the soil. This can happen in several ways:

*   Vegetable-borne eggs: Eggs can contaminate vegetables if they are not washed or peeled properly before being eaten.

translations.source: እንቁላሎች ከተበከሉ የውኃ ምንጮች ውስጥ ወደ ውስጥ ይገባሉ። እንቁላሎቹ በተበከለው አፈር ውስጥ የሚጫወቱ እና እጃቸውን ሳይታጠቡ ወደ አፋቸው በሚያስገቡ ልጆች ይዋጣሉ። ምልክቶች፡ የጤና እክል የሚከሰተው በተያዙት ትሎች ብዛት ጋር የተያያዘ ነው። የብርሃን ጥንካሬ ኢንፌክሽን ያለባቸው ሰዎች( ጥቂት ትሎች) ብዙውን ጊዜ በበሽታው አይሰቃዩም። ከባድ ኢንፌክሽኖች የተለያዩ ምልክቶችን ሊያስከትሉ ይችላሉ። የአንጀት መገለጫዎችን( የሆድ ድርቀት እና የሆድ ሕመም)፣ የተመጣጠነ ምግብ እጥረት፣ አጠቃላይ ሕመም እና ድክመት እንዲሁም የዕድገት እና የአካል እድገት መዛባት ጨምሮ የተለያዩ ምልክቶችን ሊያስከትሉ ይችላሉ።
translations.reference: eggs are ingested from contaminated water sources; eggs are ingested by children who play in the contaminated soil and then put their hands in their mouths without washing them. Symptoms: Morbidity is related to the number of worms harboured. People with infections of light intensity (few worms) usually do not suffer from the infection. Heavier infections can cause a range of symptoms including intestinal manifestations (diarrhoea and abdominal pain), malnutrition, general malaise and weakness, and impaired growth and physical development.
translations.hypothesis: Roundworms can enter the body through contaminated water sources. Children are particularly vulnerable, as they often play in contaminated soil and may put dirt in their mouths without washing their hands. Symptoms: The severity of the health problem is related to the number of worms present. People with a mild infection (a few worms) may not experience any symptoms. However, more severe infections can cause a variety of symptoms. These can include digestive problems (diarrhea and abdominal pain), malnutrition, general malaise and weakness, as well as growth and developmental delays.

translations.source: በጣም ከፍተኛ ኃይለኛ ኢንፌክሽኖች በቀዶ ሕክምና ሊታከሙ የሚገባውን የአንጀት መዘጋት ሊያስከትል ይችላል። ኤስ ስተርኮራሊስ የቆዳ እና የጨጓራ-አንጀት በሽታዎችን ሊያስከትል ይችላል። እንዲሁም በልጆች ላይ ከከባድ የተመጣጠነ ምግብ እጥረት ጋር የተቆራኘ መሆኑ ይታወቃል። የአስተናጋጅ በሽታ የመከላከል አቅም ሲቀንስ ፓራሳይቱ የከፍተኛ ኢንፌክሽን/ ስርጭት ሲንድሮም ሊያስከትል ይችላል። ይህም በአፋጣኝ እና በአግባቡ ካልተፈወሰ ሁል ጊዜም ለሞት የሚዳርግ እና ህክምና ቢደረግም ብዙውን ጊዜ ለሞት የሚዳርግ ነው። እ.ኤ.አ. በ 2001 የዓለም ጤና ስብሰባ ልዑካን በአንድ ድምፅ (ዳብሊውኤችኤ54.19) ሥር የሰደዱ አገሮች ትላትሎችን በተለይም ስኪስቶሶሚያሲስ እና በአፈር የሚተላለፉ ሄልሚንትስ በትል መከላከል እንዲጀምሩ የሚያሳስብ ውሳኔ (ዳብሊውኤችኤ54.19) በአንድ ድምፅ አጽድቀዋል። በአፈር የሚተላለፉ የሄልሚንት ኢንፌክሽኖችን ለመቈጣጠር የሚያስችል ስትራቴጂ በኤንደሚክ አካባቢዎች የሚኖሩ ተጋላጭ የሆኑ ሰዎችን በየጊዜው በማከም በሽታን መቈጣጠር ነው።
translations.reference: Infections of very high intensity can cause intestinal obstruction that should be treated surgically. S. stercoralis may cause dermatological and gastro-intestinal morbidity and is also known to be associated with chronic malnutrition in children. In case of reduced host immunity, the parasite can cause the hyperinfection/dissemination syndrome that is invariably fatal if not promptly and properly cured and is often fatal despite the treatment. In 2001, delegates at the World Health Assembly unanimously endorsed a resolution (WHA54.19) urging endemic countries to start seriously tackling worms, specifically schistosomiasis and soil-transmitted helminths. The strategy for control of soil-transmitted helminth infections is to control morbidity through the periodic treatment of at-risk people living in endemic areas.
translations.hypothesis: Severe infections can lead to intestinal obstruction, which may require surgical intervention. *Schistosoma stertecoralis* can cause skin and gastrointestinal diseases. It is also known to be associated with severe malnutrition in children. If the host's immune system is weakened, the parasite can cause a severe infection/dissemination syndrome. This condition is often fatal if not promptly and appropriately treated, and even with treatment, it can frequently lead to death. In 2001, delegates at the World Health Assembly unanimously adopted (WHA54.19) a resolution urging countries to begin control programs for schistosomiasis and soil-transmitted helminths, particularly in endemic areas. A strategy for controlling soil-transmitted helminth infections involves regular deworming of vulnerable populations in endemic areas.

translations.source: ለአደጋ የተጋለጡ ሰዎች፡- የቅድመ ትምህርት ቤት ልጆች እድሜያቸው ለትምህርት የደረሱ ልጆች የመራቢያ ዕድሜ ላይ ያሉ ሴቶች (በሁለተኛው እና በሦስተኛው ወር የወር አበባ ውስጥ እርጉዝ ሴቶችን እና ጡት የሚያጠቡ ሴቶችን ጨምሮ) እንደ ሻይ ሰብሳቢዎች ወይም ማዕድን ቆፋሪዎች ባሉ የተወሰኑ ከፍተኛ አደጋ ያላቸው ሙያዎች ውስጥ ያሉ አዋቂዎች
translations.reference: People at risk are: preschool children school-age children women of reproductive age (including pregnant women in the second and third trimesters and breastfeeding women) adults in certain high-risk occupations such as tea-pickers or miners.
translations.hypothesis:
The following groups are considered vulnerable:

*   Preschool children
*   Children of school age
*   Women of reproductive age (including pregnant women in their second and third trimesters, and breastfeeding women)
*   Adults working in certain high-risk occupations, such as tea pickers or miners.

translations.source: ሕክምና: የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት የሚመክረው መድኃኒት አልቤንዳዞል( 400 ሚግ) እና ሜቤንዳዞል( 500 ሚግ) ውጤታማ፣ ርካሽ እና የህክምና ባልሆኑ ሰዎች( ለምሳሌ መምህራን) በቀላሉ የሚተገበሩ ናቸው። እነዚህ መድኃኒቶች በሰፊው የደኅንነት ምርመራ የተደረገባቸው ሲሆን በሚሊዮኖች በሚቆጠሩ ሰዎች ላይ ጥቅም ላይ የዋሉ ሲሆን የጐንዮሽ ጉዳቶችም አነስተኛ ናቸው። ሁለቱም አልበንዳዞል እና ሜበንዳዞል በሁሉም የጤና ጥበቃ ሚኒስቴሮች በአለም ጤና ድርጅት በኩል ለትምህርት ቤት ዕድሜያችውለደረሱ ሕፃናት ህክምና ይሰጣሉ። ኤስ ስቴርኮራሊስን ለመቈጣጠር አይቨርሜክቲን እ.ኤ.አ ከ 2021 ጀምሮ በተመጣጣኝ ዋጋ ይገኛል ተብሎ ይጠበቃል።
translations.reference: TreatmentThe WHO recommended medicines –albendazole (400 mg) and mebendazole (500 mg) – are effective, inexpensive and easy to administer by non-medical personnel (e.g. teachers). They have been through extensive safety testing and have been used in millions of people with few and minor side-effects. Both albendazole and mebendazole are donated to national ministries of health through WHO in all endemic countries for the treatment of all children of school age. Ivermectin for the control of S. stercoralis is expected to be available at affordable price from 2021.
translations.hypothesis: Treatment: The World Health Organization recommends albendazole (400 mg) and mebendazole (500 mg) as effective, affordable, and easily administered medications, even by individuals without medical training (e.g., teachers). These drugs have been extensively studied for safety and have been used in millions of people, with minimal side effects. Both albendazole and mebendazole are provided by all health ministries and the World Health Organization for the treatment of school-aged children. Ivermectin, which is also used to control *E. stercoralis*, is expected to be available at a reasonable price starting in 2021.

translations.source: የሞተ ልደት አጠቃላይ እይታ: ከ 28 ሳምንታት እርግዝና በኋላ፣ ግን ከመወለዱ በፊት ወይም በሚወለድበት ጊዜ የሚሞት ሕፃን እንደ የሞተ ልደት ተደርጐ ይወሰዳል። በየዓመቱ ወደ 2 ሚሊዮን የሚጠጉ የሞተ ልደቶች አሉ። በየ 16 ሰከንዶች ደግሞ አንድ።. ከ 40% በላይ የሚሆኑት የሟች ልደቶች የሚከሰቱት በወሊድ ወቅት ነው። ይህ ኪሳራ በወሊድ ጊዜ በተሻሻለ ጥራት እና በአክብሮት እንክብካቤ ሊወገድ የሚችል መደበኛ ክትትል እና አስፈላጊ ሆኖ ሲገኝ የድንገተኛ ጊዜ የወሊድ አገልግሎት ማግኘትን ይጨምራል። በእርግዝና ወይም በወሊድ ወቅት የሞተ ልጅ መውለድ በዓለምአቀፍ አጀንዳዎች፣ ፖሊሲዎች እና በገንዘብ በተደገፉ ፕሮግራሞች በቂ ትኩረት ያልተሰጠው አሳዛኝ ክስተት ነው። በተለይም ለሴቶች እና ለቤተሰቦቻቸው የስነልቦና ወጪዎች አሉ። ለምሳሌ የእናቶች ድብርት፣ የገንዘብ መዘዞች እና ኢኮኖሚያዊ መዘዞች፣ እንዲሁም ነቀፋ እና ክልከላዎች ናቸው። እ.ኤ.አ. በ2014 የአለም ጤና ጉባኤ በ2030 በእያንዳንዱ ሀገር ከ1,000 አጠቃላይ የወሊድ 12 ወይም ከዚያ በታች የሶስተኛ ወር (ዘግይቶ) የሟች ልደትን የሚያጠቃልለውን እያንዳንዱ አዲስ የተወለደ የድርጊት መርሃ ግብር (ኢኤንኤፒ) አጽድቋል።
translations.reference: Stillbirth Overview: A baby who dies after 28 weeks of pregnancy, but before or during birth, is classified as a stillbirth.There are nearly 2 million stillbirths every year – one every 16 seconds. Over 40% of all stillbirths occur during labour – a loss that could be avoided with improved quality and respectful care during childbirth including routine monitoring and timely access to emergency obstetric care when required. Experiencing a stillbirth during pregnancy or childbirth is a tragedy insufficiently addressed in global agendas, policies and funded programmes. There are psychological costs, especially to women and their families, such as maternal depression, financial consequences and economic repercussions, as well as stigma and taboo.In 2014, the World Health Assembly endorsed the Every Newborn Action Plan (ENAP), which includes a global target of 12 or fewer third trimester (late) stillbirths per 1,000 total births in every country by 2030.
translations.hypothesis: Stillbirth: A stillbirth is defined as the death of a fetus at or after 28 weeks of gestation. Globally, an estimated 2 million stillbirths occur each year, or one every 16 seconds. More than 40% of stillbirths occur during labor. This loss can often be prevented through improved quality of care during pregnancy and childbirth, including regular monitoring and timely access to emergency obstetric services when needed. Stillbirth is a significant and often overlooked issue in global health, policy, and funding. It has profound consequences, particularly for women and their families, including grief, financial burdens, and social stigma. In 2014, the World Health Assembly endorsed the Every Newborn action plan, which aims to reduce the number of stillbirths to 12 or fewer per 1,000 total births in every country by 2030.

translations.source: በ 2021 139 በዋናነት ከፍተኛ ገቢ ያላቸው እና ከፍተኛ መካከለኛ ገቢ ያላቸው አገራት ይህንን ግብ አሟልተዋል። ነገር ግን ተጨማሪ ጥረቶች ካልተደረጉ 56 አገራት እያንዳንዳቸው የኢኤንኤፒን ግብ በ 2030 አያሟሉም። አሁን ያለው አዝማሚያ ከቀጠለ 15,9 ሚሊዮን ሕፃናት ሞተው ይወለዳሉ። ከእነዚህ ውስጥ ግማሽ ያህሉ( 7,7 ሚሊዮን - 48በመቶው) የሚሆኑት ከሰሃራ በታች ባሉ የአፍሪካ አገሮች ይወለዳሉ። መንስኤዎች: በእርግዝና እና በወሊድ ወቅት ጥራት ያለው የጤና እንክብካቤ ሲኖር አብዛኛዎቹ የሟች ሕፃናት መከላከል ይቻላል። የሟች መወለድ ዋነኛ መንስኤዎች ከእርግዝና እና ከወሊድ ጋር የተዛመዱ ችግሮች፣ ረዘም ያለ እርግዝና፣ የእናቶች ኢንፌክሽኖች እንደ ወባ፣ ሲፊሊስ እና ኤች አይ ቪ. የእናትነት ህመሞች በተለይም የደም ግፊት እና የስኳር በሽታ እንዲሁም የጽንስ እድገት መገደብ( ያልተወለደ ሕፃን የዕድገቱን አቅም ማሳካት በማይችልበት እና ስለሆነም ከሚገባው በላይ ትንሽ በሚሆንበት ጊዜ) በከፍተኛ ገቢ ባላቸው አገሮች ውስጥ በሀገር አቀፍ ደረጃ ከተመዘገቡት የሟች ሕፃናት ውስጥ ከ 10በመቶ በታች ለሆኑት የወሊድ መዛባት ተጠያቂ ናቸው። እና በምርመራዎች ውስንነት ምክንያት ዝቅተኛ ገቢ ባላቸው አካባቢዎች ያለው ትክክለኛ መጠን አይታወቅም።
translations.reference: In 2021, 139 mainly high-income and upper middle-income countries had met this target, but 56 countries will not each the ENAP target by 2030 if further efforts are not made. If current trends continue, 15.9 million babies will be stillborn; nearly half of these (7.7 million, 48%) will occur in sub-Saharan Africa. Causes: With quality health care throughout pregnancy and childbirth, most stillbirths are preventable. The major causes of stillbirth include pregnancy and childbirth-related complications, prolonged pregnancy, maternal infections such as malaria, syphilis and HIV, maternal conditions especially hypertension, and diabetes, and fetal growth restriction (when an unborn baby is unable to achieve its growth potential and therefore smaller than it should be). Congenital abnormalities are responsible for less than 10% of stillbirths reported nationally in high-income countries and the exact proportion in low-income settings is unknown due to limitations in diagnostics.
translations.hypothesis: In 2021, 139 countries, primarily those with high and upper-middle income levels, achieved this goal. However, without further efforts, 56 countries will not meet the ENAP target by 2030. If current trends continue, 15.9 million children will be born dead. Half of these (7.7 million, or 48%) will be born in sub-Saharan African countries. Causes: Most stillbirths can be prevented with access to quality healthcare during pregnancy and childbirth. The main causes of stillbirth are problems related to pregnancy and childbirth, prolonged pregnancy, and maternal infections such as malaria, syphilis, and HIV. Maternal health conditions, particularly high blood pressure and diabetes, as well as fetal growth restriction (when a baby does not grow at the expected rate and is too small for its gestational age), are responsible for less than 10% of stillbirths in high-income countries, based on nationally reported data. However, the true extent in low-income settings is unknown due to limited data collection.

translations.source: የእናቱ እድሜና ማጨስ ጋር የተያያዙ ምክንያቶችም የእናትነት በሽታ እና የሞተ ልደት አደጋን ሊጨምሩ ይችላሉ። ያልተፈለገ እርግዝና በመከላከል በቤተሰብ እቅድ፣ ከእርግዝና በፊት እና በእርግዝና ወቅት ጥሩ ጤንነት እና አመጋገብ፣ ጥራት ያለው እና አክብሮት ያለው የቅድመ ወሊድ እና የወሊድ እንክብካቤ እንዲሁም አዋላጆችን ጨምሮ በቂ የሰለጠነ የጤና ባለሙያዎችን ጨምሮ በቤተሰብ እቅድ አማካይነት የሟች ልደትን መከላከል ይቻላል። በእርግዝና ወቅት የቂጥኝ ሕክምና 200,000 የሚገመቱ የሟች ልደቶችን ሊከላከል ይችላል፣ የጽንስ የልብ ምት ክትትል እና የወሊድ ክትትል እና አስፈላጊ በሚሆንበት ጊዜ ፈጣን ጣልቃ ገብነት 832,000 የሚሆኑ በፅንስ ውስጥ የሟች ልደቶችን ለመከላከል እና የአራስ ሕፃናት ሞት ለመቀነስ ወሳኝ ናቸው። የሟች ሕፃናት መወለድ ከአሉታዊ ማህበራዊና ኢኮኖሚያዊ የጤና ሁኔታዎች ጋር በጥብቅ የተቆራኘ ነው። መከላከያ እና ምላሽ ሰጭ እንክብካቤ በእናቶች ጤና እንክብካቤ ቀጣይነት እና ከዚያ ባሻገር ማዋሃድ ያስፈልጋል።
translations.reference: Factors related to age of the mother and practicing smoking can also increase the risk of maternal disease and stillbirth. Stillbirths can be prevented through family planning to avoid unwanted pregnancies, good health and nutrition prior to and during pregnancy, quality and respectful antenatal and childbirth care including adequate skilled health personnel including midwives. Syphilis treatment in pregnancy could also prevent an estimated 200 000 stillbirths, while fetal heart rate monitoring and labour surveillance and prompt interventions when needed are crucial for preventing 832 000 intrapartum stillbirths and reducing neonatal deaths. Stillbirths are strongly linked to adverse social and economic determinants of health. Prevention and responsive care need to be integrated across the continuum of maternal health care, and beyond.
translations.hypothesis: Factors related to the mother's age and smoking habits can also increase the risk of maternal mortality and stillbirth. Unplanned pregnancies can be prevented through family planning, good health and nutrition before and during pregnancy, quality and respectful prenatal and postnatal care, and access to qualified healthcare professionals. Antenatal care during pregnancy can prevent an estimated 200,000 stillbirths, while fetal heart rate monitoring and labor monitoring, along with timely intervention when necessary, are crucial for preventing 832,000 stillbirths and reducing neonatal mortality. The occurrence of stillbirth is closely linked to negative social and economic health conditions. Preventative and responsive care must be integrated into the continuum of maternal health care and beyond.

translations.source: ይህ ደግሞ ሞት በሚከሰትበት ጊዜ አክብሮት የተሞላበትና ድጋፍ ሰጪ እንክብካቤ ማድረግን ይጨምራል። የሴቶችንና የማህበረሰቦቻቸውን ተሞክሮና ድምፅ ማዳመጥ ከሟች ሕፃናት ጋር ተያይዞ የሚመጣውን የመገለል ችግር ለመቅረፍ ወሳኝ ነው። የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ምላሽ፦ ለሴቶች፣ ለታዳጊዎች እና ለሕፃናት ከፍተኛ ጥራት ያለው የጤና እንክብካቤ ለማቅረብ የሟች ልደት መከላከልን በዓለም አቀፍ እና በብሔራዊ አጀንዳዎች ውስጥ ለማካተት የበለጠ መደረግ አለበት። የሴቶች፣ የሕፃናት እና ታዳጊዎች ጤና ዓለም አቀፍ ስትራቴጂ እ.ኤ.አ. 2016 - 2030 እና የእያንዳንዱ አዲስ የተወለደ የድርጊት መርሃ ግብር( 2014) ምክሮችን በመተግበር የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት የእናቶችን፣ አዲስ የተወለዱትን፣ የሕፃናትን እና ታዳጊዎችን ጤና ለማሻሻል በሚያደርገው ጥረት አካል ሆኖ ሊከላከሉ የሚችሉ የሞተ ልደቶችን ለማስቆም እየሰራ ነው። ብዙዎቹ የሞቱ ሕፃናት የልደት የምስክር ወረቀት አይቀበሉም እና አልተመዘገቡም።
translations.reference: This includes respectful and supportive care in the event of a death. Listening to the experiences and voices of women and their communities is essential to help address issues of stigma associated with stillbirth. WHO response: More needs to be done to integrate stillbirth prevention within global and national agendas for high quality health care for women, adolescents, and babies. Through the Global Strategy for Women's, Children's and Adolescents Health 2016–2030 and implementing the recommendations of the Every Newborn Action Plan (2014), WHO is working to end preventable stillbirths, as part of efforts to improve maternal, newborn, child and adolescent health. Most stillbirths do not receive a birth certificate and are not registered.
translations.hypothesis: This also involves providing respectful and supportive care during the time of death. Listening to the experiences and voices of women and communities is crucial to addressing the stigma associated with infant mortality. World Health Organization's Response: More needs to be done to integrate prevention of stillbirths into global and national agendas to provide high-quality health care for women, adolescents, and children. The World Health Organization is working to prevent stillbirths as part of its efforts to improve the health of women, newborns, children, and adolescents, by implementing the recommendations of the Global Strategy for Women's and Children's Health (2016-2030) and the Every Newborn Action Plan (2014). Many of the babies who die are not given a birth certificate and are not registered.

translations.source: የወሊድ እና የአራስ ሞትን ሪፖርት የማድረግ ሥርዓቶችን ማሻሻል የሰብዓዊ መብቶች ጒዳይ ሲሆን የሟች ልደትን ለመቀነስ ቅድመ ሁኔታ ነው። በተጨማሪም የሞቱ ሕፃናትን በትክክል ለመያዝ እና ሪፖርት ለማድረግ መደበኛ የጤና መረጃ ስርዓቶችን ማጠናከር እና የእንክብካቤ ጥራትን ማሻሻል ያስፈልጋል። የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት አገራት የሟች ሕፃናት እና የሕፃናት ሞት መረጃዎቻቸውን እንዲያሻሽሉ የሚያግዙ መሣሪያዎችን አቅርቧል። የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ሀገሮች የግለሰቦችን ሞት እንዲገመግሙ እና እንዲመረመሩ የሚረዳ መመሪያ አለው ስለዚህ ለወደፊቱ ተመሳሳይ ሁኔታዎችን ለመከላከል መፍትሄዎችን እንዲመክሩ እና እንዲተገብሩ እና ለቅድመ ወሊድ ሞት (አይሲዲ-ፒኤም) መደበኛ ምደባ ስርዓት። የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት እና ዩኒሴፍ ሊከላከሉ የሚችሉ የሞተ ልደቶችን ለማስቆም የጋራ እርምጃ እንዲወሰድ ጥሪን እየደገፉ ነው።
translations.reference: Improving systems for reporting births and neonatal deaths is a matter of human rights and a prerequisite for reducing stillbirths. In addition, there is a need to strengthen routine health information systems to accurately capture and report stillbirths for monitoring and improving the quality of care. WHO provides tools to help countries improve their data on both stillbirths and neonatal deaths. WHO also has guidance to help countries review and investigate individual deaths so they can recommend and implement solutions to prevent similar ones in the future and a standard classification system for perinatal deaths (ICD-PM). WHO and UNICEF are supporting a call for collective action to end preventable stillbirths by:
translations.hypothesis: Improving systems for reporting births and stillbirths is a human rights issue and a prerequisite for reducing infant mortality. Furthermore, it is necessary to strengthen routine health information systems to accurately capture and report on infant deaths, and to improve the quality of care. The World Health Organization (WHO) has provided countries with tools to help them improve their data on stillbirths and infant mortality. The WHO has a guideline that helps countries to assess and investigate individual deaths, so that they can identify solutions to prevent similar events in the future, and a standardized classification system for pre-eclampsia and eclampsia. The WHO and UNICEF are advocating for joint action to prevent preventable stillbirths.

translations.source: ግንዛቤን መጨመር እና መገለልን መቀነስ። የተጐዱ ሴቶችን እና ቤተሰቦችን መደገፍ። ከፍተኛ ጥራት ያለው እንክብካቤ ለመስጠት የጤና ሥርዓቶችን ማጠናከር። የሞተ ልደት ዒላማዎችን ብሔራዊ እና አካባቢያዊ ማድረግ። ማስረጃዎችን እና እውቀትን ለማሳደግ የሞተ ልደት መለኪያዎችን ማሻሻል።
translations.reference: increasing awareness and reducing stigma supporting bereaved women and families strengthening health systems to provide high-quality care nationalizing and localizing stillbirth targets improving measurement of stillbirths to enhance evidence and knowledge.
translations.hypothesis: Increasing awareness and reducing stigma. Supporting affected women and families. Strengthening healthcare systems to provide high-quality care. Nationalizing and localizing maternal mortality targets. Improving maternal mortality indicators to increase evidence and knowledge.

translations.source: ዝቅተኛ ጥራት ያላቸው እና የተጭበረበሩ የህክምና ምርቶች አጠቃላይ እይታ፡ በአለም ላይ እስከ ሁለት ቢሊዮን የሚደርሱ ሰዎች አስፈላጊ የሆኑ መድሃኒቶችን፣ ክትባቶችን፣ በብልቃጥ ምርመራን ጨምሮ የህክምና መሳሪያዎች እና ሌሎች የጤና ምርቶችን አያገኙም። ይህም ብዙውን ጊዜ ጥራት በሌላቸው እና በተጭበረበሩ ምርቶች የተሞላ ክፍተት ይፈጥራል። ይህ ችግር እየጨመረ የመጣው ዓለም አቀፋዊ የአቅርቦት ሰንሰለቶች ይበልጥ ውስብስብ እየሆኑ በመምጣታቸው በአንድ ሀገር ውስጥ የተመረቱ ምርቶች በሁለተኛ ሀገር ውስጥ የታሸጉ እና በሦስተኛ ሀገር ውስጥ ለሸማቾች ለመሸጥ ወይም ለመሸጥ ድንበር ተሻጋሪ ሊሆኑ ይችላሉ። የኤሌክትሮኒክ ንግድ እድገት ለዚህ አዝማሚያ አስተዋጽኦ የሚያበረክተው ብዙውን ጊዜ ከማይፈቀዱ ምንጮች በመስመር ላይ መድኃኒቶችን መግዛትን ቀላል በማድረግ ነው። በዝቅተኛ እና መካከለኛ ገቢ ባላቸው ሃገራት ከአሥር መድኃኒቶች ውስጥ አንዱ ዝቅተኛ ጥራት ያለው ወይም የተጭበረበረ መሆኑን ከግምት በማስገባት የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ይህንን ጒዳይ ለሚቀጥሉት አስርት ዓመታት አስቸኳይ የጤና ችግሮች አንዱ አድርጎታል።
translations.reference: Substandard and falsified medical products Overview: Up to two billion people around the world lack access to necessary medicines, vaccines, medical devices including in vitro diagnostics, and other health products, which creates a vacuum that is too often filled by substandard and falsified products. This problem is growing as global supply chains become more complex, meaning products manufactured in one country may be packaged in a second country and distributed across borders to be marketed or sold to consumers in a third. The growth of e-commerce also contributes to this trend by making it easier to purchase medicines online, often from unauthorized sources. WHO has identified this issue one of the urgent health challenges for the next decade given that more than one in ten medicines in low- and middle-income countries are estimated to be substandard or falsified.
translations.hypothesis: The global landscape of substandard and falsified medical products: An estimated two billion people worldwide lack access to essential medicines, vaccines, medical devices, and other health products. This often results in a gap filled with products of poor quality or that are counterfeit. This problem is exacerbated by increasingly complex global supply chains, where products manufactured in one country may be packaged in another and then distributed across borders to consumers. The growth of e-commerce has also contributed to this trend, as it often makes it easier to purchase medicines from unregulated sources. Considering that, on average, one in ten medicines in low- and middle-income countries is estimated to be substandard or falsified, the World Health Organization considers this issue a major public health problem for the coming decades.

translations.source: በዚህ ጒዳይ ያልተነካ ሀገር የለም። የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ከሁሉም የዓለም ክልሎች ጥራት የሌላቸው ወይም የተጭበረበሩ የህክምና መድኃኒቶች፣ ክትባቶች እና በብልቃጥ ውስጥ ምርመራዎች ሪፖርቶችን ተቀብሏል። ለካንሰር ከሚያገለግሉ በጣም ውድ ምርቶች ጀምሮ እስከ ሕመም ማስታገሻ በጣም ርካሽ ምርቶች ድረስ ሁለቱም አጠቃላይ እና ፈጠራ መድኃኒቶች ሊታለሉ ይችላሉ። የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት አባል አገራት ጥራት ያላቸውን፣ ደህንነታቸው የተጠበቀ፣ ውጤታማ እና ጥራት ያላቸውን የህክምና ምርቶች ተደራሽነት ለማሳደግ በመከላከል፣ በመለየት እና ምላሽ ላይ ያተኰረ አጠቃላይ ዓለም አቀፍ ስትራቴጂ ላይ ተስማምተዋል። ተጽዕኖ: ጥራት የሌላቸውና የተጭበረበሩ የህክምና ምርቶች መድኃኒቱን ለሚወስዱ ግለሰቦችም ሆኑ ለቤተሰቦች፣ ለትልቁ ኅብረተሰብ፣ ለጤና እንክብካቤ ሥርዓት እና ለአቅርቦት ሰንሰለት ብዙ ችግሮችን ይፈጥራሉ። መድኃኒቶች በተገቢው መንገድ የማይሰሩ ከሆነ በሽታን ማከም ወይም መከላከል አይችሉም። ለፀረ-ተህዋሲያን መድኃኒቶች የመቋቋም አቅም እንዲኖር አስተዋጽኦ ያደርጋሉ። ውድ ሀብቶችን ያባክናሉ። እንዲሁም በመድኃኒቶች እና በጤና አጠባበቅ አቅራቢዎች ላይ እምነት ማጣት ያስከትላሉ።
translations.reference: No country remains untouched from this issue, and WHO has received reports of substandard or falsified medical medicines, vaccines and in vitro diagnostics from all regions of the world. Both generic and innovator medicines can be falsified, ranging from very expensive products for cancer to very inexpensive products for treatment of pain. WHO Member States have agreed on a comprehensive global strategy focused on prevention, detection and response to move towards achieving increased access to quality, safe, effective and quality medical products. Impact: Substandard and falsified medical products create many problems for both the individual taking the medicine and for families, the larger society, health care system and supply chain. When medicines do not work the way they should, they can fail to treat or prevent disease, contribute to antimicrobial resistance, waste precious resources and lead to a loss of confidence in medicines and distrust of healthcare providers.
translations.hypothesis: No country is immune to this issue. The World Health Organization (WHO) has received reports from all regions of the world regarding substandard or falsified medical products, vaccines, and in vitro diagnostics. These products can range from expensive cancer treatments to cheap painkillers, and can be either completely fake or deliberately altered. The WHO member states have agreed on a comprehensive global strategy focused on prevention, detection, and response to increase access to safe, effective, quality medical products. Impact: Substandard and falsified medical products pose significant risks to individuals, families, and communities, as well as to healthcare systems and supply chains. If these products do not work as intended, they can fail to treat or prevent diseases. They can also contribute to the development of antimicrobial resistance. Furthermore, they waste resources and erode trust in medicines and healthcare providers.

translations.source: ይህ አሉታዊ ማህበረ-ኢኮኖሚያዊ ተፅእኖን ሊያስከትል ይችላል። ለምርታማነት መጥፋት እና ለግለሰብ እና ለሀገር አቀፍ የጤና ስርዓት ወጪዎችን ይጨምራል። ጥራት ያለው እና ደህንነቱ የተጠበቀ የህክምና ምርቶች አቅርቦት ውስንነት፣ የአስተዳደር ጉድለት እና የቴክኒክ አቅም ደካማ በሆነባቸው ሁኔታዎች ውስጥ ታካሚዎችን የመድረስ እድላቸው ሰፊ ነው። ምንም እንኳን ይህ ጉዳይ ሁሉንም ሀገራት የሚመለከት ቢሆንም ደካማ ወይም የቁጥጥር ስርዓቶች የለሉባቸው ሀገራት ደረጃቸውን ያልጠበቁ እና የተጭበረበሩ የህክምና ምርቶች ትልቁን ሸክም ይሸከማሉ። ከጊዜ ወደ ጊዜ እየጨመረ በሚሄደው ግሎባላይዜሽን ዓለም ውስጥ አንድም ሀገር ይህንን ጒዳይ በተናጠል ለመቋቋም የሚያስችል በቂ ሀብትና አቅም የለውም። እንደ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ዓለም አቀፍ ክትትል እና ቁጥጥር ስርዓት እና የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት አባል ሀገር ሜካኒዝም ባሉ ዓለም አቀፍ ስርዓቶች እና ሂደቶች
translations.reference: This can have a negative socioeconomic impact, contributing to related loss in productivity and adding expenses to the individual and national health system. They are most likely to reach patients in situations where there is constrained access to quality and safe medical products, poor governance and weak technical capacity. Although this issue impacts all countries, countries where there are weak or non-existent regulatory systems bear the greatest burden of substandard and falsified medical products. In an increasingly globalized world, no one country has sufficient resources and capacity to deal with this issue in isolation. Through global systems and processes such as the WHO Global Surveillance and Monitoring System and WHO Member State Mechanism,
translations.hypothesis: This can lead to negative socio-economic consequences. It can result in a loss of productivity and increased costs for both individuals and national healthcare systems. In situations where access to quality and safe medical products is limited, and where there are administrative deficiencies or a lack of technical capacity, patients are more likely to be exposed to substandard or falsified products. While this issue affects all countries, developing countries with weak regulatory systems bear the greatest burden of substandard and falsified medical products. In an increasingly globalized world, no single country has sufficient resources or capacity to address this issue alone. Therefore, international systems and processes, such as the World Health Organization's global surveillance and control system, and the WHO member state mechanism, are crucial.

translations.source: አባል ሀገራት ከደረጃ በታች የሆኑ እና የተጭበረበሩ የህክምና ምርቶችን ለመከላከል፣ ለመለየት እና ምላሽ ለመስጠት በጋራ እየሰሩ ነው።
translations.reference: Member States are working together to prevent, detect and respond to substandard and falsified medical products.
translations.hypothesis: Member states are working together to prevent, detect, and respond to substandard and falsified medical products.

translations.source: ራስን ማጥፋትን መከላከል አጠቃላይ እይታ:- በየዓመቱ ከ700,000 በላይ ሰዎች ራሳቸውን ያጠፋሉ። ለእያንዳንዱ ራስን ማጥፋት ከ20 በላይ ራስን የማጥፋት ሙከራዎች እንዳሉ ይገመታል። ራስን ማጥፋት በማንኛውም የሕይወት ደረጃ እና በሁሉም የዓለም ክፍሎች ሊከሰት ይችላል። እ.ኤ.አ. በ 2019 ራስን ማጥፋት በአለም አቀፍ ደረጃ ከ15-29 አመት እድሜ ክልል ውስጥ ለሚከሰቱ አራተኛው ሞት ምክንያት ሲሆን 77በመቶ የሚሆኑት ራስን የማጥፋት ድርጊቶች የተከሰቱት ዝቅተኛ እና መካከለኛ ገቢ ባላቸው ሀገራት ነው።
translations.reference: Suicide prevention Overview: More than 700 000 people die by suicide every year. For each suicide, there are an estimated more than 20 suicide attempts. Suicide can occur at any stage of life and in all regions of the world. In 2019, suicide was the fourth leading cause of death among 15–29-year-olds globally, and 77% of all suicides happened in low- and middle-income countries.
translations.hypothesis: Suicide Prevention: A Global Perspective: Every year, over 700,000 people die from suicide. For every suicide that occurs, there are estimated to be more than 20 suicide attempts. Suicide can occur at any stage of life and in all parts of the world. In 2019, suicide was the fourth leading cause of death globally for people aged 15-29. Furthermore, 77% of suicides occurred in low- and middle-income countries.

translations.source: በዓለም አቀፍ ደረጃ 20% የሚሆኑት ራሳቸውን የሚያጠፉ ሰዎች ራሳቸውን በፀረ ተባይ መድኃኒቶች በመመረዝ ነው። ራስን ማጥፋት እና የአዕምሮ ጤና ሁኔታዎች( በተለይም የመንፈስ ጭንቀት እና የአልኮል አጠቃቀም ችግሮች) እና ቀደም ሲል ራስን የማጥፋት ሙከራ በከፍተኛ ገቢ ባላቸው ሀገሮች ውስጥ በደንብ የተረጋገጠ ቢሆንም ብዙ ራስን የማጥፋት ድርጊቶች በችግር ጊዜያት በድንገት ይከሰታሉ። ራስን የማጥፋት አደጋ የሚፈጥሩ ሌሎች ምክንያቶች ደግሞ የኑሮ ውድነት፣ ብቸኝነት፣ መድልዎ፣ የግንኙነት መቋረጥ፣ የገንዘብ ችግር፣ ሥር የሰደደ ህመምና ሕመም፣ ጥቃት፣ በደል፣ ግጭት ወይም ሌሎች የሰብዓዊ አደጋዎች ናቸው። በአእምሮ ጤንነት ችግሮች እና ራስን በማጥፋት ዙሪያ ያለው ቅሌት (መገለል) ብዙ ሰዎች ራስን የማጥፋት ችግር እርዳታ አይፈልግም ብለው ማሰባቸው ነው። ራስን ማጥፋት እና ራስን የማጥፋት ሙከራዎች በቤተሰብ፣ በጓደኞች፣ በስራ ባልደረቦች፣ በማህበረሰቦች እና ማህበራት ላይ የሚያሳድሩት ተፅዕኖዎች አሉት።
translations.reference: Almost 20% of global suicides were due to pesticide self-poisoning. While the link between suicide and mental health conditions (in particular, depression and alcohol use disorders) and a previous suicide attempt is well established in high-income countries, many suicides happen impulsively in moments of crisis. Further risk factors for suicide include experience of loss, loneliness, discrimination, a relationship break-up, financial problems, chronic pain and illness, violence, abuse, and conflict or other humanitarian emergencies. Stigma around mental health conditions and suicide means many people thinking about suicide do not seek help. Suicides and suicide attempts have a ripple effect that impacts on families, friends, colleagues, communities and societies.
translations.hypothesis: Globally, approximately 20% of individuals who die by suicide do so using antidepressants. While suicide and mental health conditions (particularly depression and alcohol abuse) are strongly linked, and previous suicide attempts are a significant risk factor, many suicides occur impulsively during times of crisis. Other factors that can increase the risk of suicide include financial difficulties, loneliness, discrimination, social isolation, unemployment, chronic illness, abuse, violence, conflict, or other traumatic events. The stigma surrounding mental health issues and suicide often prevents people from seeking help. Suicide and suicide attempts have a significant impact on families, friends, colleagues, communities, and organizations.

translations.source: ራስን ማጥፋት: በዓለም ዙሪያ ያሉ እውነታዎች እና ቍጥሮች። እ.ኤ.አ. በ2019 በዓለም ዙሪያ ራስን ማጥፋት ሪፖርት ህይወትን መኖር: በአገሮች ውስጥ ራስን ማጥፋትን ለመከላከል የአተገባበር መመሪያ። መከላከል: ራስን ማጥፋትንና ራስን የማጥፋት ሙከራን ለመከላከል በግለሰብ፣ በማህበረሰብና በብሔራዊ ደረጃ ብዙ ነገር ማድረግ ይቻላል። የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት የህይወትን መኖር አቀራረብ በአራት ውጤታማ፣ በማስረጃ ላይ የተመሰረቱ ቍልፍ ጣልቃ ገብነቶች ይመክራል። እነዚህም ራስን ለማጥፋት በሚደረገው እያንዳንዱ ብሔራዊ ምላሽ ውስጥ መካተት አለባቸው።
translations.reference: Suicide: facts and figures globally Suicide worldwide in 2019 report LIVE LIFE: an implementation guide for suicide prevention in countries Prevention: Much can be done at individual, community and national levels to prevent suicide and suicide attempts. WHO’s LIVE LIFE approach recommends four effective, evidence-based key interventions which should be included in every national response to suicide:
translations.hypothesis: Suicide: Facts and Figures Worldwide. In 2019, the World Suicide Report highlighted the importance of "Living Life: A Global Action Plan to Prevent Suicide." Prevention: A great deal can be done at individual, community, and national levels to prevent suicide and attempted suicide. The World Health Organization's approach to promoting well-being is based on four effective, evidence-based strategies, which should be included in every national response to suicide.

translations.source: ራስን ወደ ማጥፋት የሚያመሩ ነገሮችን መገደብ። ራስን ማጥፋትን በኃላፊነት ሪፖርት ለማድረግ ከመገናኛ ብዙኃን ጋር መስተጋብር መፍጠር። በጉርምስና (በአስራዎቹ) ዕድሜ ላይ ባሉ ወጣቶች ላይ ማህበራዊ-ስሜታዊ የህይወት ክህሎቶችን ማዳበር። ራስን የማጥፋት ዝንባሌ ያላቸውን ሰዎች በቅድሚያ መለየት፣ መገምገም፣ ማስተዳደር እና ክትትል ማድረግ። የእርምጃዎቹ መሰረታዊ ምሰሶዎች የሁኔታ ትንተና፣ ዘርፈ ብዙ ትብብር፣ የግንዛቤ ማስጨበጫ፣ የአቅም ግንባታ፣ ፋይናንስ፣ ቁጥጥር፣ ክትትል እና ግምገማ ናቸው።
translations.reference: limit access to the means of suicide interact with the media for responsible reporting of suicide foster socio-emotional life skills in adolescents early identify, assess, manage and follow up anyone who is affected by suicidal behaviours. The foundational pillars of the interventions are situation analysis, multisectoral collaboration, awareness raising, capacity building, financing, surveillance, monitoring and evaluation.
translations.hypothesis: Restricting access to means of self-harm. Establishing communication channels with the media to responsibly report on suicide. Developing social-emotional life skills in adolescents. Identifying, assessing, managing, and monitoring individuals with suicidal tendencies. The core elements of these actions include situation analysis, broad collaboration, awareness raising, capacity building, finance, control, monitoring, and evaluation.

translations.source: ራስን ማጥፋትን ለመከላከል የሚደረገው ጥረት በጤና፣ በትምህርት፣ በሰራተኛ፣ በግብርና፣ በንግድ፣ በፍትህ፣ በሕግ፣ በመከላከያ፣ በፖለቲካና በመገናኛ ብዙኃን ዘርፎች መካከል ቅንጅትና ትብብርን ይጠይቃል። ራስን ማጥፋት ዋነኛው የህብረተሰብ ጤና ችግር መሆኑን በመገለል እና በግንዛቤ ማነስ ምክንያት ራስን ማጥፋትን መከላከል በቂ ምላሽ አይሰጥም። ራስን ማጥፋትን በመከላከል ረገድ ማኅበረሰቦችም ወሳኝ ሚና ይጫወታሉ። የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት የማህበረሰብ ተሳትፎ መሳሪያ ስብስብ ማህበረሰቦችን ቅድሚያ የሚሰጣቸውን ነገሮች በመለየት እና ተግባራትን እንደየአካባቢው ሁኔታ እንዲተገብሩ መመሪያ ይሰጣል። የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ምላሽ፦ ራስን የማጥፋት ድርጊቶችን ለመከላከል አስቸኳይ እርምጃ መውሰድ እንዳለበት በከፍተኛ ደረጃ ተገንዝቦ ቅድሚያ ተሰጥቶታል።
translations.reference: Suicide prevention efforts require coordination and collaboration among multiple sectors, including health, education, labour, agriculture, business, justice, law, defence, politics and the media. Suicide prevention is often not adequately addressed because of stigma and lack of awareness that suicide is a major public health problem. Communities also play a crucial role in preventing suicide. The WHO community engagement toolkit guides communities in identifying priorities and implementing activities according to local contexts. WHO response: The urgency to act to prevent suicides has been recognized and prioritized at the highest levels.
translations.hypothesis: Efforts to prevent suicide require coordination and collaboration across various sectors, including health, education, labor, agriculture, commerce, justice, law, defense, politics, and media. Because suicide is a major public health problem, and often due to stigma and lack of awareness, prevention efforts are often inadequate. Communities also play a crucial role in preventing suicide. The World Health Organization's community engagement toolkit provides guidance on how to identify community priorities and tailor interventions to local contexts. The World Health Organization's response: There is a strong recognition that urgent action is needed to prevent suicide, and it is a high priority.

translations.source: በአገሮች ውስጥ ራስን የማጥፋት መጠን መቀነስ በተባበሩት መንግሥታት ዘላቂ የልማት ግቦች( ለአእምሮ ጤና ብቸኛው አመላካች) በዓለም የጤና ድርጅት አጠቃላይ የሥራ ፕሮግራም እና በዓለም የጤና ድርጅት አጠቃላይ የአዕምሮ ጤና የድርጊት መርሃ ግብር 2013 - 2030 ውስጥ አመላካች ነው። እ.ኤ.አ. በ 2014 የታተመው የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት የመጀመሪያ የዓለም ራስን ማጥፋት ሪፖርት ራስን ማጥፋትን መከላከል ዓለም አቀፋዊ ግዴታ ነው። ራስን የማጥፋት እና ራስን የማጥፋት ሙከራዎች የሕዝብ ጤና ጠቀሜታ ግንዛቤን ለማሳደግ እና ራስን የማጥፋት መከላከያ የሕዝብ ጤና ቅድሚያ እንዲሰጥ ለማድረግ ነበር.። መንግሥታት ራስን ማጥፋትን ለመከላከል ውጤታማ እና በማስረጃ ላይ የተመሰረቱ ቍልፍ ጣልቃ ገብነቶች የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት የ ህይወትን መኖር ጥቅል ተግባራዊ እንዲያደርጉ እና በበርካታ ዘርፎች የሕዝብ ጤና አቀራረብ ውስጥ ራስን ማጥፋትን ለመከላከል አጠቃላይ ብሔራዊ ስትራቴጂዎችን እንዲያዘጋጁ ወይም እንዲያጠናክሩ ይበረታታሉ። ራስን ማጥፋትን ለመከላከል ብሔራዊ አመራር እና ቅንጅት በብሔራዊ ራስን ማጥፋት መከላከል ስትራቴጂዎች-እድገቶች ፣ ምሳሌዎች እና አመላካቾች በማተም የበለጠ ይደገፋሉ ። መረጃ እና ስታቲስቲክስ ለጥብቅና እና ውሳኔ አሰጣጥ አስፈላጊ መሆናቸውን በመገንዘብ፣ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት እ.ኤ.አ. በ2019 ራስን ማጥፋትን በዓለም ዙሪያ አሳተመ።
translations.reference: The reduction of suicide rates in countries is an indicator in the UN Sustainable Development Goals (the only indicator for mental health), WHO’s General Programme of Work and WHO’s Comprehensive Mental Health Action Plan 2013–2030. The first WHO world suicide report, Preventing suicide: a global imperative, published in 2014, aimed to increase awareness of the public health significance of suicide and suicide attempts and make suicide prevention a public health priority. Governments are encouraged to implement WHO’s LIVE LIFE package of effective and evidence-based key interventions for suicide prevention, and to develop or strengthen comprehensive national suicide prevention strategies in a multisectoral public health approach. National leadership and coordination on suicide prevention are further supported by the publication of National suicide prevention strategies: progress, examples and indicators. Recognizing that data and statistics are necessary for advocacy and decision-making, WHO published Suicide worldwide in 2019: Global Health Estimates.
translations.hypothesis: The decline in suicide rates is an indicator within the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (specifically, the one related to mental health), the World Health Organization's (WHO) global health priorities, and the WHO's Mental Health Action Plan 2013-2030. The WHO's first global report on suicide, published in 2014, highlights suicide prevention as a global imperative. The aim is to raise public awareness of the public health importance of suicide and to ensure that suicide prevention is prioritized within public health initiatives. Governments are encouraged to implement effective, evidence-based interventions to prevent suicide, to operationalize the WHO's "Life Course Approach" to health, and to develop or strengthen comprehensive national strategies for suicide prevention across multiple sectors. National leadership and coordination in suicide prevention are further supported through the publication of national suicide prevention strategies, progress reports, case studies, and indicators. Recognizing the importance of data and statistics for advocacy and decision-making, the WHO published a global report on suicide in 2019.

translations.source: የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ራእይ ራስን በማጥፋት ሞት ከአሁን በኋላ ዋነኛው የሞት መንስኤ የማይሆንበት እና ራስን የማጥፋት ሙከራ እያደረጉ ወይም እያሰቡ ያሉ ሰዎች ለሁሉም ብቃት ባለው እና በአክብሮት የሚሰጥ እርዳታ በመፈለግ ምቾት የሚሰማቸው የሚሆኑበትን ዓለም ማየት ነው።
translations.reference: WHO’s vision is a world where death by suicide is no longer a leading cause of death and where people who are attempting or thinking about suicide feel comfortable seeking help which is provided capably and respectfully to all.
translations.hypothesis: The World Health Organization envisions a world where suicide is no longer a leading cause of death, and where individuals who are attempting or contemplating suicide feel comfortable seeking help that is accessible, appropriate, and delivered with respect.

translations.source: ዘላቂ ልማት(ቀጣይነት ያለው እድገት) አጠቃላይ እይታ፡ ዘላቂ ልማት ማለት ለወደፊት የአካባቢን፣ ማህበራዊ እና የግል ጤናን ሳይከፍሉ ዛሬ ጥቅማጥቅሞችን የሚሰጡ ፖሊሲዎችን፣ ፕሮጀክቶችን እና ኢንቨስትመንቶችን ለመግለጽ ሰፊ ቃል ነው። እነዚህ ፖሊሲዎች ብዙውን ጊዜ አረንጓዴ ተብለው ይጠራሉ። ምክንያቱም የልማትን በአካባቢ ላይ የሚያሳድረውን ተጽዕኖ ለመገደብ ያተኮሩ ናቸው። ሆኖም የዘላቂ ልማት ጥቅሞች በሰፊ የሰው ልጅ ጤና እና ደህንነት ላይም ይሰማሉ። ይህም የብክለት እና የአካባቢ-ነክ በሽታዎችን መቀነስ፣ የተሻሻሉ የጤና ውጤቶችን እና የጭንቀት መቀነስን ይጨምራል። የአየር ንብረት ለውጥ ስጋቶች ከጊዜ ወደ ጊዜ እየጨመሩ በመምጣታቸው የአየር ንብረት ለውጥን ለመቀነስ፣ ለጤና መሻሻል እና ለወጪ ቁጠባ ሁሉን ተጠቃሚ የሚያደርጉ ስትራቴጂዎች ለተለያዩ ባለድርሻ አካላት በርካታ ጥቅሞችን ይሰጣሉ።
translations.reference: Sustainable development Overview: Sustainable development is a broad term to describe policies, projects and investments that provide benefits today without sacrificing environmental, social and personal health in the future. These policies are often described as green because they focus on limiting the impact of development on the environment. However, the benefits of sustainable development are also felt across a wide cross section of human health and well-being, including reductions in pollution- and environment-related disease, improved health outcomes and decreased stress. As the threats of climate change become increasingly important, win-win strategies for mitigation, health improvement and cost savings offer a range of advantages for various stakeholders.
translations.hypothesis: Sustainable development: A broad overview. Sustainable development refers to policies, projects, and investments that provide benefits today without compromising the environment, social well-being, or human health for future generations. These policies are often referred to as "green" because they focus on minimizing the environmental impact of development. However, the benefits of sustainable development extend to the broader health and well-being of humanity, including reduced pollution and environmentally-related diseases, improved health outcomes, and decreased stress. As the risks of climate change continue to grow, climate change mitigation strategies that benefit health, improve well-being, and save costs offer numerous advantages to various stakeholders.

translations.source: ለምሳሌ አረንጓዴ የጤና እንክብካቤ ክዋኔዎች ለታካሚዎች እና ለሰራተኞች የጤና ጥቅሞችን ሊያመጡ ይችላሉ። እንዲሁም ኃይል ይቆጥባሉ፣ የአየር ንብረት አደጋዎችን ይቀንሳሉ እና የረጅም ጊዜ ወጪ ቁጠባን ይፈጥራሉ። የዓለም የጤና ድርጅት የወደፊቱን ሕዝብ ከዛሬው እየጨመረ ከሚመጣ የጤና አደጋ ለመጠበቅ በሚያደርገው ሥራ ሁሉ ዘላቂ ልማት ለማሳካት ቁርጠኛ ነው። ተጽዕኖ: ብዙ ዘላቂ የልማት ስትራቴጂዎች ለጤና፣ ለአየር ንብረት እና ለአካባቢ ከፍተኛ ትርፍ ሊያገኙ ይችላሉ። እናም ጥቅሞቹ ወዲያውኑ ሊታዩ ይችላሉ። ለምሳሌ በዓመት ወደ 7 ሚሊዮን የሚገመቱ ያለዕድሜ ሞት ምክንያት የሆነውን የአየር ብክለትን በመታገል ብዙ የጤና እና የልማት ግቦችን በአንድ ጊዜ ማሳካት ይቻላል። የብክለት ልቀቶች ኃይለኛ የአጭር ጊዜ የአየር ንብረት በካይ (ኤስኤልሲፒዎች) -በተለይ ጥቁር ካርቦን፣ ኦዞን እና ሚቴን ለጤና በጣም ጎጂ የሆኑ ነገር ግን አጭር የከባቢ አየር ህይወት ያላቸው ናቸው።
translations.reference: For example, greener health care operations can generate patient and worker health benefits while also saving energy, mitigating climate risks and creating long-term cost savings. The World Health Organization is committed to pursuing sustainable development in all its work to help protect the people of tomorrow from the health growing health risks of today. Impact: Many sustainable development strategies can offer significant wins for health, climate and the environment, and the benefits can be seen almost immediately. For example, many health and development goals can be achieved simultaneously by tackling air pollution, which is responsible for an estimated 7 million premature deaths annually. Polluting emissions include powerful short-lived climate pollutants (SLCPs)—particularly black carbon, ozone and methane—which are very harmful to health but have only brief atmospheric lifetimes.
translations.hypothesis: For example, green healthcare practices can bring health benefits to patients and staff, while also conserving energy, reducing climate risks, and creating long-term cost savings. The World Health Organization is committed to achieving sustainable development in all its work to protect future generations from the growing health risks of today. Impact: Many sustainable development strategies can yield significant benefits for health, climate, and the environment, and these benefits can be realized quickly. For example, addressing air pollution, which is estimated to cause 7 million premature deaths per year, can simultaneously achieve multiple health and development goals. Air pollution releases short-lived climate pollutants (SLCPs) – particularly black carbon, ozone, and methane – which are highly harmful to health but have a relatively short lifespan in the atmosphere.

translations.source: የንጹሕ የሕዝብ ትራንስፖርትን የሚደግፉ የትራንስፖርት ፖሊሲዎች እና ኢንቨስትመንቶች በእግር እና በብስክሌት መጓዝ በአየር ጥራት ላይ ከፍተኛ ተጽዕኖ ሊያሳድሩ ይችላሉ። ይህ የደም ቧንቧን፣የልብ ድካም፣ የሳንባ በሽታ እና አንዳንድ የካንሰር በሽታዎችን ጨምሮ በርካታ የጤና አደጋዎችን በአንድ ጊዜ ሊቀንስ ይችላል። በተጨማሪም በየዓመቱ ከትራፊክ ጕዳት የሚሞቱትን 1,25 ሚሊዮን የሚገመቱ ሰዎችን ለመቀነስ እና የጤና አገልግሎቶችን በተለይም በሰፊው በሚኖሩ አካባቢዎች ተደራሽነትን ለማሻሻል ይችላሉ። ዘላቂ ልማት የቤቶች ፖሊሲዎች እንደ የሕንጻ ቦታ እና የመሬት አጠቃቀም፣ የግንባታ ቁሳቁሶች ምርጫ፣ የዲዛይን ባህሪዎች እና የአየር ማናፈሻ እና የኃይል አቅርቦት ባሉ ጒዳዮች አማካይነት ልቀቶችን የበለጠ ሊቀንስ ይችላል። የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ምላሽ: የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ሥራ የሚመራው በዘላቂ ልማት ግቦች በተለይም በግብ 3 ነው። ጤናማ ኑሮዎችን ማረጋገጥ እና በሁሉም የዕድሜ ክልል ውስጥ ላሉት ሁሉ ደኅንነትን ማጐልበት።
translations.reference: Transport policies and investments that favour clean public transport, along with walking and cycling, can have a dramatic impact on air quality. This can reduce multiple health risks at the same time, including stroke, heart attack, lung disease and some cancers. They can also reduce the estimated 1.25 million deaths annually from traffic injury, while also improving access to health services, particularly in densely populated areas. Sustainable development housing policies can further reduce emissions through considerations such as building siting and land use, choices of construction materials, design features and ventilation and energy. WHO response: WHO’s work is guided by the Sustainable Development Goals, particularly Goal 3: “Ensure healthy lives and promote well-being for all at all ages.”
translations.hypothesis: Transportation policies and investments that promote clean public transportation can significantly improve air quality. This can reduce the risk of numerous health problems, including cardiovascular disease, respiratory illnesses, and certain types of cancer. Furthermore, it can help reduce the estimated 1.25 million deaths that occur annually due to traffic accidents and improve access to healthcare, especially in underserved areas. Sustainable development policies can also further reduce emissions through measures related to building design and land use, the selection of construction materials, design features, and issues such as ventilation and energy supply. World Health Organization Response: The World Health Organization's work is guided by the Sustainable Development Goals, particularly Goal 3, which aims to ensure healthy lives and promote well-being for all at all ages.

translations.source: ግቦቹ በመላ ድርጅቱ ፕሮጀክቶች እና ፖሊሲዎች ላይ የመኖሪያ ቤት፣ የከተማ ልማት፣ የትራንስፖርት ሥርዓቶች እና የጤና እንክብካቤ አቅርቦትን ጨምሮ መመሪያዎችን ይሰጣሉ። ለምሳሌ: በ 2018 ለመጀመሪያ ጊዜ የታተሙት የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት የመኖሪያ ቤት እና የጤና መመሪያዎች የከተማ እድገት፣ የአየር ንብረት እና የስነህዝብ ለውጦች አንጻር የመኖሪያ ቤት ሁኔታዎች በሰዎች ጤና ላይ የሚያሳድሩትን ተጽዕኖ ያሳያሉ። መመሪያዎቹ ለአባል አገራት፣ ለአጋሮች እና ለሕዝብ በቂ ያልሆነ የመኖሪያ ቦታ( የሕዝብ ብዛት)፣ ዝቅተኛ እና ከፍተኛ የቤት ውስጥ ሙቀቶች፣ በቤት ውስጥ የመጉዳት አደጋዎች እና የአካል ጉዳተኞች የመኖሪያ ቤት ተደራሽነት ጋር የሚዛመዱ በማስረጃ ላይ የተመሰረቱ ምክሮችን ይሰጣሉ። የከተማ ጤና ኢኒሼቲቭ (ዩኤችአይ) በከተማ የአየር ብክለት እና በአጭር ጊዜ የሚቆዩ የአየር ንብረት ብክለትን (ኤስኤልሲፒስ) ላይ ውጤታማ እርምጃ በመውሰድ ጤናን፣ አካባቢን እና ዘላቂ ልማትን በማስተሳሰር ህይወትን በመታደግ ጤናን በሚያስችሉ መንገዶች ላይ ያተኩራል። በተጨማሪም የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት የአየር ብክለትን ለመቀነስ እና በየዓመቱ የሚያደርሰውን 7 ሚሊዮን ያለጊዜው የሚሞቱትን ሞት በመቀነስ በሕይወት መተንፈስ ዘመቻ ላይ ከአየር ንብረት እና ንፁህ አየር ጥምረት (ሲሲኤሲ) ጋር ይተባበራል።
translations.reference: The Goals inform projects and policies across the Organization, including guidelines on housing, urban development, transportation systems and health care delivery. For example, the WHO Housing and health guidelines, first published in 2018, highlight the increasing impact of housing conditions on human health in light of urban growth, climate and demographic changes. The guidelines provide Member States, partners and the public with evidence-based recommendations relevant to inadequate living space (crowding), low and high indoor temperatures, injury hazards in the home, and accessibility of housing for people with functional impairments. The Urban Health Initiative (UHI) also focuses on ways for cities to enable good health by catalysing effective action on urban air pollution and short-lived climate pollutants (SLCPs)—saving lives by linking health, environment and sustainable development. WHO also collaborates with the Climate and Clean Air Coalition (CCAC) on the Breathe Life campaign, which aims to cut air pollution and reduce the 7 million premature deaths annually that it causes.
translations.hypothesis: The guidelines provided by these organizations cover a wide range of projects and policies, including housing, urban development, transportation systems, and healthcare delivery. For example, the World Health Organization's guidelines on housing and health, first published in 2018, highlight the impact of housing conditions on people's health in the context of urban growth, climate change, and demographic shifts. The guidelines offer evidence-based recommendations to member states, partners, and the public on issues such as inadequate housing density, extreme indoor temperatures, indoor injury risks, and the accessibility of housing for people with disabilities. The Urban Health Initiative (UHI) focuses on protecting health by addressing the interconnectedness of health, environment, and sustainable development through effective action on urban air pollution and short-lived climate pollutants (SLCPs). Furthermore, the World Health Organization collaborates with the Climate and Clean Air Coalition (CCAC) on the Breathe Life campaign to reduce air pollution and prevent the approximately 7 million premature deaths that occur each year.

translations.source: ቂጥኝ አጠቃላይ እይታ: ሲፊሊስ በ ትሬፖኔማ ፓሊደም ምክንያት የሚመጣ በባክቴሪያ የሚተላለፍ በግብረ ሥጋ ግንኙነት የሚተላለፍ ኢንፌክሽን( ኤስ.ቲ.አይ) ነው። ይህም ከፍተኛ ሕመም እና ሞት ያስከትላል። እና ሊድን ይችላል። ቂጥኝ የሚተላለፈው በወሲባዊ ግንኙነት፣ በደም ዝውውር ወይም ከነፍሰ ጡር ሴት ወደ ፅንሱ ነው። ከእናት ወደ ልጅ የሚተላለፈው የቂጥኝ በሽታ በእርግዝና መጀመሪያ ላይ ካልተገኘ ወይም በበቂ ሁኔታ ካልታከመ በፅንስ ላይ ከፍተኛ ጕዳት ያስከትላል። የእናቶች ቂጥኝ ህክምና ካልተደረገለት፣ ዘግይቶ ህክምና ከተደረገለት ወይም ፔኒሲሊን ካልተደረገለት በቂጥኝ ደረጃ ላይ በመመርኮዝ ከ50−80% በሚሆኑ ጒዳዮች ላይ አሉታዊ የወሊድ ውጤቶች( ኤቢኦ) ያስከትላል።
translations.reference: Syphilis Overview: Syphilis is a bacterial sexually transmitted infection (STI) caused by Treponema pallidum which results in substantial morbidity and mortality, and it is curable. Syphilis is transmitted through sexual contact with the infectious lesions, via blood transfusion or from a pregnant woman to her fetus. Mother-to-child transmission of syphilis is usually devastating to the fetus in cases that were not detected or treated sufficiently early in pregnancy. Maternal syphilis, when untreated, treated late or not treated with penicillin, results in adverse birth outcomes (ABOs) estimated in 50−80% of cases, depending on the stage of syphilis.
translations.hypothesis: Syphilis Overview: Syphilis is a bacterial sexually transmitted infection (STI) caused by the bacterium *Treponema pallidum*. It can cause serious health problems and even death if left untreated, but it is curable. Syphilis is transmitted through sexual contact, blood transfusions, or from a pregnant woman to her baby. Congenital syphilis, which is transmitted from mother to child during pregnancy, can cause serious health problems for the baby if it is not detected and treated early in pregnancy. If a pregnant woman does not receive treatment for syphilis, or if treatment is delayed or ineffective, up to 50-80% of cases can result in adverse pregnancy outcomes (APOs).

translations.source: እነዚህ ኤቢኦስ ብዙ ጊዜ ከባድ ናቸው። እና በሞት መወለድ (በተለምዶ)፣ በአራስ ሕፃን ሞት፣ ያለጊዜው መወለድ፣ ዝቅተኛ ክብደት እና በወሊድ የተያዙ ሕፃናትን ያጠቃልላሉ። በትክክል እና ተከታታይ ኮንዶም መጠቀምን ጨምሮ ደህንነቱ በተጠበቀ የግብረ ሥጋ ግንኙነት ቂጥኝን መከላከል ይቻላል። ብዙ የቂጥኝ በሽታ ያለባቸው ሰዎች ምንም ምልክት አይታይባቸውም ወይም በቀላሉ የማይታዩ ጥቃቅን ምልክቶች አሏቸው። በላብራቶሪ ምርመራዎች አማካኝነት ኢንፌክሽኑን መለየት እና አዎንታዊ ጒዳዮችን ማከም ተጨማሪ ስርጭትን እና የተወለደ ቂጥኝ ጨምሮ አሉታዊ የእርግዝና ውጤቶችን ይከላከላል። ምርመራው በፈጣን ምርመራዎች ሊከናወን ይችላል። ይህም ውጤቱን ከ 20 ደቂቃ ባነሰ ጊዜ ውስጥ ሊሰጥ ይችላል። ይህም ፈጣን ሕክምናን ይፈቅዳል።
translations.reference: These ABOs are often severe and include stillbirth (most commonly), neonatal death, prematurity, low birth weight, and congenitally infected infants. Syphilis can be prevented through safer sex practices including the correct and consistent use of condoms. Many people with syphilis do not have any symptoms or have only minor symptoms that go unnoticed. Identifying infection through laboratory tests and treatment of positive cases will prevent further transmission and adverse pregnancy outcomes including congenital syphilis. Screening can be done using rapid tests that can provide results in less than 20 minutes, allowing for immediate treatment.
translations.hypothesis: These STIs can often be serious. They include conditions like birth defects (congenital abnormalities), infant mortality, premature birth, low birth weight, and complications during childbirth. Safe sexual practices, including the correct and consistent use of condoms, can prevent the transmission of STIs. Many people with STIs have no symptoms or only experience mild symptoms. Laboratory testing can identify infections and treat positive cases, preventing further spread and negative pregnancy outcomes. The testing can be performed using rapid tests, which can provide results in less than 20 minutes, allowing for prompt treatment.

translations.source: ካልታከመ ቂጥኝ ለብዙ ዓመታት የሚቆይ ሲሆን በተለያዩ ደረጃዎች ውስጥ ያልፋል። የቂጥኝ ሕክምና በፔኒሲሊን ቤንዛታይን በመርፌ የሚሰጥ ሲሆን እንደ በሽታው ደረጃ ከ 1 እስከ 3 መጠን ይወሰዳል። የትውልድ ቂጥኝን መከላከል የሚችለው ፔኒሲሊን ብቻ ነው። ከፍተኛ፣ ዝቅተኛ እና መካከለኛ ገቢ ያላቸውን አገሮችን ጨምሮ በበርካታ አገሮች ውስጥ የቤንዛቲን ፔኒሲሊን በየጊዜው እና ረዘም ላለ ጊዜ እጥረት በመኖሩ የፔኒሲሊን ህክምና ተግዳሮት ሆኗል። የበሽታው ምልክቶች፦ በቂጥኝ የተያዙ ብዙ ሰዎች የበሽታው ምልክቶች አይታዩባቸውም ወይም እንደታዩባቸው አይገነዘቡትም።
translations.reference: When untreated, syphilis lasts many years and goes through different stages. Treatment for syphilis is done with injectable penicillin benzathine, with 1 to 3 doses depending on the stage of the disease. Only penicillin can prevent congenital syphilis. Treatment with penicillin has been hampered by periodic and prolonged shortages of benzathine penicillin in numerous countries, including high-, low- and middle-income countries. Symptoms: Many people with syphilis do not present symptoms or do not perceive them.
translations.hypothesis: Syphilis is a sexually transmitted infection that can cause serious health problems if left untreated. It progresses through several stages. Treatment for syphilis typically involves injections of penicillin benzathine, with the dosage ranging from 1 to 3 depending on the stage of the infection. Only penicillin can effectively treat congenital syphilis, which can affect a developing fetus. In many countries, including those with high, low, and middle incomes, there are often shortages of benzathine penicillin, making penicillin treatment a challenge. Symptoms: Many people infected with syphilis may not experience any symptoms, or they may not recognize the symptoms they do have.

translations.source: ህክምና ካልተደረገለት ቂጥኝ ለበርካታ ዓመታት የሚቆይ ሲሆን በተለያዩ ደረጃዎች ይገለጻል። ወሲባዊ ስርጭት በአብዛኛው የሚከሰተው በቅድሚያ ደረጃዎች ማለትም ከበሽታው በኋላ እስከ 2 ዓመት ድረስ ነው። በአማካይ 21 ቀናት በሚቆየው የመጀመሪያ ደረጃ( የመጀመሪያው ሲፊሊስ) ወቅት በክትባት ቦታ( በግንኙነት) ላይ ብቸኛ፣ ሕመም የሌለው፣ ብዙውን ጊዜ ከባድ እና ክብ የሆነ ቍስል( ቻንከር) ይታያል። ብዙ ጊዜ በሴት ብልት፣ በወንድ ብልት ወይም ፊንጢጣ (ነገር ግን ከብልት ውጪ ሊሆን ይችላል) ይህ ሳይስተዋል አይቀርም። ቻንክሬው ከ3 እስከ 10 ሳምንታት ውስጥ በህክምናም ሆነ ያለ ህክምና የሚፈወስ ነው። ህክምና ካልተደረገለት በሽታው ወደ ሁለተኛ ደረጃ ይሸጋገራል።
translations.reference: Untreated, syphilis lasts many years and is characterized into different stages. Sexual transmission typically occurs during early stages, i.e., up to 2 years after infection. During the initial phase (primary syphilis), which lasts on average 21 days, a solitary, painless, usually hard and round sore (chancre) appears at the site of inoculation (contact), often in the vagina, penis or anus (but it may also be extra-genital) and may go unnoticed. The chancre heals with or without treatment within 3 to 10 weeks. If untreated, the disease progresses to the secondary stage.
translations.hypothesis: Untreated syphilis can persist for many years and progresses through various stages. The most common mode of transmission is through sexual contact, primarily during the early stages, which can occur up to 2 years after infection. The primary stage, lasting an average of 21 days, is characterized by a single, painless, firm, round sore called a chancre, which typically appears at the site of infection (e.g., during sexual intercourse). This chancre often appears on the genitals (penis, vulva, or anus), but can also occur in other areas. The chancre usually heals within 3 to 10 weeks, with or without treatment. If left untreated, the disease progresses to the secondary stage.

translations.source: የሁለተኛ ደረጃ ቂጥኝ በሰፊው ሊለያይ በሚችል እና ሌሎች ተላላፊ ወይም ተላላፊ ያልሆኑ ሁኔታዎችን በሚመስሉ ብጕር ተለይቶ ይታወቃል። ግን በተለምዶ መዳፎችን እና እግሮችን ይነካል። ሽፍታው የማያሳክክ እና ሊታለፍ የሚችል አነስተኛ ሊሆን ይችላል። እንደ ፊንጢጣ እና ከናፍረምስ ባሉ ሞቃታማ እና እርጥብ በሆኑ የሰውነት ክፍሎች ውስጥ ዋናው ቻንከር (ኮንዶሎማ ላታ) በሚገኝበት ቦታ ላይ ትልቅ ነጭ ወይም ግራጫ ከፍ ያሉ ቁስሎች ሊፈጠሩ ይችላሉ። የሁለተኛ ደረጃ ቂጥኝ ህመሞች እና ምልክቶች ያለ ህክምና እንኳን ሳይቀር በራስ-ሰር ይስተካከላሉ። ህክምና ካልተደረገለት የተደበቀው ደረጃ ይጀምራል።
translations.reference: Secondary syphilis is characterized by a rash that can vary widely and mimic other infectious or non-infectious conditions, but characteristically affects the palms and soles. The rash is non-itchy and can be minimal enough to be overlooked. In warm and moist areas of the body, such as the anus and labia, large white or grey raised lesions may develop at the location of the primary chancre (condyloma lata). The symptoms and signs of secondary syphilis spontaneously resolve, even without treatment. Without treatment, the latent stage starts.
translations.hypothesis: Secondary syphilis is characterized by a widespread rash that can be easily confused with other infectious or non-infectious conditions. However, it typically affects the palms of the hands and soles of the feet. The rash itself may be subtle and easily overlooked. Large, white or grayish lesions, known as condylomata lata, can also develop in warm, moist areas of the body, such as the groin, armpits, and around the anus. Symptoms of secondary syphilis, including the rash, will often resolve on their own, even without treatment. However, if left untreated, the infection can progress to a latent stage.

translations.source: የተደበቀ ሲፊሊስ ምንም ዓይነት ክሊኒካዊ ህመሞች ወይም ምልክቶች የሉትም። እናም ህክምናው ከመጀመሪያው ደረጃ የተለየ ነው። ለዓመታት ወይም ለአስርተ ዓመታት ህክምና ካልተደረገ የነርቭ፣ የልብና የደም ቧንቧ በሽታዎች ከሌሎች ጋር ሊከሰቱ ይችላሉ። ህክምና: የቂጥኝ ምርመራ የሚደረገው በሰውየው ታሪክ፣ በአካላዊ ምርመራ፣ በላቦራቶሪ ምርመራ እና አንዳንዴም በሬዲዮሎጂ ነው። ምክንያቱም ምልክቶቹ የተለመዱ ወይም የሚስተዋሉ አይደሉም። በላቦራቶሪ ምርመራዎች አማካኝነት ምልክት የሌለው ኢንፌክሽን መለየት እና አዎንታዊ ጒዳዮችን ማከም ተጨማሪ ስርጭትን እና የተወለደ ሲፊሊስን ጨምሮ አሉታዊ የእርግዝና ውጤቶችን ይከላከላል። ለቂጥኝ የሚደረጉ የላቦራቶሪ ምርመራዎች የቲ. ፓሊዲምን ቀጥተኛ ምርመራ ወይም እንደ ሴሮሎጂ ያሉ ቀጥተኛ ያልሆኑ ዘዴዎችን ያካትታሉ።
translations.reference: Latent syphilis presents no clinical symptoms or signs and the treatment is different from the early phase. After years or decades without treatment, neurological and cardiovascular diseases among others may occur. Treatment: Syphilis diagnosis is based on the person’s history, physical examination, laboratory testing and sometimes radiology, as symptoms are not common or noticeable. Identifying asymptomatic infection through laboratory tests and treatment of positive cases will prevent further transmission and adverse pregnancy outcomes, including congenital syphilis. Laboratory tests for syphilis include direct detection of T. pallidum or indirect methods such as serology.
translations.hypothesis: Latent syphilis is a stage of the disease where there are no clinical symptoms or signs. Therefore, the treatment differs from that of other stages. If left untreated for years or even decades, it can lead to serious health problems, including neurological, cardiac, and vascular diseases. Diagnosis: The diagnosis of syphilis is based on a combination of the patient's medical history, physical examination, laboratory tests, and sometimes radiological studies. This is because the symptoms are often non-specific or may not be apparent. Laboratory tests help to identify asymptomatic infections and to treat positive cases, preventing further spread and congenital syphilis, which can have devastating effects on the fetus. Laboratory tests for syphilis include direct tests for *Treponema pallidum* or indirect tests such as serology.

translations.source: ፈጣን ምርመራ እና የምርመራ ሙከራዎች እንዲሁ ይገኛሉ። እናም ውጤቱን ከ 20 ደቂቃ ባነሰ ጊዜ ውስጥ መስጠት ይችላሉ። ይህም ወዲያውኑ ህክምናን ለመጀመር ያመቻቻል። የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ቂጥኝ በሽታው በደረጃው ላይ በመመርኮዝ በ 1 ወይም በ 3 መጠኖች በመርፌ በሚሰጥ ፔኒሲሊን( ቤንዛቲን ፔኒሲሊን ጂ) እንዲታከም ይመክራል። የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ለሲፊሊስ ዝርዝር የህክምና መመሪያዎች አሉት። እና አንደኛው በተለይ በእርግዝና ወቅት ቂጥኝን ለመመርመር እና ለማከም። በዘር የሚተላለፍ ቂጥኝ በአሁኑ ጊዜ በዘር ለሚተላለፍ ቂጥኝ ምንም ዓይነት የምርመራ ሙከራዎች የሉም።
translations.reference: Rapid screening and diagnostic tests are also available and can provide results in less than 20 minutes, facilitating immediate treatment initiation. WHO recommends syphilis to be treated with injectable penicillin (benzathine penicillin G) with 1 or 3 doses, depending on the stage of the disease. WHO has detailed treatment guidelines for syphilis, and one specifically for the screening and treatment of syphilis in pregnancy. Congenital syphilis There are currently no diagnostic tests for congenital syphilis.
translations.hypothesis: Rapid tests and confirmatory assays are also available. These can provide results in less than 20 minutes, allowing for immediate treatment to be initiated. The World Health Organization recommends treating syphilis with penicillin (benzathine penicillin G) administered by injection, with the dosage determined by the stage of the disease. The World Health Organization also provides detailed treatment guidelines for syphilis, including specific recommendations for screening and treating syphilis during pregnancy. Regarding sexually transmitted syphilis, there are currently no specific tests available to differentiate it from other types of syphilis.

translations.source: ቂጥኝ ያለባቸው ሴቶች የሚወልዷቸው ሕፃናትም ሆኑ የሞቱ ሕፃናት በሙሉ በተወለዱበት ጊዜ በዘር የሚተላለፍ ቂጥኝ ምልክት አለመኖሩን መመርመር አለባቸው። በህይወት የተወለዱ ጨቅላ ህጻናት በተወለዱበት ጊዜ የሕክምና መስፈርቶችን ለመወሰን መመርመር እና መሞከር አለባቸው። በሚጠቁሙበት ጊዜ እንዲሁም በወርሃዊ ክፍተቶች ውስጥ ለ 3 ወራት የጨቅላ ሴሮሎጂ እስኪያልቅ ድረስ እና አሉታዊ ሆኖ እስኪቆይ፣ የእናቶች ፀረ-እንግዳ አካላት በእርግዝና ወቅት ወደ ሕፃኑ በቀላሉ ሊተላለፉ ይችላሉ።
translations.reference: All live or stillborn infants of women with syphilis should be examined for evidence of congenital syphilis. Live-born infants should be examined and tested at birth to define treatment requirement. When indicated, also at monthly intervals for 3 months until the infant’s serology are, and remain, negative, as maternal antibodies can be passively transmitted to the baby during pregnancy.
translations.hypothesis: Women who are carriers of genetic conditions should have their children screened for the presence of the genetic marker at birth, regardless of whether the child is born alive or stillborn. Furthermore, newborn infants should be screened to determine any necessary medical interventions. During the breastfeeding period, and for three months after, regular screening of the infant's blood should be conducted to ensure that maternal antibodies have cleared from the infant's system and remain negative. This is because maternal antibodies can sometimes interfere with the infant's own immune response.

translations.source: ታይኒስስ እና ሳይስቲክሴርሲስ አጠቃላይ እይታ፡ በታፕ ትል ታኒያ ሶሊየም ኢንፌክሽን ሁለት የተለያዩ ሁኔታዎችን ሊያስከትል ይችላል። ታይኒስስ እና ሳይስቲክሴርሲስ ታኒያሲስ በታፕ ትል የሚመጣ የአንጀት ኢንፌክሽን ሲሆን አንድ ሰው ጥሬ ወይም ያልበሰለ የተበከለ የአሳማ ሥጋ ሲመገብ ይከሰታል። ታኒያሲስ በሰዎች ጤንነት ላይ ከፍተኛ ተጽዕኖ አያሳድርም። ሆኖም የታፕ ትል እንቁላሎች በታፕ ተሸካሚ ሰገራ ውስጥ ያልፋሉ እናም ተገቢ የንፅህና አጠባበቅ ባለመኖሩ አካባቢውን ሊበክሉ ይችላሉ።
translations.reference: Taeniasis and cysticercosis Overview: Infection with the tapeworm Taenia solium can result in two distinct conditions: taeniasis and cysticercosis. Taeniasis is the intestinal infection with the tapeworm and it occurs when a person eats raw or undercooked, infected pork. Taeniasis has no major impact on human health. However, tapeworm eggs pass in the faeces of the tapeworm carrier and in the absence of a proper sanitation may contaminate the environment.
translations.hypothesis: Understanding Taeniasis and Cysticercosis: Ingestion of *Taenia solium* eggs can lead to two distinct conditions.
