The resolution urges Member States to acknowledge the role of behavioural science in achieving better health outcomes, to identify opportunities for increased use and, to establish functions and units for generating and translating evidence to inform policies and programmes. It also requests the Director-General to mainstream the use of behavioural science within the organization and to provide support to Member States through the development of guidance and the provision of technical assistance. Member States recognized the achievement of the Behavioural Sciences for Better Health Initiative led by the secretariat, congratulated Malaysia, sponsor of the resolution, as well as the other 19 countries that joined as co-sponsors, and thanked the Director-General for the report.
translations.hypothesis: For the first time, a decision was made regarding behavioral science to improve health. Today, member states adopted a resolution that promotes the integration of behavioral science concepts and methods with health topics and public health strategies. The resolution aims to encourage member states to recognize the importance of behavioral science in improving health outcomes and to establish teams that can use behavioral science evidence to inform policies and programs. Furthermore, the Director-General called for the integration of behavioral science into the work of the World Health Organization and for the provision of guidance and support to member states. Member states acknowledged the success of the "Behavioral Science for Health" initiative, which is led by the Secretariat, and expressed their gratitude to Malaysia, which provided financial support, as well as to the other 19 participating countries, and to the Director-General.

translations.source: በዚሁ ዘርፍ በተለይም በክልል መሥሪያ ቤቶች ክህሎትን ማሳደግ እንዲሁም የመረጃ ቋትን በማቋቋም በተለያዩ ዘርፎች ማለትም በትምህርት እና በግሉ ሴክተር ላይ ትብብር ማድረግ እንደሚያስፈልግ አስገንዝበዋል። ተዛማጅ ሰነዶች (Related documents) : EB152 (23) - ለተሻሻለ ጤና የባህሪ ሳይንስ EB152/25 ፦ ለተሻለ ጤና የባህሪ ሳይንስ፣ የዋናው ዳይሬክተር ሪፖርት “የባህሪ ሳይንስ ለተሻለ ጤና” ተነሳሽነትን በተመለከተ የተጠቀሱትን ጉዳዮች በሰነድ A76/7 ራዕ.1 ላይ ተዘርዝረዋል። - የጄኔራል ዳይሬክተር አጠቃላይ ሪፖርት ቀርበዋል።
translations.reference: They stressed the importance of building capacity in this area, particularly in regional offices, and of creating a repository of evidence and synergies between sectors, including with academia and the private sector. Related documents: EB152(23) - Behavioural sciences for better health EB152/25 - Behavioural sciences for better health, Report by the Director-General Behavioural Sciences for Better Health Initiative The above items were discussed as part of the document A76/7 Rev.1 - Consolidated report by the Director-General.
translations.hypothesis:
He emphasized the need to enhance the capacity of regional offices, particularly in this area, and to establish a data platform to facilitate collaboration across various sectors, including education and the private sector.

Related documents:
EB152 (23) - Behavioral Science for Improved Health
EB152/25: Report from the Director-General on "Behavioral Science for Improved Health," with details on the issues mentioned in document A76/7, revision 1.
- The Director-General's comprehensive report has been submitted.

translations.source: ሰባ ስድስተኛው የዓለም ጤና ስብሰባ - ዕለታዊ መረጃ፡ እ.ኤ.አ. ግንቦት 27፣ 2023 በኢንፌክሽን (ሰውነት ጎጂ በሆኑ ረቂቅ ተዋህሲያን መጠቃት) መከላከል እና ቁጥጥር ላይ የመጀመሪያው ዓለም አቀፍ ስልት፦ የዓለም ጤና ጥበቃ ጉባኤ ወደ ሁለት አስርት ዓመታት በሚጠጋው የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት እና አጋሮቹ መሪነት ጥረት የተዘጋጀውን የመጀመሪያውን ዓለም አቀፍ የኢንፌክሽን መከላከል እና ቁጥጥር ስልት ዛሬ አጽድቋል። ይህ ስትራቴጂ ፀረ ተሕዋስያን መድሃኒቶችን የሚቋቋሙትን ጨምሮ ከጤና አጠባበቅ ጋር የተገናኙ ኢንፌክሽኖችን (health care-associated infections (HAIs)) አደጋ በከፍተኛ ሁኔታ ለመቀነስ ለአባላት ሀገራት ስትራቴጂካዊ መመሪያ ይሰጣል። ከጤና አጠባበቅ ጋር የተገናኙ ኢንፌክሽኖች (HAI) በጤና እንክብካቤ አሰጣጥ ወቅት በጣም ከተለመዱት አሉታዊ ክስተቶች መካከል አንዱ ነው። ኮቪድ-19 እና በቅርብ ጊዜ የተከሰቱ ወረርሽኞች እንደ ኢቦላ ፣ የመካከለኛው ምስራቅ የመተንፈሻ አካላት በሽታ (the Middle East respiratory syndrome) እና የሱዳን ቫይረስ በሽታዎች በዓለም ዙሪያ በኢንፌክሽን መከላከል ፕሮግራሞች ላይ ያሉ ክፍተቶችን በግልፅ አሳይተዋል።
translations.reference: Seventy-sixth World Health Assembly – Daily update: 27 May 2023 First global strategy on infection prevention and controlThe World Health Assembly agreed today on the first-ever global strategy on infection prevention and control (IPC), which builds on almost two decades of efforts led by WHO and partners. The strategy provides Member States with strategic directions to substantially reduce the ongoing risk of health care-associated infections (HAIs), including those that exhibit antimicrobial resistance. HAIs are among the most frequent adverse events occurring in the context of health service delivery. The COVID-19 pandemic and recent major disease outbreaks such as Ebola virus disease, the Middle East respiratory syndrome and the Sudan virus disease have clearly exposed the existing gaps in IPC programmes in all countries.
translations.hypothesis: 76th World Health Assembly - Daily Update: May 27, 2023.  First Global Strategy on Infection Prevention and Control: The World Health Assembly today endorsed the first-ever global strategy on infection prevention and control, developed under the leadership of the World Health Organization and its partners over the past two decades. This strategy provides member states with strategic guidance to significantly reduce the burden of infections associated with healthcare, including those caused by antimicrobial-resistant organisms. Healthcare-associated infections (HAIs) are among the most common adverse events during healthcare delivery. The COVID-19 pandemic and recent outbreaks such as Ebola, Middle East respiratory syndrome (MERS), and the Sudan virus have highlighted gaps in infection prevention and control programs around the world.

translations.source: ስልቱ ግልጽ የሆነ ግብ አለው ይህም እ.ኤ.አ. በ2030 ሁሉም የጤና አገልግሎት የሚያገኙ ወይም የሚያቀርቡ ሰዎች ከኢንፌክሽን መጠበቅ ነው። በሦስት ቁልፍ ዓላማዎች ላይ ያተኩራል እነዚም፦ በጤና አጠባበቅ ተቋማት ውስጥ ኢንፌክሽንን መከላከል፣ የኢንፌክሽን መከላከል ፕሮግራሞችን ትግበራ ማረጋገጥ እና ጥረቶችን ከሌሎች ዘርፎች ጋር ማስተባበር ናችው። ስልቱ በጤና አጠባበቅ ስርዓቱ ውስጥ ባሉ ሁሉም ቦታዎች ላይ ደህንነቱ የተጠበቀ የጤና እንክብካቤ አቅርቦትን በማረጋገጥ ላይ ያተኩራል። እንደ መሰረታዊ የጤና መብት ተቆጥሮ፤ ተጠያቂነት እና ዘላቂነት ባለው መንገድ ፍትሃዊ እንክብካቤ የመስጠት መርህ ላይ አፅንዖት ይሰጣል። ከአለም አቀፍ የኢንፌክሽን መከላከል እና ቁጥጥር ስልት ጎን ለጎን እ.ኤ.አ. በ2023 እና 2024 መካከል የተዘጋጀ ተዛማጅ አለም አቀፍ የድርጊት መርሃ ግብር እና የክትትል ማዕቀፍ ይኖራል፣ ይህም ውጤታማነቱን ያሳድጋል። ተዛማጅ ሰነድ፡ EB152/9 - በዋና ዳይሬክተሩ ስለኢንፌክሽን መከላከል እና ቁጥጥር የተዘጋጀ አለም አቀፍ ስትራቴጂ ረቂቅ ማጠቃለያ ዘገባ።
translations.reference: The strategy sets a clear vision: by 2030, everyone accessing or providing health care is safe from associated infections. Its three key objectives are: to prevent infection in health care; act to ensure IPC programmes are in place and implemented; and coordinate IPC activities with other areas and sectors. The strategy is focused on any setting where health care is delivered, across the health system; it is based on the principle of clean and safe care as a fundamental component of the right to health, which is equity driven, and which should ensure accountability and sustainability. The global IPC strategy will be complemented by, and used in conjunction with, an associated global action plan and monitoring framework, that will be developed in 2023–2024. Related document EB152/9 - Draft global strategy on infection prevention and control, Executive summary Report by the Director-General
translations.hypothesis: The strategy has a clear objective: to ensure that everyone who receives or provides healthcare is protected from infection by 2030. It focuses on three key goals: preventing infections within healthcare facilities, strengthening infection prevention and control programs, and coordinating efforts across different sectors. The strategy emphasizes providing safe healthcare services in all areas of the healthcare system. It highlights the principle of providing equitable care, considering healthcare as a fundamental right, and emphasizing accountability and sustainability. Alongside the global strategy for infection prevention and control, a related global action plan and monitoring framework will be developed between 2023 and 2024 to enhance its effectiveness. Related document: EB152/9 - Summary report on the draft global strategy for infection prevention and control, prepared by the Director-General.

translations.source: የዓለም ጤና ጉባኤ ዛሬ ታሪካዊ ውሳኔን አጽድቋል፣ ይህም የመልሶ ማቋቋሚያ አገልግሎቶችን በጤና ስርዓቶች ውስጥ እንዲዋሃድ አድርጓል። የመልሶ ማቋቋሚያ አገልግሎቶች ሰብአዊ መብቶችን ለማስከበር ቁልፍ ሚና ይጫወታሉ ይህም ከፍተኛውን የአካላዊ እና የአዕምሮ ጤና ጨምሮ ነው። የጾታ እና የስነ ተዋልዶ ጤናን እንዲሁም የስራ እና የትምህርት መብቶችንም ለማስከበር ይጠቅማል። ይህ አስደናቂ የውሳኔ ሃሳብ በመልሶ ማቋቋም ላይ ያሉ ተግዳሮቶችን ለመፍታት ያለመ ሲሆን እነዚህም፡ ቅድሚያ የሚሰጣቸው የጤና ሁኔታዎች እና የምርምር አጀንዳዎች ሲመጡ ስለመልሶ ማቋቋም ያለን ግንዛቤ ማሳደግ፣ አስፈላጊ ግብዓቶችን መመደብ፣ ትብብርን ማጎልበት እና የቴክኖሎጂ ሽግግር ማድረግ፣
translations.reference: WHO's work on infection prevention and control Historic resolution paves the way for strengthening rehabilitation in health systemsToday the World Health Assembly agreed a landmark resolution on strengthening rehabilitation in health systems. Rehabilitation services play a key role in ensuring the enjoyment of human rights including the highest attainable standard of physical and mental health. It also promotes sexual and reproductive health, and recognizes the right to work and the right to education. This landmark resolution aims to address the challenges in rehabilitation such as the need to: increase awareness of rehabilitation when setting health priorities and research agendas, allocating resources, promoting cooperation and enabling technology transfer;
translations.hypothesis: Today, the World Health Assembly endorsed a landmark decision that integrates rehabilitation services into health systems. Rehabilitation services play a crucial role in upholding human rights, including the right to the highest attainable standard of physical and mental health. They are essential for promoting sexual and reproductive health, as well as the rights to work and education. This significant decision aims to address the challenges in rehabilitation by: increasing awareness of rehabilitation needs and research priorities, allocating necessary resources, strengthening collaboration, and facilitating technology transfer.

translations.source: በድንገተኛ አደጋዎች ምክኒያት የእርዳታ ቴክኖሎጂን ጨምሮ የመልሶ ማቋቋም ፍላጎት መጨመር በሚኖርበት ጊዜ አገሮች ምላሽ ለመስጠት ዝግጁ መሆናቸውን ማረጋገጥ፤ በአስቸጋሪ እና ተጋላጭ በሆኑ ሁኔታዎች ውስጥ ያሉ ሰዎች ተመጣጣኝ፣ ጥራት ያለው እና ተገቢ የሆነ የመልሶ ማቋቋም አገልግሎቶችን እንዲያገኑ ማድረግ፤ ሰዎች ለማገገሚያ አገልግሎቶች እና አጋዥ ቴክኖሎጂዎች ከኪሳቸው ከፍተኛ ወጭ እንዳያወጡ ማድረግ እና የህዝቡን ፍላጎት ለማሟላት አሁን ያለውን በቂ ያልሆነ የሰለጠነ የሰው ሃይል ችግር መፍታት ናቸው። የውሳኔ ሃሳቡ በዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ሴክሬታሪያት የሚወሰዱ የተለያዩ እርምጃዎችን ይዘረዝራል፣ እነዚህም፡-
translations.reference: ensure countries are better equipped to respond to the sudden increase in rehabilitation needs including assistive technology due to health emergencies; ensure persons in marginalized and vulnerable situations have access to affordable, quality and appropriate rehabilitation services including assistive technology; avoid high out-of-pocket costs for people to access rehabilitation services and assistive technology that can cause financial hardships; and address the current insufficient level of rehabilitation workforce to serve the needs of the population. The resolution lists a range of actions to be taken by the WHO Secretariat such as:
translations.hypothesis: To ensure that countries are prepared to respond to the increasing demand for rehabilitation services, including assistive technology, in the event of emergencies; to ensure that people in vulnerable and challenging situations have access to appropriate, quality, and effective rehabilitation services; to reduce the out-of-pocket costs that people face when accessing rehabilitation services and assistive technology; and to address the current shortage of trained personnel needed to meet the population's needs. The resolution outlines a range of actions to be taken by the World Health Organization Secretariat, including:

translations.source: እ.ኤ.አ. በ2026 መገባደጃ ላይ ሀገራት የመልሶ ማቋቋሚያ አገልግሎቶችን እንዴት በጥሩ ሁኔታ እንደሚሰጡ የሚያሳይ ሪፖርት እንዲያሳትሙ፤ እ.ኤ.አ. በ2030 ሁሉም ሰው የሚፈልገውን የመልሶ ማቋቋም አገልግሎት ማግኘቱን ለማረጋገጥ ግቦችን እና መለኪያዎችን ማውጣት፤ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት አባል ሃገራትን በቴክኒካል መመሪያዎችና ግብዓቶች ለመደገፍ በቂ ገንዘብ እንደመደበ ማረጋገጥ እና፤ አባል ሀገራት ድንገተኛ አደጋዎችን ለመቋቋም እቅዳቸው ውስጥ የመልሶ ማቋቋም እና አጋዥ ቴክኖሎጂ እንዲያካትቱ ናቸው። የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ሴክሬታሪያት እ.ኤ.አ. በ2026፣ 2028 እና 2030 የዚህን የውሳኔ ሃሳብ አፈፃፀም ለጤና ጉባኤው ሪፖርት ያደርጋል።
translations.reference: publishing a baseline report by the end of 2026 with information on the capacity of Member States to respond to rehabilitation needs; developing targets and indicators for effective coverage of rehabilitation services by 2030; ensuring appropriate resources are allocated at WHO to support Member States in implementing technical guidance and resources; and supporting Member States to integrate rehabilitation and assistive technology in their emergency preparedness and response plan. The WHO Secretariat will report on progress in the implementation of this resolution to the Health Assembly in 2026, 2028 and 2030.
translations.hypothesis: By the end of 2026, countries should publish reports demonstrating how they are effectively providing resilience services. By 2030, targets and indicators should be established to ensure that everyone has access to the resilience services they need. The World Health Organization (WHO) should allocate sufficient funding to support member states with technical guidance and resources. Member states should integrate resilience and assistive technology into their plans to prepare for and respond to emergencies. The WHO Secretariat will report to the Health Assembly on the implementation of this decision in 2026, 2028, and 2030.

translations.source: ተዛማጅ ሰነድ (Related document)፡ EB152/8 - በዋና ዳይሬክተሩ የተዘጋጀ በጤና ስርዓት ውስጥ የመልሶ ማቋቋም አገልግሎቶችን የተሻለ ስለማድረግ ሪፖርት። ተዛማጅ አገናኝ (Related link) የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት የመልሶ ማቋቋም ስራ፤ ከላይ የተዘረዘሩት በዋና ዳይሬክተሩ የተጠናከረው A76/7 Rev.1 ሰነድ አካል ናቸው። የምርመራ አቅምን ለማጠናከር ውሳኔ፤
translations.reference: Related document: EB152/8 - Strengthening rehabilitation in health systems, Report by the Director-General Related link WHO's work on rehabilitation The items above were discussed as part of the document A76/7 Rev.1 - Consolidated report by the Director-General. Resolution on strengthening diagnostics capacity
translations.hypothesis: Related document: EB152/8 - A report prepared by the Director-General on improving rehabilitation services within the health system. Related link: World Health Organization rehabilitation program; the above-mentioned document, A76/7 Rev.1, which was strengthened by the Director-General, is part of this. Decision to strengthen assessment capacity.

translations.source: እ.ኤ.አ. በግንቦት 26 አባል ሀገራት የምርመራ አቅምን ለማጠናከር እና የምርመራ አገልግሎት ተደራሽነት ለማሻሻል ውሳኔን አጽድቀዋል። ይህ ሰፊ የውሳኔ ሃሳብ ተላላፊ ለሆኑ፣ ተላላፊ ላልሆኑ፣ ችላ ለተባሉ የሃሩሩ አካባቢ በሽታዎች፣ ላልተለመዱ በሽታዎች፣ ለድንገተኛ አደጋ እና ለአካል ጉዳት መከላከል፣ ክትትል፣ ምርመራ እና ህክምና የምርመራ አገልግሎቶች ወሳኝ መሆናቸውን ግንዛቤ ውስጥ ያስገባል። የምርመራ አገልግሎቶች በሽታዎችን በትክክል ለይቶ ለማወቅ ያስችላሉ፤ ስለዚህም ለተሻለ የጤና ውጤት ትክክለኛ ሕክምና በወቅቱ ለመጀመር ጠቃሚ ናቸው። የውሳኔ ሃሳቡ በላብራቶሪ ከሰውነት በብልቃጥ (in vitro) ናሙና ተወስዶ የሚደረጉ ምርመራዎችን እንደ (rapid diagnostic tests) እና (polymerase chain reaction (PCR)) እንዲሁም በሰውነት አካል ውስጥ እንዳለ (in vivo) የሚደረጉ ምርመራዎች እንደ ኢሜጅንግ ወይም የደም ግፊት መለኪያ ያሉትን ጨምሮ ሁሉንም አይነት የመመርመሪያ ሙከራዎች ያካትታል። ለምርምርና ልማት፣ ለምርታማነት (የአገር ውስጥ ምርትና ቴክኖሎጂ ሽግግርን ጨምሮ)፣ ለመመሪያዎች፣ ለምርጫና ግዥ፣ ለግንዛቤ መፍጠር እና በአጠቃላይ የተደራሽነት ማነቆዎችን ለመፍታት እርምጃዎችን ያካትታላል።
translations.reference: On 26 May Member States endorsed a resolution to strengthen diagnostics capacity in countres and to improve access to diagnostic services. The broad ranging resolution recognizes that diagnostic services are vital for the prevention, surveillance, diagnosis, case management, monitoring and treatment of communicable, noncommunicable, neglected tropical and rare diseases, injuries, and disabilities. Diagnostics allow for the precise identification of diseases, and therefore the timely initiation of the correct treatments for better health outcomes. The resolution considers the full spectrum of “diagnostics”, thus including both “in vitro” laboratory tests e.g. rapid diagnostic tests and polymerase chain reaction (PCR) and “non in vitro” diagnostics e.g. imaging or blood pressure measurement devices. It covers actions for research and development, manufacturing (including local production and technology transfer), regulation, selection and procurement, awareness, advocacy and addressing access barriers in general.
translations.hypothesis: On May 26, 2023, member states adopted a resolution to strengthen diagnostic capacity and improve access to diagnostic services. This comprehensive resolution recognizes the critical role of diagnostic services in the prevention, detection, and treatment of communicable and non-communicable diseases, neglected tropical diseases, emerging and re-emerging infectious diseases, and injuries, as well as in emergency preparedness and response. Diagnostic services enable the accurate identification of diseases, which is essential for initiating appropriate and timely treatment, leading to improved health outcomes. The resolution encompasses all types of diagnostic tests, including laboratory-based *in vitro* tests such as rapid diagnostic tests and polymerase chain reaction (PCR), as well as *in vivo* tests such as imaging and blood pressure measurement. It includes actions related to research and development, productivity (including local manufacturing and technology transfer), guidelines, procurement and supply chain management, awareness-raising, and addressing access barriers.

translations.source: የውሳኔ ሃሳቡ ትግበራ ሀገራት የተሻለ የምርመራ አገልግሎትን እንዲያገኙ ለመርዳት ቀደም ባሉት ጊዜያትና እና በአሁኑ ጊዜ በዓለም ጤና ድርጅት በሶስት ደረጃዎች እየተከናወነ ያለውን ስራ ለመገንባትና ለማስፋፋት ያስችላል። ሴክሬታሪያቱ እ.ኤ.አ. በ2025 የአፈጻጸም ሂደቱን ሪፖርት እንዲያቀርብ ተጠይቆል። ተዛማጅ ሰነድ EB152/6 የምርመራ አቅምን ማጠናከር
translations.reference: The implementation of the resolution will build and expand on previous and current work at three levels of WHO to help countries improve access to diagnostic services. The Secretariat is requested to report on implementation progress in 2025. Related document EB152/6 Strengthening diagnostics capacity
translations.hypothesis: The decision aims to facilitate the development and expansion of a three-tiered system implemented by the World Health Organization to provide conflict-affected countries with improved assessment services. The Secretariat has been requested to report on the progress of the implementation process by 2025. The relevant document is EB152/6, which focuses on strengthening assessment capacity.

translations.source: በሰባ ስድስተኛው የዓለም የጤና ጉባኤ ወቅት ለህብረተሰብ ጤና አስተዋጾ ላደረጉ ሽልማት ተበርክቷል። በጄኔቫ በተካሄደው ሰባ ስድስተኛው የዓለም ጤና ጉባኤ ላይ ለሕዝብ ጤና ከፍተኛ አስተዋጽኦ ላደረጉ ከዓለም ዙሪያ የተውጣጡ ግለሰቦች እና ተቋማትን ሽልማት ተበርክቷል። የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ዋና ዳይሬክተር ዶ/ር ቴዎድሮስ አድሃኖም ገብረእየሱስ "እ.ኤ.አ. የ2023ቱ የህብረተሰብ ጤና ሽልማት ለአለም አቀፍ ጤና ከመደበኛ ስራቸው በላይ ከፍተኛ አስተዋፅዖ ያደረጉ ግለሰቦችን እውቅና ይሰጣል" ብለዋል። "ዓለማችን ፈታኝ ጊዜያት ሲያጋጥሟት፤ እያንዳንዱ ግለሰብ ለህብረተሰብ ጤና እና ደህንነት መሻሻል ልናሳካው የምንችለውን እድገት ማሳያ ሆኖ ያገለግላል።" የዓለም ጤና ጉባኤ ከተጠናቀቀ በኋላ ለእያንዳንዱ ሽልማት እጩ በየዓመቱ መጥሪያ ይላካል።
translations.reference: Awards for outstanding contributions to public health presented during the Seventy-sixth World Health Assembly During a moving ceremony at the Seventy-sixth World Health Assembly in Geneva, awards were presented to persons and institutions from around the world for their outstanding contributions to public health. “The 2023 public health prizes and awards celebrate people who have made a significant contribution to global health, and whose work has extended far beyond the call of normal duty,” said Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, WHO Director-General. “At a time when the world faces many challenges, each is an inspiration and a reminder of the progress that can be made to improve health and wellbeing for all.” The call for nominations of candidates for each prize is sent out each year after closure of the World Health Assembly.
translations.hypothesis: During the 76th World Health Assembly, awards were presented to individuals and organizations for their contributions to public health. At the 76th World Health Assembly, held in Geneva, awards were given to individuals and organizations from around the world who have made significant contributions to public health. Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Director-General of the World Health Organization, stated, "The 2023 Public Health Award recognizes individuals who have made outstanding contributions to global health beyond their regular work." He added, "In these challenging times, each individual serves as an example of the progress we can achieve for the health and well-being of our communities." Following the conclusion of the World Health Assembly, invitations are sent annually to each award nominee.

translations.source: እጩዎቹ የሚመረጡት በአለም ጤና ድርጅት አባል ሀገራት ብሄራዊ የጤና አስተዳደር ወይም ከዚህ ቀደም ሽልማቶችን በተቀበለ ሰው ሊሆን ይችላል። እ.ኤ.አ. በጥር 2023 ባካሄደው 152ኛው ክፍለ ጊዜ የስራ አስፈፃሚ ቦርዱ የ2023 ሽልማት አሸናፊዎችን ሰይሟል። ሰባቱ ተሸላሚዎች በየሀገራቸው እና በአለም አቀፍ ደረጃ ለህብረተሰብ ጤና ያበረከቱትን ልዩ አስተዋፅኦ እውቅና ለመስጠት ተመርጠዋል። ተሸላሚዎቹም አራት የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ክልሎችን ይወክላሉ፡- አሜሪካ፣ ምስራቃዊ-ሜዲትራኒያን፣ ደቡብ-ምስራቅ እስያ እና ምዕራባዊ ፓስፊክ ናቸው። ከአምስቱ የግል አሸናፊዎች መካከል ሦስቱ ሴቶች ናቸው።
translations.reference: Nominations can be made by national health administrations of a WHO Member State and by any former recipient of the prizes. At its 152nd session in January 2023, the Executive Board designated the 2023 winners of the prizes, on the basis of proposals made by the dedicated selection panels for each prize. The seven laureates have been selected to celebrate their unique role and contribution for public health in their countries and globally. They come from four WHO Regions – for the Americas, the Eastern-Mediterranean, South-East Asia and the Western Pacific. Five winners are individuals, and three among them are women.
translations.hypothesis: The nominees for the award are selected by the national health authorities of WHO member states, or by individuals who have previously received awards. In January 2023, during its 152nd session, the Executive Board of the World Health Organization announced the winners of the 2023 awards. The seven winners were selected to recognize their outstanding contributions to public health at both the national and global levels. The winners represent four WHO regions: the Americas, the Eastern Mediterranean, South-East Asia, and the Western Pacific. Of the five individual winners, three are women.

translations.source: በተጨማሪም እ.ኤ.አ. በ 2023 ሽልማቶችን የተቀበሉት ሁለት ተቋማት በሴቶች ይመራሉ ። እነዚህም እ.ኤ.አ. የ 2023 የ”ሳሳካዋ” የጤና ሽልማት አሸናፊዎች፡ የ”ኒክ ሲሞንስ” ተቋም ከኔፓል እና ፕሮፌሰር ቪቻይ ቲየንታቮርን ከታይላንድ ናቸው። “ኒክ ሲሞንስ” ተቋም በኔፓል ገጠራማ አካባቢዎች የጤና እንክብካቤን ለማሻሻል የተቋቋመ መንግሥታዊ ያልሆነ ድርጅት ነው። ከኔፓል የህብረተሰብ ጤና ጥበቃ ሚኒስቴር ጋር በቅርበት በመስራት ተቋሙ በገጠር 7000 የሚሆኑ የህክምና ባለሙያዎችን (እንደ አዋላጆች እና የማደንዘዣ ባለሙያዎች) አሰልጥኗል፤ እነዚህም ባለሙያዎች አጠቃላይ ሐኪሞች እና ስፔሻሊስቶች እጥረት ባለበት አካባቢ በጣም ጠቃሚ ናቸው። ተቋሙ በ44 ሆስፒታሎች ያለውን የህክምና ባለሙያዎች እጥረት ለመቅረፍ አስፈላጊ የሆኑ ቁሳቁሶችንና ተጨማሪ የጤና ባለሙያዎችን አቅርቧል እንዲሁም ከ100 በላይ የገጠር ሆስፒታሎች የተሻለ ጥራት ያለው አገልግሎት ለመስጠት እንዲችሉ የተግባር እቅድ ነድፎ ድጋፍ አድርጓል።
translations.reference: In addition, the two institutions that have received a 2023 award are led by women. Winners of the 2023 prizes Sasakawa Health PrizeThe 2023 laureates: The Nick Simons Institute from Nepal, and Professor Vichai Tienthavorn from Thailand The Nick Simons Institute is a nongovernmental organization aiming to improve health care in the rural areas of Nepal. Working closely with the Ministry of Health and Population of Nepal, the Institute has trained 7000 medical professionals (such as midwives and anaesthetic assistants) in rural areas, who now provide most-needed services to local communities in the absence of a sufficient pool of general practitioners and specialists. The Institute has supplied essential equipment and provided additional health personnel to alleviate the lack of medical professionals in 44 hospitals, and supported the design of an action plan for better quality services in more than 100 rural hospitals.
translations.hypothesis: Furthermore, two of the organizations that received awards in 2023 are led by women. These are the recipients of the 2023 "Sasakawa" Health Prize: the "Nick Simmons" Foundation from Nepal and Professor Vichai Tientavorapong from Thailand. The "Nick Simmons" Foundation is a non-governmental organization established in Nepal to improve healthcare in remote areas. Working closely with the Ministry of Public Health in Nepal, the organization has trained 7,000 healthcare workers (such as community health workers and pharmacy technicians) in rural areas, who are essential in areas where there is a shortage of general practitioners and specialists. The organization has also provided essential supplies and additional healthcare workers to address the shortage of healthcare professionals in 44 hospitals, and has developed and supported practical plans to help over 100 rural hospitals provide higher quality services.

translations.source: ተቋሙ በኔፓል በሚገኙ የገጠር የጤና ሰራተኞች ላይ ተጽእኖ የሚያሳድሩ አመለካከቶችን እና ፖሊሲዎችን ለመቅረጽ የምርምር እንቅስቃሴዎችን ያካሂዳል። ፕሮፌሰር ቪቻይ ቲየንታቮርን በታይላንድ የህዝብ ጤና ጥበቃ ሚኒስቴር የ”ፕራቦራማራጅቻኖክ” (Praboromarajchanok) ተቋም ፕሬዝዳንት ናቸው። ከመሯቸው ሀገር አቀፍ ውጥኖች አንዱ ታላሴሚያን (በዘር የሚተላለፍ የቀይ የደም ሴሎችን የሚያጠቃ በሽታ) ለመቅረፍ የሚረዳ አዲስ የጤና ፖሊሲ ሲሆን በስራው ውጤትም በሽታውን ለመከላከል እና ለመቆጣጠር ውጤታማ የሆነ ሀገራዊ ፖሊሲ ቀርቧል። በተጨማሪም የስኳር በሽታ እና የደም ግፊትን ለመከላከልና ለመቆጣጠር ዜጎች አመጋገብ እና አኗኗራቸውን እንዲያስተካክሉ የሚረዳ ውጤታማ የቅደመ ምርመራ ዘዴ ነድፏል። በዚህም ጥረት ምክኒያት በሀገሪቱ የጤና ገጽታ ላይ ከፍተኛ አወንታዊ ለውጦችን በማምጣት ሀገራዊ ፖሊሲ ተነድፏል።
translations.reference: The Institute also conducts research and advocacy activities to shape perceptions and influence policies that affect rural health workers in Nepal. Professor Vichai Tienthavorn is President of the Praboromarajchanok Institute at the Ministry of Public Health of Thailand. One of the nation-wide initiatives he has led is an innovative health promotion policy to help address thalassemia, with his work resulting in the adoption of an effective national policy for thalassemia prevention and control. He also designed an effective communications tool and screening method to help citizens to adapt their behaviours in order to prevent and control diabetes and hypertension. As a result of this work, a national policy was adopted with significant positive impact on the country’s disease burden.
translations.hypothesis: The institution conducts research to identify factors and policies that impact the effectiveness of rural healthcare workers in Nepal. Professor Vichai Tientavora is the President of the "Praboromarajchanok" Institute at the Ministry of Public Health in Thailand. One of his key national initiatives involved developing a new health policy to address thalassemia (a genetic blood disorder). This work resulted in the implementation of a national policy that effectively prevents and controls the disease. Furthermore, he pioneered an effective screening method to help citizens modify their diet and lifestyle to prevent and manage diabetes and hypertension. As a result of these efforts, a national policy was developed that has brought significant positive changes to the country's health landscape.

translations.source: ፕሮፌሰር ቲየንታቮርን በተጨማሪም በተለይ በታይላንድ ድንበር እና ገጠራማ አካባቢዎች የጤና እንክብካቤ ሰራተኞችን ያልተመጣጠነ ምደባ ችግር ለመፍታት ወሳኝ ሚና ተጫውተዋል ። የሁለተኛ ደረጃ ተማሪዎችን በመመልመል፣ ሥልጠና እና የተፋጠነ የሕክምና ሥርዓተ ትምህርት በመስጠት ወደ ማህበረሰቡ እንዲቀላቀሉ በማድረግ በመንደር ከሚገኙ ትምህርት ቤቶች ጋር አብረው ሰርተዋል። የተባበሩት አረብ ኤሚሬቶች የጤና ፋውንዴሽን ሽልማት እ.ኤ.አ. የ2023 ተቀባይ፡ ዶ/ር ማሪያ አሱንሲዮን ሲልቬስትሬ ሲሆኑ በፊሊፒንስ የታወቁ ሃኪም እና ተመራማሪ ከመሆናቸው በተጨማሪ፣ ዶ/ር ማሪያ ልጆችን እስከ ስድስት ወር ድረስ የእናት ጡትን ብቻ ማጥባት ተከራካሪ እንዲሁም የ”Kalusugan ng Mag-Ina” (የእናት እና ልጅ ጤና) - በፊሊፒንስ ውስጥ መንግስታዊ ያልሆነ ድርጅት መስራች ናቸው። ዶ/ር ሲልቬስትሬ እንደ ማህበረሰብ ባለሙያ ያላትን ልምድ ምርምር ለማድረግ እና የእናቶችን እና ጨቅላ ሕፃናትን ጤና ለማሻሻል ያለመ ፕሮቶኮል ለማዘጋጀት ተጠቅማለች። እሷ ያዘጋጀችው የፈጠራ ፕሮቶኮል እናቶችን በወሊድ ወቅት እንዲሁም ጨቅላ ሕፃናትን ከወሊድ በኋላ ለሚከታተሉ የጤና አጠባበቅ ሠራተኞች ቀላል እርምጃዎችን ይዘረዝራል።
translations.reference: Professor Tienthavorn also played an essential role in addressing the challenge of uneven distribution of the health workforce, especially in border and rural areas of Thailand. He led the establishment of partnerships with village schools to recruit high-school students, train them and help them settle back locally after having accomplished an accelerated medical curriculum. United Arab Emirates Health Foundation Prize2023 award laureate: Dr Maria Asuncion Silvestre from the PhilippinesIn addition to being a renowned clinician and researcher, Dr Maria Asuncion Silvestre is a passionate advocate for exclusive breastfeeding and the founder of Kalusugan ng Mag-Ina (Health of Mother and Child) – a Philippine non-governmental organization. Dr Silvestre has leveraged her experience as a community practitioner to conduct research and design a protocol to help improve the health of mothers and newborns. The pioneering protocol she created describes a simple set of actions to be undertaken by health workers attending a mother during delivery and her newborn after birth.
translations.hypothesis: Professor Tientavorn has also played a crucial role in addressing the issue of unequal distribution of healthcare workers, particularly in border and remote areas of Thailand. He has worked closely with schools in rural areas, involving secondary school students through mentorship, training, and a structured medical education program, to encourage them to join the healthcare profession and serve their communities. The United Arab Emirates Health Foundation Award for 2023 was awarded to Dr. Maria Asuncion Silvestre. In addition to being a renowned physician and researcher in the Philippines, Dr. Silvestre is a strong advocate for exclusive breastfeeding for infants up to six months of age and is the founder of "Kalusugan ng Mag-Ina" (Mother and Child Health), a non-governmental organization in the Philippines. Dr. Silvestre has used her experience as a community professional to conduct research and develop protocols aimed at improving the health of mothers and young children. The innovative protocol she developed provides healthcare workers with simple steps to follow during and after childbirth, making it easier for them to provide care to mothers and newborns.

translations.source: ይህ ፕሮቶኮል በሁሉም ሁኔታዎች ውስጥ ተፈጻሚ ሊሆን የሚችል ሲሆን በእናቶች እና ህጻናት ጤና አጠባበቅ ፍትሃዊነት ላይ ያሉትን ክፍተቶች ለማስተካከል ይረዳል። ከፊሊፒንስ መንግስት እና ከዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ምዕራባዊ ፓሲፊክ ክልል አካባቢ ካሉ 17 ሀገራት ጋር በመተባበር ለጨቅላ ህጻናት እንክብካቤ የመጀመሪያዉን ዘመቻ በማካሄድ ረገድ አስተዋፅዖ አበርክታለች፤ ይህም በዓለም ጤና ድርጅት የሚመከር እና ወጪ ቆጣቢ የሆነ መፍትሄ ነው። የተከበሩ ሼክ ሳባህ አል-አህመድ አል-ጀብር አል-ሳባህ በአረጋውያን ጤና አጠባበቅ እና ጤናማነትን ማስተዋወቅ ላይ ያበረከቱት ሽልማት፤ እ.ኤ.አ. የ2023 ተሸላሚዎች፦ የቻይናው ሥር የሰደዱ እና ተላላፊ ያልሆኑ በሽታዎች መቆጣጠሪያ እና መከላከያ ብሔራዊ ማዕከል እና ዶክተር አብላ መሂዮ ሲባይ ከሊባኖስ ናቸው። የቻይና የበሽታ መቆጣጠሪያና መከላከያ ማዕከል አካል የሆነው ሥር የሰደዱ እና ተላላፊ ያልሆኑ በሽታዎች መቆጣጠሪያ እና መከላከያ ብሔራዊ ማዕከል እ.ኤ.አ. ከ2013 ጀምሮ የአረጋውያንን ጤና በማስተዋወቅ ረገድ ንቁ ተሳትፎ አድርጓል።
translations.reference: This protocol, which is applicable in all resource settings, helps to bridge the health equity gap in mother-and-child care. In partnership with the national government of the Philippines as well as 17 countries in the WHO Western Pacific Region, she has played a key role in scaling up the First Embrace campaign for Early Essential Newborn Care – a set of simple WHO-recommended and cost-effective interventions. His Highness Sheikh Sabah Al-Ahmad Al-Jaber Al-Sabah Prize for Research in Health Care for the Elderly and in Health Promotion 2023 award laureates: The National Center for Chronic and Noncommunicable Disease Control and Prevention from China; and Dr Abla Mehio Sibai from Lebanon The National Center for Chronic and Noncommunicable Disease Control and Prevention, part of the Chinese Center for Disease Control and Prevention (China CDC), has hosted a department focusing on the health of older people since 2013.
translations.hypothesis: This protocol can be applied in all situations and helps to address the gaps in maternal and child health equity. The Philippines, in collaboration with 17 countries in the Western Pacific region of the World Health Organization, has made a significant contribution to the first campaign for newborn care, which is a recommended and cost-effective solution by the World Health Organization. The Sheikh Sabah Al-Ahmad Al-Jaber Al-Sabah Award for Health Development, recognizing contributions to elderly health and the promotion of well-being, was awarded for 2023 to the National Center for Non-Communicable Diseases Control and Prevention of China and Dr. Abla Mehio from Lebanon. The National Center for Non-Communicable Diseases Control and Prevention, which is part of the China Center for Disease Control and Prevention, has been actively involved in promoting elderly health since 2013.

translations.source: ይህ ማዕከል የተለያዩ ፕሮግራሞችን እና ተነሳሽነቶችን ተግባራዊ ያደርጋል፤ ከነዚህም መካከል በአገር አቀፍ ክትትል እና ጥናቶች የበሽታዎችን መንስኤ መረዳት እንዲሁም በማስረጃ ላይ የተመሰረተ ፖሊሲዎችን ጨምሮ የጤና ማስተዋወቅን ማስፋፍት ይጠቀሳሉ። አዳዲስ ተነሳሽነቶቹም የሚከተሉትን ያጠቃልላሉ፤ ለአረጋውያን የሚሆን ልዩ መሣሪያ፣ ለ2000 የመጀመሪያ ደረጃ እንክብካቤ ሠራተኞች ሥልጠና፣ የአረጋዊያንን ጤና ማስተዋወቂያ ሳምንት እንዲሁም በከተማ እና በገጠር የሚኖሩ ከግማሽ ሚሊዮን በላይ የሚሆኑ አዛውንቶችን የአእምሮ ጤና ለማሳደግ የሚያስችል አዲስ ፕሮጀክት ያካትታሉ። ዶ/ር አብላ ሜሂዮ ሲባይ በቤይሩት ሊባኖስ የሚገኘው አሜሪካ ዩኒቨርሲቲ የ”Epidemiology” (የበሽታ ስርጭት ጥናት) ፕሮፌሰር እና የጤና ሳይንስ ፋኩልቲ ዲን ናቸው። በሊባኖስ እና በአካባቢው ላሉ አረጋውያን ጤና ከፍተኛ አስተዋፅዖ አበርክተዋል። ጤናማ እርጅናን ለማስፋፋት ሁለንተናዊ አቀራረብን አስተዋውቃለች፣ ይህም በየዓመቱ ወደ 600 የሚጠጉ አረጋዊያንን በቤሩት የአሜሪካ ዩኒቨርሲቲ መመዝገብ እና የህዝብ ጤና ባለሙያዎችን ማሰልጠንን ጨምሮ ነው።
translations.reference: It delivers a broad range of programmes and activities that advance health promotion for older people, ranging from better understanding the causes of morbidity and mortality through national surveillance for noncommunicable diseases, surveys, death registries and research, to tailored programmes to promote health literacy and evidence-based policies. Initiatives include a dedicated toolkit for older people, capacity‑building of 2000 primary care staff, a health advocacy week dedicated to older people, and an innovative project to improve the mental and cognitive health of more than half a million older persons in both urban and rural areas. Dr Abla Mehio Sibai is Professor of Epidemiology and Dean of the Faculty of Health Sciences at the American University of Beirut in Lebanon. She has made an outstanding contribution to older people’s health in Lebanon and the region, developing a holistic approach to the promotion of healthy ageing. She led the innovative initiative of enrolling close to 600 older people every year at the University for Seniors at the American University of Beirut, in addition to training public health professionals.
translations.hypothesis: This center implements various programs and initiatives, including expanding health promotion through nationwide surveillance and studies to understand the causes of diseases, and developing evidence-based policies. The new initiatives include a specialized program for seniors, training for 2,000 primary care workers, a week-long health promotion campaign for seniors, and a new project aimed at improving the mental health of over half a million older adults living in urban and rural areas. Dr. Abla Mehio Sibai is a professor of Epidemiology and Dean of the Faculty of Health Sciences at the American University of Beirut in Lebanon. She has made a significant contribution to the health of older adults in Lebanon and the region. She has introduced a holistic approach to promoting healthy aging, which includes registering approximately 600 seniors annually at the American University of Beirut and training public health professionals.

translations.source: ዶ/ር ሲባይ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት በሊባኖስ የዓለም አቀፍ የበሽታ ጥናትን መርተዋል (እ.ኤ.አ. በ2000) እና ተላላፊ ባልሆኑ በሽታዎች እና የአደጋ መንስኤዎች ላይ የመጀመሪያውን ሀገር አቀፍ የዳሰሳ ጥናት፣ የፖሊሲ ማውጣት እና ጤና ማስተዋወቅ የፕሮግራም እቅድ አዘጋጅተዋል። በርካታ ስኬቶቿን በማስፋት፣ ዶ/ር ሲባይ በሊባኖስ (እ.ኤ.አ. 2020–2030) “የአረጋውያን ብሄራዊ ስትራቴጂ” ቀርጻለች። ዶ/ር ሲባይ በአከባቢ፣ በክልላዊ እና በአለም አቀፍ ደረጃ በምርምር እና በፖሊሲ መካከል ያለውን ልዩነት ለማስተካከል የእርጅና ጥናት ማዕከል “Centre for Studies on Ageing” እና “Arab Ageing Network” አቋቁሟል። የዶ/ር ሊ ጆንግ-ዎክ ለሕዝብ ጤና የመታሰቢያ ሽልማት፤ እ.ኤ.አ. የ2023 ተሸላሚ፦ ዶ/ር ሆርጅ ፍራንሲስኮ መንሴስ ከጓቲማላ
translations.reference: She co-led the landmark WHO Global Burden of Disease Study in Lebanon (2000) and conducted the first national survey on noncommunicable diseases and risk factors, informing advocacy, policy-making and programme planning for health promotion. Building on her numerous achievements, Dr Sibai has developed the “National Strategy for Older Persons” in Lebanon (2020–2030). Dr Sibai also created the Arab Ageing Network and the Centre for Studies on Ageing to create links between research and policy at the national, regional and international levels. Dr LEE Jong-wook Memorial Prize for Public Health 2023 award laureate: Dr Jorge Francisco Meneses from Guatemala
translations.hypothesis: Dr. Sibai conducted a global study on disease in Lebanon for the World Health Organization (in 2000) and led the first national survey on non-communicable diseases and risk factors, developing policy recommendations and a health promotion program. Building on her numerous successes, Dr. Sibai developed a "National Strategy for Older Adults" for Lebanon (2020-2030). Dr. Sibai established the "Centre for Studies on Ageing" and the "Arab Ageing Network" to bridge the gap between research and policy at the local, regional, and global levels. The Dr. Lee Jong-wook Public Health Award winner for 2023 is Dr. Jorge Francisco Mansés from Guatemala.

translations.source: ከሁለት አስርት ዓመታት በላይ የማህበረሰብን ተሳትፎ በሆስፒታል እንክብካቤ ውስጥ በማስተዋወቅ ባላቸው ልምድ፣ ዶ/ር ሆርጅ ፍራንሲስኮ ሜነስ በታካሚዎች፣ በቤተሰቦቻቸው እና በማህበረሰቡ ደህንነት እና እርካታ ላይ ያተኮሩ ማህበረሰቡን ያማከሉ ኮሚቴዎችን መስርተዋል። የታካሚ እንክብካቤ ጥራትን ለማሳደግ ያደረገው ጥረት ያመጣውን አወንታዊ ተፅእኖ በመገንዘብ የነደፈው ታካሚን ማዕከል ያደረገ የእንክብካቤ ሞዴል በአገር አቀፍ ደረጃ ተተግብሯል። በአሁኑ ጊዜ በጓቲማላ የብሔራዊ ሆስፒታል ተቆጣጣሪ ሆኖ በማገልገል ላይ ሲሆን፣ ለታካሚዎች እና ለቤተሰቦቻቸው የአገልግሎት ጥራትን ለማሳደግ ያለመ ፕሮግራም አዘጋጅቷል። ዶ/ር መንሴ በቅርቡ በደቡብ ጓቲማላ አካባቢ የሀገሪቱ የመጀመሪያ የሆነውን የተቀናጀ የጤና አገልግሎት መረብ ለመመስረት የሚያስችል የፈጠራ ተነሳሽነትን መርተዋል፤ በዚህም አገልግሎት ለማያገኙ የማህበረሰብ ክፍሎች ቅድሚያ ሰጥተዋል። የእሳቸው ተነሳሽነት በመካከለኛው አሜሪካ የመጀመሪያው ብሔራዊ የተቀናጀ የጤና አገልግሎት አውታር እንዲፀድቅ አድርጓል።
translations.reference: Having promoted community participation in hospital care for over 20 years, Dr Jorge Francisco Meneses set up community-based committees focusing on the safety and satisfaction of patients, their families and the community. Given the positive impact of his work in improving the quality of patient care, the model of ethical and patient-centred care that he designed was scaled up at the national level. Nowadays, as the national supervisor for hospitals of Guatemala, he has designed a programme to improve the quality of services for hospital patients and their families. Dr Meneses has recently coordinated an innovative pilot project in the Guatemala South Health Area to build the first national integrated network of health services, with a priority focus on underserved communities. His work led to the adoption of a national strategy of integrated health services networks, the first in Central America.
translations.hypothesis: With over two decades of experience in promoting community engagement in hospital care, Dr. Jorge Francisco Menes has established community-focused committees that prioritize the safety and satisfaction of patients, their families, and the community. Recognizing the positive impact of his efforts to improve the quality of patient care, a patient-centered care model has been implemented nationwide. Currently serving as the National Hospital Supervisor in Guatemala, he has developed programs aimed at improving the quality of services for patients and their families. Dr. Menes recently spearheaded an innovative initiative to establish the country's first integrated healthcare network in southern Guatemala, prioritizing underserved communities. This initiative led to the approval of the first national integrated healthcare network in Central America.

translations.source: የኔልሰን ማንዴላ የጤና ሽልማት፤ እ.ኤ.አ. የ2023 ተሸላሚ፦ ዶ/ር ማርያም አትቢ አል ጃላህማ ከባህሬን። የባህሬን ብሔራዊ የጤና ቁጥጥር ባለስልጣን ዋና ስራ አስፈፃሚ ሆነው ከተጫወቱት ሚና ጎን ለጎን ዶ/ር ማሪያም አቲቢ አል ጃላህማ በሀገራቸው ውስጥ ባሉ የመንግስት እና የትምህርት ተቋማት ውስጥ ቁልፍ የአመራር ቦታዎች ላይ ሠርተዋል። ዶ/ር አል ጃላህማ በመጀመሪያ ደረጃ የጤና እንክብካቤ፣ የህጻናት እና የጉርምስና ዕድሜ ጤና፣ ሥር የሰደዱ በሽታዎች እና ማጨስ ማቆም ላይ ያተኮሩ በርካታ ሀገራዊ እና ክልላዊ ስልቶችን እና እቅዶችን በመቅረጽ ትልቅ አስተዋፅዖ አድርገዋል። ዶ/ር አል ጃላህማ አሁን ባለችበት የስራ መደብ ለጤና ተቋማት ብሄራዊ እውቅና አሰጣጥ ስርዓት ትግበራ፣ የብሄራዊ ጤና ቁጥጥር ባለስልጣን ወደ ዲጂታል አሰራር ለውጥ እንዲሁም በጤና አጠባበቅ ዘርፍ ውስጥ ቅልጥፍና እና የላቀ ደረጃ ላይ ያተኮሩ የተለያዩ ውጤታማ ስራዎችን በመምራት ላይ ትገኛለች። .
translations.reference: Nelson Mandela Award for Health Promotion 2023 award laureate: Dr Mariam Athbi Al Jalahma from Bahrain In addition to being the Chief Executive Officer of the National Health Regulatory Authority of Bahrain, Dr Mariam Athbi Al Jalahma has held various leadership positions in government and academic institutions in her country. Dr Al Jalahma has played a major role in the development of numerous national and region-wide strategies and plans in the field of primary healthcare, child and adolescent health, chronic diseases and antismoking. In her current role, Dr Al Jalahma has led major successful initiatives, such as the national accreditation system for health institutions, the digital transformation of the National Health Regulatory Authority, and several initiatives leading to increased efficiency and excellence in the health sector.
translations.hypothesis: The Nelson Mandela Health Award for 2023 has been awarded to Dr. Mariam Atbi Al-Jalahma from Bahrain. In addition to her role as CEO of the Bahraini National Health Regulatory Authority, Dr. Al-Jalahma has held key leadership positions in government and educational institutions within her country. Dr. Al-Jalahma has made significant contributions by developing numerous national and regional strategies and plans focused on primary healthcare, child and adolescent health, non-communicable diseases, and smoking cessation. Currently, Dr. Al-Jalahma is leading various impactful initiatives, including the national accreditation system for healthcare institutions, the digital transformation of the National Health Regulatory Authority, and efforts to improve efficiency and quality within the healthcare sector.

translations.source: በሁሉም የባህረ ሰላጤ የትብብር ህብረት ሀገራት ውስጥ በተለያዩ መድረኮች ጤናማነትን በማስተዋወቅ ታዋቂ ተሟጋችም ነች።
translations.reference: She is also a prominent voice for health promotion across various channels throughout all countries of the Gulf Cooperation Council.
translations.hypothesis: She is a well-known advocate for health promotion in various forums across all the countries that are members of the Indian Ocean Rim Association.

translations.source: የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት እና የኮሪያ ሪፐብሊክ የባዮማኑፋክቸሪንግ (የባዮሎጂ ስርዓቶችን በመጠቀም ምርትን ማምረት) አቅምን ለማሳደግ ከፍተኛ ስምምነት ላይ ደርሰዋል። በባዮማኑፋክቸሪንግ (የባዮሎጂ ስርዓቶችን በመጠቀም ምርትን ማምረት) ላይ ያተኮረ ዓለም አቀፍ የሥልጠና ማዕከል ለመፍጠር የመግባቢያ ስምምነት ተፈራርመዋል። ይህ የሥልጠና ተቋም ሁሉንም ዝቅተኛ እና መካከለኛ ገቢ ያላቸውን አገሮች ያገለግላል፣ ይህም እንደ ክትባቶች፣ ኢንሱሊን፣ antibodies (ያልተፈለገ ንጥረ ነገር ወደ ሰውነት ሲገባ የሚከላከሉ ፕሮቲኖች) እና የካንሰር መድኃኒቶች ያሉ ምርቶችን እንዲያመርቱ ያስችላቸዋል። የኮቪድ-19 ወረርሽኝ አስፈላጊ የጤና ምርቶችን ፍትሃዊ ተደራሽነት ለማረጋገጥ ዝቅተኛ እና መካከለኛ ገቢ ያላቸው አገሮች ውስጥ የአካባቢ እና ክልላዊ የክትባት ምርትን የማስፋፋት አስፈላጊነት አሳይቷል። የmRNA (በሴሎች ውስጥ መልዕክትን አስተላላፊ ፕሮቲን) ክትባቶችን ለማምረት የሚያስችል እውቀት ለሃገሮች ለማጋራት፤ በአለም ጤና ድርጅት ከአጋር ድርጅቶች ጋር በመተባበር የmRNA ቴክኖሎጂ ሽግግር መርሃ ግብርን በደቡብ አፍሪካ ማዕከል እ.ኤ.አ. በሚያዝያ 2023 በይፋ ተመርቋል።
translations.reference: WHO and Republic of Korea sign landmark agreement to boost biomanufacturing capacity WHO and the Republic of Korea have signed a Memorandum of Understanding to establish a global training hub in biomanufacturing. This global training centre will serve all low- and middle-income countries (LMICs) to produce biologicals, such as vaccines, insulin, monoclonal antibodies, and cancer medicines. The COVID-19 pandemic highlighted the need to expand the local and regional production of vaccines and other essential health products in LMICs to ensure equitable access. To provide countries with the know-how to rapidly produce mRNA vaccines, WHO, in collaboration with partners, established the mRNA Technology Transfer programme, with the formal inauguration of the Hub facility in South Africa in April 2023.
translations.hypothesis: The World Health Organization (WHO) and the Republic of Korea have reached a significant agreement to enhance biomanufacturing capacity. A memorandum of understanding has been signed to establish an international training center focused on biomanufacturing. This training institution will serve all low- and middle-income countries, enabling them to produce essential products such as vaccines, insulin, antibodies, and cancer medications. The COVID-19 pandemic highlighted the need to expand local and regional vaccine production in low- and middle-income countries to ensure equitable access to essential health products. To share knowledge and enable countries to produce mRNA (messenger RNA) vaccines, the WHO, in collaboration with partner organizations, officially launched the mRNA Technology Transfer Hub in South Africa in April 2023.

translations.source: ይህ የጋራ ተነሳሽነት እና ሌሎች የቅርብ ጊዜ የሀገር ውስጥ የምርት ውጥኖች በምርት ውስጥ ልዩ ችሎታ ያላቸው በእነዚህ ዘርፎች ውስጥ የሚሰሩ ሰራተኞችን ለመቅጠር፣ ለማሰልጠን እና የተካኑ ሰራተኞችን ለማቆየት አጠቃላይ የሰው ሃይል ልማት ስትራቴጂዎችን ይፈልጋል። ይህንን የክህሎት ክፍተት ለመቅረፍ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት እና የኮሪያ ሪፐብሊክ ከአንድ አመት በላይ በቅርበት ሲሰሩ የቆዩ ሲሁን ይህም አለምአቀፍ የስልጠና ማዕከል ለመመስረት ያለመ ነው። "ይህንን የመግባቢያ ሰነድ እውን በማድረግ፣ አጋርነታችንን ወደ ላቀ ደረጃ በማሸጋገር እና አጠቃላይ የሥልጠና ሥርዓተ ትምህርት በማዘጋጀት ላይ በመተባበራችን በጣም ደስተኞች ነን። አላማችን ይህ ከፍተኛ ደረጃ ያለው የስልጠና ማዕከል፤ የሰለጠነ የሰው ሀይል ማፍራት እና ጥራት ያለው የጤና ምርቶችን በአለም አቀፍ ደረጃ ማምረት ማስቻል ነው።" ሲሉ የመድሀኒት እና የጤና ምርቶች ተደራሽነት ዋና ዳይሬክተር ዶክተር ዩኪኮ ናካታኒ ተናግረዋል። የኮሪያ ሪፐብሊክ መንግሥት አጠቃላይ ባዮማኑፋክቸሪንግን (የባዮሎጂ ስርዓቶችን በመጠቀም ምርትን ማምረት) የሚሸፍኑ በርካታ ብሔራዊ የሥልጠና ተቋማትን እና መርሃ ግብሮችን እያቀረበ ሲሆን እ.ኤ.አ. በ2025 ሊጀመር በተያዘው ዓለም አቀፍ የባዮማኑፋክቸሪንግ ትምህርት ቤት ግንባታ ሲጠናቀቅ ከሌሎች ሀገራት ብዙ ሰልጣኞችን ለማስተናገድ ተደራሽነቱን በማስፋት ላይ ይገኛል።
translations.reference: This multilateral effort and other recent local production initiatives call for ambitious workforce development strategies to recruit, upskill and retain the staff working at these facilities, with unique competencies required in bioproduction. To address this gap, WHO and the Republic of Korea have engaged in closed collaboration for over a year and are now formalizing their commitment towards the establishment of a global training hub in biomanufacturing. “We are delighted to sign this Memorandum of Understanding to elevate our partnership to the next level and collaborate in the development of a comprehensive training curriculum. We hope this world-class training hub will help to build a skilled workforce and ensure quality health products can be manufactured around the world,” said Dr Yukiko Nakatani, WHO Assistant Director-General, Access to Medicines and Health Products. The Government of the Republic of Korea is offering a large range of national training facilities and programmes covering the full spectrum of biological manufacturing and is expanding its operations to accommodate more trainees from other countries with the construction of a Global Bio Education Campus to be launched in 2025.
translations.hypothesis: This collaborative initiative and other recent domestic manufacturing agreements seek comprehensive human resource development strategies to recruit, train, and retain skilled workers in these sectors, who possess specialized expertise in manufacturing. To address this skills gap, the World Health Organization and the Republic of Korea have been working closely together for over a year to establish an international training center. "We are delighted to be working together to realize this memorandum of understanding, strengthening our partnership and collaborating on the development of a comprehensive training program. Our goal is to create a world-class training center that will produce a skilled workforce and enable the production of high-quality health products on a global scale," said Dr. Yukiko Nakatani, Director of the Department of Access to Medicines and Health Products. The Republic of Korea is investing in numerous national training institutions and programs that cover the entire biomanufacturing process. With the completion of the International Biomanufacturing School, scheduled to open in 2025, the country aims to expand its capacity to train professionals from other countries.

translations.source: "ይህ የመግባቢያ ስምምነት በኮሪያ ሪፐብሊክ እና በአለም ጤና ድርጅት መካከል ያለውን ትብብር እንደሚያጠናክር አምናለሁ፣ ዝቅተኛ እና መካከለኛ ገቢ ያላቸውን አገሮችን የማምረት አቅም ያሳድጋል።" ያሉት የኮሪያ ሪፐብሊክ የጤና ጥበቃ ሚኒስቴርና የዓለም አቀፍ የክትባት ማዕከል ዋና ዳይሬክተር የሆኑት ሁዋንግ ሴንጊዮን ናቸው። በተመሳሳይም የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት የቁጥጥር ስርዓቶችን ለማጠናከር እንዲሁም የጤና ምርቶችን ጥራት እና ደህንነት ለማረጋገጥ ጥረቶችን በማድረግ ላይ ይገኛል። የተጠናከሩ የቁጥጥር ተቋማት መኖር በአገር ውስጥ በተመረቱ ምርቶች ላይ እምነትን ያሳድጋል እንዲሁም የተሳሳቱ፣ ደህንነታቸውን እና ደረጃቸውን ያልጠበቁ ወይም የተጭበረበሩ መድሃኒቶችን ለማስቆም ይጠቅማል።
translations.reference: “I hope this Memorandum of Understanding will enhance cooperation between the Republic of Korea and WHO to strengthen biomanufacturing capacities of LMICs,” said Mr Hwang Seunghyun Director General, Global Vaccine Hub Office at the Ministry of Health and Welfare from Republic of Korea. In parallel, WHO is intensifying regulatory system strengthening to ensure the quality and safety of health products. Stronger regulatory agencies will also enhance confidence in locally produced products and counter misinformation and the availability of unsafe substandard or falsified medicines.
translations.hypothesis: "I believe this memorandum of understanding will strengthen the cooperation between the Republic of Korea and the World Health Organization, and enhance the manufacturing capacity of low- and middle-income countries," said Huang Seung-gyun, Minister of Health and Welfare of the Republic of Korea and Director-General of the International Vaccine Institute. Similarly, the World Health Organization is working to strengthen regulatory systems and ensure the quality and safety of health products. Strong regulatory institutions foster confidence in domestically produced products and help prevent the circulation of substandard, unsafe, or counterfeit medicines.

translations.source: የአለም ጤና ድርጅት መንግስታት ለትምባሆ እርሻ የሚያደርጉትን ድጎማ እንዲያቆሙ ጠይቋል። በአለም የትምባሆ አልባ ቀን፣ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ለትምባሆ ልማት የሚሰጠውን የገንዘብ ድጋፍ እንዲያቆሙ እና በምትኩ በሚሊዮኖች ለሚቆጠሩ ሰዎች ምግብ የሚያቀርቡ ተጨማሪ ዘላቂ ሰብሎችን እንዲያስተዋውቁ አሳስቧል። "ትንባሆ በዓመት 8 ሚሊዮን ሰዎችን ይገድላል፣ ነገር ግን በዓለም ዙሪያ ያሉ መንግስታት ለትንባሆ ልማት በሚሊዮኖች የሚቆጠሩ የገንዘብ ድጋፎችን ያደርጋሉ።" ሲሉ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ዋና ዳይሬክተር ዶክተር ቴዎድሮስ አድሃኖም ገብረእየሱስ ተናግረዋል። "ከትንባሆ ይልቅ የምግብ ሰብሎችን በማምረት ለጤና ቅድሚያ እንሰጣለን፣ ስነ-ምህዳርን እንጠብቃለን እንዲሁም ለሁሉም ሰው የምግብ ዋስትናን እናሻሽላለን።" ሲሉም አክለዋል። በዓለም ዙሪያ ከ300 ሚሊዮን በላይ ሰዎች ለከፋ የምግብ ዋስትና እጦት የተዳረጉ ናቸው።
translations.reference: WHO urges governments to stop subsidizing life-threatening tobacco crops On World No Tobacco Day, WHO urges governments to stop subsidizing tobacco farming and support more sustainable crops that could feed millions. “Tobacco is responsible for 8 million deaths a year, yet governments across the world spend millions supporting tobacco farms,” said Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, WHO Director-General. “By choosing to grow food instead of tobacco, we prioritize health, preserve ecosystems, and strengthen food security for all.” More than 300 million people globally are faced with acute food insecurity.
translations.hypothesis: The World Health Organization (WHO) has called on governments to stop subsidizing tobacco farming. On World No Tobacco Day, the WHO urged countries to end financial support for tobacco production and instead invest in sustainable crops that can feed the millions who face food insecurity. "Tobacco kills 8 million people every year, yet governments around the world provide billions of dollars in subsidies for tobacco production," said WHO Director-General Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus. "We must prioritize food security by investing in crops that nourish people, protect the environment, and ensure food for all." More than 300 million people worldwide are facing severe food insecurity.

translations.source: ይህ በእንዲህ እንዳለ ከ120 በሚበልጡ አገሮች ውስጥ ከ3 ሚሊዮን ሄክታር በላይ መሬት ጎጂ የሆኑ ትምባሆዎችን ለማምረት ይውላሉ፣ ሌላው ቀርቶ ሰዎች በረሃብ በሚሰቃዩባቸው አገሮችም ጭምር። “ትምባሆ ሳይሆን፤ ምግብ እናሳድግ” በሚል ርዕስ የወጣው አዲሱ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ሪፖርት የትምባሆ እርሻን ጉዳቶች እንዲሁም ወደ ዘላቂ የምግብ ሰብሎች መሸጋገር ለገበሬዎች፣ ለማህበረሰቡ፣ ለኢኮኖሚ፣ ለአካባቢ እና ለአለም ህዝብ ያለውን ፋይዳ ይዘረዝራል። ሪፖርቱ የትምባሆ ኢንዱስትሪው አርሶ አደሮችን በእዳ አዙሪት ውስጥ በማጥመድ፣ የትምባሆ ልማትን ኢኮኖሚያዊ ጠቀሜታ በማጋነን እና በግብርና ግንባር ቡድኖች በኩል በግድ እንዲያመርቱ በማስገደድ እንደሚሰራ አጋልጧል። የትምባሆ እርባታ የአርሶ አደሮችን ጤና አደጋ ላይ ይጥላል፣ ከ1 ሚሊየን በላይ ህጻናት በጉልበት ሰራተኝነት በትምባሆ እርሻ ላይ እንደሚሰሩ ይገመታል፣ በዚህም የትምህርት እድሎችን እያጡ ነው። "ትምባሆ ለምግብ ዋስትና ብቻ ሳይሆን ለአጠቃላይ ጤና፣ የትምባሆ ገበሬዎችን ጨምሮ ከፍተኛ ስጋት ይፈጥራል።
translations.reference: Meanwhile more than 3 million hectares of land across more than 120 countries are being used to grow deadly tobacco, even in countries where people are starving. A new WHO report, “Grow food, not tobacco,” highlights the ills of tobacco growing and the benefits of switching to more sustainable food crops for farmers, communities, economies, the environment, and the world at large. The report also exposes the tobacco industry for trapping farmers in a vicious cycle of debt, propagating tobacco growing by exaggerating its economic benefits and lobbying through farming front groups. Tobacco farming causes diseases to the farmers themselves and more than 1 million child laborers are estimated to be working on tobacco farms, missing their opportunity for an education. “Tobacco is not only a massive threat to food insecurity, but health overall, including the health of tobacco farmers.
translations.hypothesis: While over 120 countries cultivate tobacco on more than 3 million hectares of land, even in countries where people are struggling with hunger, a new report from the World Health Organization (WHO) titled "Tobacco vs. Food: Growing Food, Not Tobacco" highlights the detrimental effects of tobacco farming and the benefits of transitioning to sustainable food crops for farmers, communities, economies, the environment, and the global population. The report reveals how the tobacco industry traps farmers in debt, exaggerates the economic benefits of tobacco production, and forces agricultural communities to produce tobacco against their will. Tobacco farming poses significant health risks to farmers, with over 1 million children estimated to be working in tobacco fields, depriving them of educational opportunities. "Tobacco not only threatens food security but also overall health, posing a significant risk to tobacco farmers and the wider population."

translations.source: አርሶ አደሮች ለኬሚካላዊ ፀረ ተባይ መድኃኒቶች፣ የትምባሆ ጭስ እና በ50 ሲጋራዎች ውስጥ ለሚገኘው ኒኮቲን ተጋልጠዋል፤ ይህም እንደ ሥር የሰደደ የሳንባ ሕመም እና የኒኮቲን መመረዝ ላሉ በሽታዎች ይዳርጋል።” ሲሉ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት የጤና ማስተዋወቅ ዳይሬክተር ዶክተር ሩዲገር ክሬች አብራርተዋል። የትምባሆ ልማት ዓለም አቀፍ ችግር ነው። ትኩረቱ በአብዛኛው በእስያ እና በደቡብ አሜሪካ ላይ ቢሆንም፣ የቅርብ ጊዜ መረጃዎች እንደሚያሳዩት የትምባሆ አምራች ድርጅቶች አሁን ሥራቸውን ወደ አፍሪካ እያስፋፉ ነው። እ.ኤ.አ. ከ2005 ጀምሮ በመላው አፍሪካ የትምባሆ እርሻ መሬት ስፋት 20 በመቶ ገደማ ጨምሯል። በኬንያ እና በዛምቢያ የሚገኙ ከ5000 በላይ አርሶ አደሮች ከትንባሆ ይልቅ ወደ ዘላቂ የምግብ ሰብሎች እንዲሸጋገሩ ለማድረግ ያለመ ከትንባሆ ነፃ እርሻዎች ተነሳሽነትን የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ፣ የምግብ እና የእርሻ ድርጅት እንዲሁም የዓለም የምግብ ፕሮግራም ድጋፍ እየሰጡ ይገኛሉ።
translations.reference: Farmers are exposed to chemical pesticides, tobacco smoke and as much nicotine as found in 50 cigarettes – leading to illnesses like chronic lung conditions and nicotine poisoning,” said Dr Ruediger Krech, Director of Health Promotion at WHO. Tobacco growing is a global problem. The focus has so far been in Asia and South America, but the latest data show tobacco companies are expanding to Africa. Since 2005, there has been a nearly 20% increase in tobacco farming land across Africa. WHO, the Food and Agriculture Organization and the World Food Programme support the Tobacco Free Farms initiative that will provide help to more than 5000 farmers in Kenya and Zambia to grow sustainable food crops instead of tobacco.
translations.hypothesis: "Farmers are exposed to chemical pesticides, tobacco smoke, and nicotine, which is present in up to 50 cigarettes, increasing their risk of diseases such as lung cancer and nicotine addiction," explained Dr. Rudiger Krech, Director of Health Promotion at the World Health Organization. "Tobacco cultivation is a global problem. While the focus has largely been on Asia and South America, recent data shows that tobacco companies are now expanding their operations into Africa. Since 2005, the area of land used for tobacco cultivation in Africa has increased by approximately 20 percent." The World Health Organization, the Food and Agriculture Organization, and the World Food Programme are supporting a tobacco-free farming initiative aimed at helping over 5,000 farmers in Kenya and Zambia transition from tobacco to more sustainable food crops.

translations.source: በየዓመቱ የዓለም “ትምባሆ የሌለበት ቀን” ትንባሆን ለመቆጣጠር በሚደረገው ጥረት አስተዋጽኦ የሚያደርጉ ግለሰቦችን እና ድርጅቶችን አውቀና ይሰጣል። በዚህ አመት ከተሸላሚዎቹ መካከል ወይዘሮ ስፕሪና ሮቢ ቻቻ የተባሉት ኬንያዊት ሴት አርሶ አደር አንዷ ሲሆኑ ከትንባሆ እርባታ ወደ ከፍተኛ ባቄላ ልማት በመሸጋገር እንዲሁም በመቶዎች የሚቆጠሩ አርሶ አደሮችን ተመሳሳይ ስራ እንዲሰሩ በማሰልጠን ሽልማት ተሰጥቷቸዋል። 182 የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት አባላት “ለትንባሆ ሠራተኞች እና አብቃዮች አዋጭ ኢኮኖሚያዊ አማራጮችን ለማስተዋወቅ” ቃል ገብተዋል። ይህንን ቁርጠኝነት የሚወጡበት ቁልፍ መንገድ ለትምባሆ ልማት የሚደረገውን ድጎማ ማቋረጥ እና በምትኩ ጤናማ ሰብሎችን ማምረትን በመደገፍ ነው። ከትንባሆ ይልቅ ምግብ ለማምረት በመምረጥ ለጤንነት ቅድሚያ እንሰጣለን፤ ስነ-ምህዳሮችን እንጠብቃለን እናም የምግብ ዋስትናን እናሳድጋለን።
translations.reference: Every year World No Tobacco Day honours those making a difference in tobacco control. This year one of the Awardees, Ms Sprina Robi Chacha, a female farmer from Kenya, is being recognized for not only switching from growing tobacco to high protein beans, but also training hundreds of other farmers on how to do this to create a healthier community. 182 Parties to the WHO Framework Convention on Tobacco Control have committed to “...promote economically viable alternatives for tobacco workers and growers”. A crucial way that countries can fulfill this obligation is by ending subsidies for tobacco growing and supporting healthier crops. By choosing to grow food instead of tobacco, we prioritize health, preserve ecosystems, and increase food security.
translations.hypothesis: Each year, the "World No Tobacco Day" recognizes individuals and organizations that contribute to efforts to control tobacco use. This year, among the winners was Ms. Sprina Robi Chacha, a Kenyan farmer, who received an award for transitioning from tobacco farming to high-value bean production, and for training hundreds of other farmers to do the same. 182 members of the World Health Organization have pledged to "promote viable economic alternatives for tobacco workers and communities." A key way to fulfill this commitment is to phase out subsidies for tobacco production and, instead, support the cultivation of healthy crops. By choosing to produce food instead of tobacco, we prioritize health, protect the environment, and promote food security.

translations.source: ኢኮኖሚን እንዴት መቀየር እንደሚቻል የሚያሳይ ጠቃሚ ሪፖርት፤ ይህም ሁሉም ሰው ጥሩ የጤና እንክብካቤ እንዲያገኝ ለማረጋገጥ ነው። በዓይነቱ ለመጀመሪያ ጊዜ ባዘጋጀው ሪፖርቱ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ምክር ቤት ለሁሉም ማህበረሰብ የጤና ተደራሽነትን ለማረጋገጥ ኢኮኖሚን እንደገና ለማዋቀር ቆራጥነት የተሞላው አዲስ መንገድን አቅርቧል። በዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ዋና ዳይሬክተር ዶ/ር ቴዎድሮስ አድሃኖም ገብረእየሱስ እ.ኤ.አ. በህዳር 2020 ለኮቪድ-19 ወረርሽኝ ምላሽ ለመስጠት የተቋቋመው እና በፕሮፌሰር ማሪያና ማዙካቶ የሚመራው ምክር ቤቱ ያለፉትን ሁለት አመታት ለሁሉም ህብረተሰብ የጤና ተደራሽነት ለማረጋገጥ ሲባል ኢኮኖሚውን በመገምገም አሳልፏል፤ የሰዎች እና የዓለማችን ደህንነት የማህበራዊ፣ የጤና እና የኢኮኖሚ ስርዓታችን እንዲሁም የፖሊሲያችን የመሰረት ድንጋይ መሆን አለበት በሚል መርህም ይመራል ። ምክር ቤቱ ከታች በተዘረዘሩት አራት ተያያዥ ጉዳዮች ላይ በማተኮር ኢኮኖሚውን ለመቀየር በአዲስ እውቀት ላይ የተመሰረተ ቆራጥነት የተሞላው አዲስ መንገድን አቅርቧል።
translations.reference: Landmark report charts route for reorienting economies to deliver health for all In the first-ever report of its kind, the WHO Council on the Economics of Health for All has outlined a bold new path to reorient economies to deliver what matters - health for all. The Council, created by WHO Director-General Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus in November 2020 in response to the COVID-19 pandemic, and chaired by Professor Mariana Mazzucato, has spent that last two years rethinking the economy from a health for all perspective, and pushing forcefully the principle that human and planetary health must be at the heart of how we design our social, health and economic systems and policies. The Council has put forward a bold new narrative grounded in new economic wisdom to reorient economies to deliver health for all across four interrelated themes:
translations.hypothesis: A crucial report outlines how to transform the economy to ensure that everyone has access to quality healthcare. In a report of its kind, the World Health Organization (WHO) Council has presented a determined new approach to restructuring the economy in order to guarantee health equity for all communities. According to WHO Director-General Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, the Council, established in November 2020 to respond to the COVID-19 pandemic and led by Professor Mariana Mazzucato, has spent the past two years examining the economy with the goal of ensuring health equity for all. It is guided by the principle that the safety and well-being of people and the planet must be the foundation of our social, health, and economic systems, as well as our policies. The Council has presented a determined new approach, based on new knowledge, to transform the economy, focusing on the four interconnected areas listed below.

translations.source: እሴት - በአዳዲስ ኢኮኖሚያዊ አመለካከቾች አስፈላጊ የሆኑትን ነገሮች ማወቅ እና መመዘን፤ ፋይናንስ - የጤና ተደራሽነትን ከአጭር ጊዜ ወጪ ይልቅ ወደ ረጅም ጊዜ ኢንቨስትመንት መለወጥ፤ ፈጠራ - ለጋራ ጥቅም የጤና ፈጠራን ማራመድ፤ አቅም - ሁሉንም የጤና ተደራሽነት እውን ለማድረግ ቀልጣፋ የመንግስት ሴክተር አቅምን ማሳደግ ናቸው። “ከሁለት ዓመት በፊት፣ መሪ የሴት ኢኮኖሚስቶችን እና የጤና ባለሙያዎችን የፓራዳይም (የአስተሳሰብ) ለውጥ እንዲያደርጉ ኃላፊነት ሰጥቻለሁ።
translations.reference: Value - valuing and measuring what matters through new economic metrics; Finance - how to finance health for all as a long-term investment, not a short-term cost; Innovation - how to advance health innovation for the common good; Capacity - how to strengthen dynamic public sector capacity to achieve health for all. “Two years ago, I asked a team of the world’s leading economists and public health experts – all women – to create a paradigm shift.
translations.hypothesis:
Value: Identifying and measuring the essential elements for a thriving economy.
Finance: Shifting the focus from short-term costs to long-term investments in healthcare accessibility.
Innovation: Fostering healthcare innovation for the common good.
Capacity: Strengthening the government's capacity to effectively implement healthcare accessibility initiatives.

"Two years ago, I tasked a group of leading female economists and healthcare professionals with driving a paradigm shift in these areas."

translations.source: የጤና አገልግሎትን ኢኮኖሚያዊ ዕድገትን ለማምጣት ብቻ የሚረዳ ነገር አድርገን ከመመልከት ይልቅ በአሁኑ ወቅት ኢኮኖሚያዊ እንቅስቃሴዎችን በማዋቀር ሁለንተናዊ የጤና አገልግሎቶችን ተደራሽነት ለማሳካት የሚያስችል ፍኖተ ካርታ አዘጋጅተናል።” ሲሉ ዶ/ር ቴድሮስ ተናግረዋል። የምክር ቤቱ ሊቀመንበር ፕሮፌሰር ማሪያና ማዙካቶ “ባለፉት ሁለት ዓመታት ውስጥ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት የጤና ኢኮኖሚክስ ምክር ቤቱ አዲስ ሃሳብ ለመቅረጽ ጥረት አድርጓል፤ ይህም የጤና ፋይናንስን ከወጪ ወደ ኢንቬስትመንት ለመቀየር ነው።” ብለዋል። "የፈጠራ ባለቤትነት ማሻሻያ፣ በመንግስትና በግሉ ዘርፍ ሽርክናዎች እና የበጀት ድልድልን ጨምሮ ጤና ለሁሉም እንዲደርስ የሚያረጋግጥ ኢኮኖሚን ለመፍጠር የሚያስፈልጉትን ለውጦችን መርምረናል። በመጨረሻው ዘገባችን ከገበያ እርማት ባለፈ ለሰዎች እና ለዓለማችን ደህንነት ቅድሚያ የሚሰጥ በጥንቃቄ እና በትብብር የሚቀርፅ አዲስ የኢኮኖሚ ፖሊሲ እንዲኖር እናበረታታለን።” ዛሬ ከሰባ ስድስተኛው የዓለም ጤና ጉባኤ ጎን ለጎን ይፋ የሆነው ዘገባ “ጤና ለሁሉም፤ አስፈላጊውን አገልግሎት ለማድረስ ኢኮኖሚን መለወጥ” (በድህረ ገጽ: https://www.who.int/groups/who-council-on-the) በሚል ርዕስ ከላይ በተጠቀሱት አራት ዋና ዋና ሃሳቦች ላይ የተመሰረተ አዲስ ማዕቀፍ ያቀርባል፣ ለእያንዳንዱም ዝርዝር ጥቆማዎች አሉት።
translations.reference: Now, instead of health for all being seen as the servant of economic growth, we have a roadmap for structuring economic activity in a way that will allow us to reach the goal of seeing all people with access to essential health services faster with better results,” said Dr Tedros. “Over the past two years, the WHO Council on the Economics of Health for All has worked to craft a new economic narrative – one that transforms financing for health from an expenditure to an investment,” said the Council’s Chair, Professor Mariana Mazzucato. “We have examined the changes needed – including to the structure of patents, public-private partnerships, and budgets – to design an economy that delivers Health for All. In our final report, we call for new economic policy that is not about market fixing but about proactively and collaboratively shaping markets that prioritize human and planetary health.” Launched today in conjunction with the Seventy-sixth World Health Assembly, the report, entitled Health for All: Transforming economies to deliver what matters (https://www.who.int/groups/who-council-on-the-economics-of-health-for-all), provides a new framework built on the above four pillars, with specific recommendations under each – drawing from the Council’s previous work.
translations.hypothesis: "Instead of viewing health services solely as a means to economic growth, we have developed a roadmap to make comprehensive health services accessible by shaping economic activities," said Dr. Tedros. The chair of the council, Professor Mariana Mazzucato, stated, "Over the past two years, the WHO's Council on Health Economics has strived to propose a new approach: to transform health financing from an expenditure to an investment." "We have examined the changes needed to create an economy that ensures health for all, including improvements in innovation, public-private partnerships, and budget allocation. In our final report, we encourage the development of a new economic policy that prioritizes the well-being of people and the planet, rather than simply focusing on market efficiency, and is carefully and collaboratively crafted." The report, released today alongside the seventy-sixth World Health Assembly, presents a new framework entitled "Health for All: Transforming the Economy to Deliver Essential Services" (available at: https://www.who.int/groups/who-council-on-the), based on the four key ideas mentioned above, with detailed recommendations for each.

translations.source: ጥቆማዎቹም፡ የኢኮኖሚያዊ እድገትን ብቻ ከማሳደድ እና ውጤቱን ሳናስብ አጠቃላይ የሃገር ውስጥ ምርትን ለማሳደግ ከመሞከር ይልቅ በእውነትም አስፈላጊ የሆኑት ነገሮችን መገምገም እና ማተኮር አለብን። ለሁሉም ህብረተሰብ የጤና ተደራሽነትን እውን ለማድረግ መንግስታት እሴቶችን እንደገና ማጤን እና በአዲስ መለኪያዎች በመመራት በማህበራዊ እና በዓለማችን ደህንነት ላይ የተመሰረት ኢኮኖሚን ማዋቀርና አቅጣጫ መቀየር አለባቸው። የጤና ወጪን እንደ የረጅም ጊዜ ኢንቨስትመንት በመመልከት የብሔራዊ እና ዓለም አቀፍ የጤና ፋይናንስ ሥርዓቶች መሠረታዊ ማሻሻያ ማድረግ አስፈላጊ ነው። ጤናን ለሁሉም ማድረስ ተጨማሪ የገንዘብ ድጋፍን ብቻ ሳይሆን ከፍተኛ ጥራት ያለው ፋይናንስንም ይጠይቃል፤ ፈጠራ የጋራ እውቀትን የሚፈልግና በጭራሽ የአንድ ኩባንያ ወይም የመንግስት ኤጀንሲ ውጤት አይደለም። ነገር ግን ፈጠራን ለጋራ ጥቅም ካላዋልነው ብዙ ሰዎች ከጥቅሞቹ መገለላቸው ይቀጥላል።
translations.reference: Key recommendations include: We need to value and measure the things that truly matter - human and planetary flourishing - rather than pursuing economic growth and GDP maximization regardless of the consequences. To achieve health for all, governments must rethink value and reshape and redirect the economy based on social and planetary well-being, guided by new metrics. A fundamental overhaul of national and international systems for financing health is needed, so that spending on health is treated as a long-term investment. Delivering Health for All will require both more money, and higher quality financing.Innovation requires collective intelligence—it is never the fruit of just one company or government agency. But unless innovation is governed for the common good, many people remain excluded from its benefits.
translations.hypothesis: The advice suggests that instead of solely focusing on increasing economic growth and overall GDP without considering the consequences, we need to carefully evaluate and prioritize what is truly important. Governments must reassess their values and shift towards building an economy based on social and global well-being, ensuring access to healthcare for all. This requires fundamental reforms to national and international health financing systems, viewing healthcare spending as a long-term investment. Achieving health for all requires not only increased financial support but also high-quality financing, innovation, and shared knowledge – something that cannot be solely the result of a single company or government agency. However, if innovation is not harnessed for the common good, many people will continue to be excluded from its benefits.

translations.source: ለጋራ ጥቅም ቅድሚያ የሚሰጥ አዲስ የጤና ፈጠራ ስነ-ምህዳር አስፈላጊ ነው። የኮቪድ-19 ወረርሽኝ የመንግስት አቅም አስፈላጊነት አጉልቶ አሳይቷል። ውጤታማ መንግስታት የግድ ትንንሾቹ አይደሉም፤ ነገር ግን በጥሩ ሁኔታ የተደራጁ እና በቂ ሀብት እና መሰረተ ልማት ያላቸው ናቸው። ጤናን ለሁሉም ለማዳረስ በመንግስት አቅም ላይ እንደገና መዋዕለ ንዋይ ማፍሰስ አስፈላጊ ነው። ሪፖርቱ በተጨማሪም እነዚህ ለውጦች እንዴት እውን እንዲሆኑ ማድረግ እንደሚቻል፤ ለምሳሌ የኢኮኖሚያዊ እሴትን፣ የጤና ፋይናንስን፣ ፈጠራን እና የህዝብ ሴክተርን አቅም ማሳደግ ያሉ ምክሮችን ይሰጣል።
translations.reference: A new end-to-end health innovation ecosystem that prioritises the common good is needed. As the COVID-19 pandemic has made clear, the quality and capacity of government matters. Effective governments are not the smallest, but those that are well-designed and properly resourced, both financially and in terms of their people and infrastructure. Re-investing in government capacity is crucial to delivering Health for All. The report also provides suggestions on what can be done in practice to implement the changes needed to reorient measures of economic value, the financing of health, innovation and building public sector capacity in the service of health for all.
translations.hypothesis: A new health innovation ecosystem that prioritizes the common good is essential. The COVID-19 pandemic has highlighted the importance of government capacity. Effective governments are not necessarily small; rather, they are well-organized and possess adequate resources and infrastructure. It is crucial to reinvest in government capacity to ensure that health reaches everyone. The report also provides recommendations on how to make these changes a reality, such as increasing economic value, strengthening health financing, fostering innovation, and enhancing the capacity of the public sector.

translations.source: በሪፖርቱ ውስጥ የተጠቀሱት አንዳንድ ምሳሌዎች የሚከተሉትን ያካትታሉ፤ በደቡብ አፍሪካ ያለው የmRNA (በሴሎች ውስጥ መልዕክትን አስተላላፊ ፕሮቲን) ቴክኖሎጂ ማዕከል፡- ፈጠራን፣ ፋይናንስን እና የአቅም ፍላጎቶችን በንቃት ለመፍታት ያለመ በእሴቶች የሚመራ ስርዓት፤ ለጋራ ጥቅም ለሚያገለግል የጤና-ኢንዱስትሪ የብራዚል ህዝብ የሚያፈሰው መዋእለ ንዋይ፤ በ ደቡቡ የዓለማችን አካባቢ ያሉ ለውጥን የሚያመቻቹ የክልል ልማት ባንኮች ከምሳሌዎቹ መካከል ናቸው። ”Wellbeing Economy Alliance” (ደህንነትን የሚያረጋግጥ የኢኮኖሚ ጥምረት) በርካታ መንግስታትን እና ከ 600 በላይ ሌሎች ድርጅቶችን የያዘ ቡድን ሲሆን ይህም የኢኮኖሚ ስርዓቶችን ለመለወጥ እና ህይወትን የተሻለ ለማድረግ የተመሰረተ ነው።
translations.reference: Among these, the report mentions several examples, including: The mRNA technology hub facility in South Africa: a values-driven system that tries to get the innovation, financing and capacity right ex-ante; Brazil’s public investment in a health-industrial complex that serves the common good; Regional development banks as enablers of change in the Global South; The Wellbeing Economy Alliance – an alliance of several governments and over 600 other organizations working together to transform economic systems in the service of life;
translations.hypothesis: The report highlights several examples, including: the mRNA (messenger RNA) technology center in South Africa, which is a value-driven system designed to actively address innovation, finance, and capacity needs; the investment made by the Brazilian health industry for the benefit of its population; and regional development banks in the Global South that are facilitating transformative change. The "Wellbeing Economy Alliance" is a group comprising numerous governments and over 600 other organizations, dedicated to transforming economic systems and improving lives.

translations.source: ብዙዎቹ ፀረ ተህዋሲያን (መድሃኒቶች) በሽታ የመቋቋም አቅማቸውን ለማጎልበት የሚወጡ ብሄራዊ የድርጊት መርሃ ግብሮች በቂ የገንዘብ ድጋፍ ስለሌላቸው የተለያዩ ዘርፎች የገንዘብ ድጋፍ እንዲያደርጉ ማድረግ አንዱ ቡድኑ የተቋቋመበት አላማ ነው። በዘገባው ውስጥ የተገለጹት ምክሮች ሀገራት ጤናን የሚገነዘቡበትን እና በጀት የሚያደርጉበትን እይታ የመቀየር አቅም አላቸው። የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ፖሊሲ አውጪዎች፣ ሲቪል ማህበረሰቦች እና የጤና እና ኢኮኖሚክስ ማህበረሰቦች አባላት እነዚህን ምክሮች ሙሉ በሙሉ እንዲያጤኑ እና አዲስ የኢኮኖሚ ፖሊሲዎችን እና አወቃቀሮችን በማዘጋጀት እንደ መመሪያ ተጠቅመው ጤና ለሁሉምን እውን እንዲያደርጉ ያሳስበናል።
translations.reference: Approaches to financing national action plans (NAPs) on antimicrobial resistance through multi sector joint budgeting, given that most NAPs remain unfunded. The recommendations included in the report could change the way countries view and finance health. WHO calls on policy-makers, civil society, and members of the health and economics communities to give full consideration to the recommendations and use them as a compass to develop new economic policies and structures that can move us along the road to making health for all a reality.
translations.hypothesis: Many national action plans aimed at strengthening the effectiveness of anti-infective drugs are underfunded. One of the goals of this initiative is to encourage various sectors to provide financial support. The recommendations outlined in the report have the potential to change how countries view and allocate resources for health. The World Health Organization urges policymakers, civil society organizations, and members of the health and economics communities to carefully consider these recommendations and use them as a guide in developing new economic policies and strategies to make health for all a reality.

translations.source: ታዋቂዎቹ አርቲስቶች ሬኔ ፍሌሚንግ እና ፕሪቲ ዬንዴ የአለም ጤና ድርጅት የበጎ ፈቃድ አምባሳደሮች ሆነው ተሹመዋል። የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት በዓለም አቀፍ ደረጃ ታዋቂ የሆኑትን ሬኔ ፍሌሚንግ እና ፕሪቲ ዬንዴን የኪነጥበብ እና ጤና የበጎ ፈቃድ አምባሳደሮች አድርጎ በመሾሙ የተሰማውን ደስታ ገልጿል። ይህ ትልቅ ዜና የተነገረው እ.ኤ.አ. በግንቦት 22 ቀን 2023 በተካሄደው የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት 75ኛ ዓመት መታሰቢያ በዓል አካል በሆነው በሰባ ስድስተኛው የዓለም ጤና ጉባኤ ወቅት ነው። በዘመናችን በጣም ታዋቂ ድምጻዊ የሆኑት ሬኔ ፍሌሚንግ እና በአስደናቂ ድምጾና ትርኢትዋ የምትታወቀው ፕሪቲ ዬንዴ ለኪነጥበብ እንዲሁም በተለያዩ የፈጠራ ዘዴዎች ለተሻሻለ የጤና ውጤት ባደረጉት አስተዋጾ ምክኒያት ተመርጠዋል። እ.ኤ.አ. ሚያዚያ 8 2023 ፍሌሚንግ እና ዬንዴ “የፈውስ ኪነጥበብ” በሚል ርዕስ በዓለም ጤና ድርጅት 75ኛ ዓመት የምስረታ በዓል ላይ በጄኔቫ ስዊዘርላንድ በተዘጋጀ ልዩ ኮንሰርት ዝግጅታቸውን አቅርበዋል።
translations.reference: Renowned artists Renée Fleming and Pretty Yende appointed as WHO Goodwill Ambassadors for Arts and Health WHO is delighted to announce the appointment of internationally acclaimed artists Renée Fleming and Pretty Yende as Goodwill Ambassadors for Arts and Health. This prestigious announcement was made as part of WHO's 75th Anniversary commemoration during the Seventy-sixth World Health Assembly on 22 May 2023. Renée Fleming, one of the most celebrated singers of our time, and Pretty Yende, an extraordinary soprano known for her dazzling performances, have been selected for their artistic achievement and advocacy for improved health outcomes through creative means. On 8 April 2023, Fleming and Yende performed for a special concert titled Healing Arts, dedicated to WHO's 75th anniversary in Geneva, Switzerland.
translations.hypothesis: The renowned artists, Renée Fleming and Pretty Yende, have been appointed as Goodwill Ambassadors for the World Health Organization (WHO). The WHO expressed its delight at appointing Renée Fleming and Pretty Yende, both internationally recognized figures, as Goodwill Ambassadors for the arts and health. This announcement was made during the 76th World Health Assembly, which took place on May 22, 2023, as part of the WHO's 75th anniversary celebrations. Renée Fleming, one of the most celebrated singers of our time, and Pretty Yende, known for her stunning voice and performances, were selected for their contributions to the arts and for their advocacy for improved health outcomes through various creative initiatives. On April 8, 2023, Fleming and Yende presented a special concert titled "The Healing Power of Art" at the WHO's 75th anniversary celebration in Geneva, Switzerland.

translations.source: የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ዋና ዳይሬክተር ዶ/ር ቴዎድሮስ አድሃኖም ገብረእየሱስ "የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ሬኔ ፍሌሚንግ እና ፕሪቲ ዬንዴ የኪነጥበብ እና ጤና በጎ ፈቃድ አምባሳደሮች በመሆን በመቀላቀላቸው ታላቅ ክብር ይሰማናል" ብለዋል። ወደር የለሽ ተሰጥኦአቸው፣ ለኪነጥበብ ያላቸው ቁርጠኝነት እና ጤናን እና ደህንነትን ለማራመድ ያላቸው ፍላጎት ለዚህ አስፈላጊ ዓላማ ተስማሚ አምባሳደሮች ያደርጋቸዋል። የእነርሱ ተሳትፎ ግለሰቦች፣ ማህበረሰቡ እና ፖሊሲ አውጪዎች ኪነ-ጥበብ ለተሻለ ጤና ያለውን ተጽዕኖ እንዲገነዘቡ ያነሳሳል። ፍሌሚንግ በአዲሱ ሚናዋ ብዙ ባለሙያዎችን ታመጣለች። በአለም አቀፍ ደረጃ አድናቂዎቿን በብሩህ ድምጿ እና በአስደናቂ የስነጥበብ ስራዋ አስደንቃለች።
translations.reference: Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, WHO Director-General, said, "WHO is honoured to have Renée Fleming and Pretty Yende join as Goodwill Ambassadors for Arts and Health. Their unparalleled talent, dedication to the arts, and commitment to advancing health and well-being make them ideal ambassadors for this important cause. Their involvement will inspire individuals, communities and policy-makers to recognize and harness the transformative power of the arts in the pursuit of better health for all. "Fleming brings a wealth of expertise to her new role. She has captivated audiences worldwide with her luminous soprano voice and compelling artistry.
translations.hypothesis: The Director-General of the World Health Organization, Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, stated, "We are honored to welcome René Fleming and Pretty Yende as Goodwill Ambassadors for the World Health Organization, representing the arts and health." Their exceptional talent, dedication to the arts, and commitment to promoting health and well-being make them ideal ambassadors for this important cause. Their involvement will help raise awareness among individuals, communities, and policymakers about the impact of the arts on health. Fleming, in her new role, will bring her extensive experience and influence to the organization. She has captivated audiences worldwide with her powerful voice and stunning artistic performances.

translations.source: ፍሌሚንግ ከአስደናቂው የድምጽ ተሰጥኦዋ ባሻገር ኪነጥበብ እና ጤና ያላቸው ግንኙነት ላይ ለምርምር ብርቱ ጠበቃ ነበረች። በዓለም ዙሪያ ከሳይንቲስቶች እና ባለሙያዎች ጋር ያቀረበቻቸው ገለጻዎች፣ በዋና ዋና ተነሳሽነቶች ውስጥ ያላት የመሪነት ሚና እንዲሁም ከዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ጋር የነበራት ግንኙነት የጥበብን የመለወጥ ሃይል ለመጠቀም ያላትን ቁርጠኝነት ያሳያል። ፍሌሚንግ “ቀዳሚው የሆነው የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ኪነ-ጥበብ በጤና ላይ ያለውን ከፍተኛ ተፅእኖ በመገንዘቡ በጣም ተደስቻለሁ። በየጊዜው በአዕምሮ ጥናት እና ምርምር ላይ የሚታዩ እድገቶች የኪነጥበብ ሕክምናዎች፣ ልምዶች እና ትምህርቶች ያላቸውን ጥቅም የበለጠ ያሳያሉ፣ ይህም በሰው ልጅ የህይወት ዘመን ሁሉ ለደህንነቱ ከፍተኛ ጥቅም አለው። ሆኖም የዚህን ስራ አስደናቂ አቅም መገንዘብ የጀመርነው ገና አሁን ነው ስለዚህም እንደ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት የበጎ ፈቃድ አምባሳደርነቴ ግንዛቤን እና ተደራሽነትን ለማስፋት እሰራለሁ።” ብላለች።
translations.reference: Beyond her remarkable vocal talent, Fleming has been a fervent advocate for research at the intersection of the arts and health. Her presentations worldwide with scientists and practitioners, her leading role in major initiatives, and her association with WHO demonstrate her commitment to harnessing the transformative power of the arts to better society. "I am elated that the preeminent global health agency is recognizing the powerful impact of the arts in health, and I am deeply honoured to be entrusted to share this message," said Fleming. “Every day, advances in neuroscience and clinical research reveal more about the benefits of arts therapies, experiences, and education, with implications for well-being throughout the human life span. However, we have only just begun to realize the amazing potential of this work, and I look forward to expanding awareness and access as a WHO Goodwill Ambassador.
translations.hypothesis:
Fleming, beyond her remarkable vocal talent, was a passionate advocate for the connection between art, health, and well-being. Her presentations to scientists and experts around the world, her leadership role in key initiatives, and her collaboration with the World Health Organization demonstrate her commitment to harnessing the transformative power of art.

Fleming stated, "I am thrilled that the World Health Organization is recognizing the profound impact of art on health. The advancements we are seeing in neuroscience and research increasingly demonstrate the benefits of art therapies, practices, and education, which can significantly improve human well-being throughout life. However, we are only beginning to understand the full potential of this work, and that is why, as a Goodwill Ambassador for the World Health Organization, I am dedicated to expanding awareness and access."

translations.source: ቆንጅየዋ ዬንዴ በአስደናቂ ድምጿ እና በሚያስደንቅ ትርኢቷ ተመልካቾችን አስገርማለች። የሙዚቃን ኃይል በጥልቀት የተረዳች ድምጻዊ እንደመሆኗ ችሎታዋን በመጠቀም ህብረተሰቡን ለማገልገል ያላትን ቁርጠኝነት አሳይታለች። ዬንዴ የሙዚቃን የመፈውስ አቅም ለማካፈል ያላትን ፍቅር እና ለማህበራዊ ጉዳዮች ያላትን ቁርጠኝነት ከዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ኪነጥበብ ለጤና አጀንዳ ጋር በትክክል ይስማማል። " የአለም ጤና ድርጅት የበጎ ፈቃድ አምባሳደር ሆኜ በመሾሜ በጣም ደስተኛ ነኝ።" ብላለች። “ኪነ-ጥበብ አካላዊ፣ አእምሯዊ እና ማህበራዊ ደህንነታችንን ለማሻሻል መሰረታዊ ነገር እንደመሆኑ ለቀጣይ የምርምር ስራዎች መሰረትን ለማስቀመጥ እንደረዳሁ ተስፋ አደርጋለሁ።
translations.reference: "Pretty Yende has mesmerized audiences with her exquisite voice and stunning performances.
