The HRP remains a vital tool within the United Nations system for conducting research on human reproduction, and continues to be a leading force in global sexual and reproductive health.

translations.source: ሰው ሀይል ምደባ እቅድ(HRP) እና አጋሮቹ እ.ኤ.አ. በ1994 በተካሄደው የህብረተሰብ እና ልማት ስብሰባ ወቅት የግለሰቦችን በተለይም የሴቶች እና የልጃገረዶች መብት፣ የዘላቂ ልማት መሰረት፣ ለማድረግ የገባውን ቃል ለማየት እንድንቃረብ አስችለዋል። ለሰው ሀይል ምደባ እቅድ(HRP) ዋና ፀሀፊዎች፣ አጋሮቹ፣ ተባባሪ የገንዘብ ለጋሽ አጋሮች እና የአለም አቀፍ የትብብር ማዕከላት እና ባለሙያዎች በሴቶች፣ ወንዶች፣ ህጻናት፣ ጎረምሶች፣ ወጣቶች እና የ ሰው ሀይል ምደባ እቅድ(HRP) ለማገልገል ቁርጠኛ የሆነባቸው የተለያዩ ማህበረሰቦች ላይ ለውጥ ለማምጣት ለተደረገው ጥረት መሳካት የበኩላቸውን አስተዋጽኦ ለማበርከት ላደረጉት እገዛ አድናቆት እና ምስጋና መግለጽ እንፈልጋለን። የፖሊሲ እና የማስተባበሪያ ኮሚቴ (PCC) አባላትን በመወከል፣ እ.ኤ.አ የ2030 አጀንዳ ስኬት የሚመሰረትበትን የሰው ሀይል ምደባ እቅድ(HRP) ግኝቶችን ለመደገፍ ከእርስዎ ጋር ያለንን አጋርነት እናረጋግጥ።
translations.reference: HRP and its partners have brought us closer to the promise of the 1994 International Conference on Population and Development and its call to put the rights of individuals, especially women and girls, at the heart of sustainable development. We want to express our appreciation and gratitude to the HRP secretariat, its partners, co-sponsors and the global network of collaborating centres and experts helping to ensure its efforts contribute to making a difference to the women, men, children, adolescents and various communities HRP is committed to serve. On behalf of PCC members, allow us to reaffirm our partnership with you to support the achievements of HRP, upon which the success of the 2030 Agenda depends.
translations.hypothesis: The Human Resources Planning (HRP) initiative and its partners were instrumental in bringing us closer to realizing the commitments made at the International Conference on Population and Development in 1994, particularly regarding the rights of individuals, especially women and girls, and the foundation for sustainable development. We would like to express our admiration and gratitude to the key authors of the Human Resources Planning (HRP), its partners, associated financial partners, and international collaborative centers and experts, for their contributions to the success of efforts aimed at bringing about positive change in the lives of women, men, children, adolescents, youth, and various communities dedicated to serving the goals of Human Resources Planning (HRP). On behalf of the Policy and Coordination Committee (PCC) members, we reaffirm our partnership with you in supporting the findings of the Human Resources Planning (HRP), which are essential for achieving the 2030 Agenda.

translations.source: የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት የወረርሽኝን ቅደመ ዝግጁነት ለማሻሻል አዲስ የድርጊት የመጀመሪያ እርምጃን ጀመረ አገሮች ለወደፊት ወረርሽኞች በተሻለ ሁኔታ እንዲዘጋጁ ለመርዳት፣ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት(WHO) እንደ ጉንፋን(influenza) ወይም ኮሮናቫይረስ ላሉ የመተንፈሻ አካላት በሽታን አምጪ ተዋህስያን ምላሽ ለመስጠት የተቀናጀ ዕቅድ ላይ መመሪያ የሚሰጥ አዲስ የድርጊት የመጀመሪያ እርምጃን ዛሬ ጀምሯል። አዲሱ የዝግጁነት እና የተቋቋሚነት ለአደጋ ስጋት የድርጊት የመጀመሪያ እርምጃ (PRET)፣ በኮቪድ-19 ወረርሽኝ እና በሌሎች የቅርብ ጊዜ የህዝብ ጤና ድንገተኛ አደጋዎች፣ ለጋራ ትምህርት እና ለጋራ እርምጃዎች ተብሎ የተፈጠሩ የቅርብ ጊዜ መሳሪያዎችን እና አቀራረቦችን ያካትታል። በድርጊት የመጀመሪያ እርምጃው ውስጥ፣ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት በበሽታ አምጪ ተህዋሲያን ቡድኖች መካከል ብዙ አቅሞች እና ባህሪያት የተለመዱ በመሆናቸው፣ ሀገራትን የወረርሽኝን እቅድ ለማውጣት ከሰው ሰው የመተላለፊያ ዘዴን የመጠቀም አቀራረብን ይጠቀማል። የዝግጁነት እና የተቋቋሚነት ለአደጋ ስጋት የድርጊት የመጀመሪያ እርምጃ (PRET)፣ በአለም ጤና ጉባኤ ውሳኔዎች ላይ እንደተገለጸው፣ የተቀናጀ ዝግጁነት እና ምላሽን ለማበረታታት እና ለማጠናከር የሙያዊ መመሪያ እና ድጋፍ ጥሪን ይመልሳል።
translations.reference: WHO launches new initiative to improve pandemic preparedness To help countries better prepare for future pandemics, WHO launched a new initiative today that provides guidance on integrated planning for responding to any respiratory pathogen such as influenza or coronaviruses. The new Preparedness and Resilience for Emerging Threats Initiative, or PRET, incorporates the latest tools and approaches for shared learning and collective action established during the COVID-19 pandemic and other recent public health emergencies. Through the initiative, WHO will use a mode of transmission approach to guide countries in pandemic planning, given that many capacities and capabilities are common among groups of pathogens. PRET answers the call for technical guidance and support for promoting and strengthening integrated preparedness and response, as outlined in World Health Assembly resolutions.
translations.hypothesis: The World Health Organization (WHO) has launched a new, action-oriented framework to improve pandemic preparedness. This framework aims to help countries better prepare for future pandemics by providing guidance on a comprehensive plan to respond to respiratory disease outbreaks, such as influenza or coronaviruses. The new Pandemic Preparedness and Response Action Framework (PRAF) incorporates lessons learned from the COVID-19 pandemic and other recent public health emergencies, as well as the latest tools and approaches for collaborative action. Within the framework, the WHO encourages countries to use a "One Health" approach to pandemic planning, recognizing that many infectious diseases share common characteristics and transmission pathways. The Pandemic Preparedness and Response Action Framework (PRAF), as outlined in the World Health Assembly resolutions, calls for professional guidance and support to promote and strengthen integrated preparedness and response.

translations.source: የኮቪድ-19 ወረርሽኝ እና ሌሎች የጤና ድንገተኛ አደጋዎች እንዳሳዩት ፣ የተዘጋጁ የቅድመ ዝግጅት እቅዶችን በእጃቸው መያዝ እና ከሌሎች እንደ ግብርና ካሉ መስኮች ጋር የተሻለ ቅንጅት እና ትብብር ማድረግን ጨምሮ፣ ሀገራት ተላላፊ በሽታዎችን ለመከላከል ለተግባር የተዘጋጁ መሆን አለባቸው። "የዝግጅት፣ የመከላከል እና ምላሽ ተግባራት የጤና መስኩ ጠቅላይ ሀላፊነት ብቻ መሆን የለባቸውም" ሲሉ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ዋና ዳይሬክተር ዶ/ር ቴዎድሮስ አድሃኖም ገብረእየሱስ ተናግረዋል። “የጤና ድንገተኛ አደጋዎች በተለያዩ ዘርፎች ላይ ተጽእኖ እንደሚያሳድሩ ሁሉ የእኛም የዝግጁነት እና የምላሽ ጥረታችን በተለያዩ ዘርፎችን፣ የትምህርት ዓይነቶችን እና በሽታ አምጪ ተህዋስያንን መካከል መዘርጋት አለባቸው። የማህበረሰብ ተሳትፎ እና ፍትሃዊነት የጥረታችን ማዕከል መሆናቸው፣ በተለይም ለተገለሉ እና ለአደጋ በተጋለጡ ህዝቦች ላይ በጣም አስፈላጊ ነው። የዝግጁነት እና የተቋቋሚነት ለአደጋ ስጋት የድርጊት የመጀመሪያ እርምጃ (PRET) በልዩ በሽታዎች ላይ ከማተኮር ይልቅ ከሰው ሰው የመተላለፊያ ዘዴን የመጠቀም አቀራረብን በመከተል፣ የወረርሽኝ ዝግጁነት አቀራረብ ዝግመተ ለውጥ ማሳያ ነው።
translations.reference: The COVID-19 pandemic and other health emergencies have shown that countries need to be operationally ready to respond to infectious disease threats, with tailored preparedness plans in hand and better coordination and collaboration with other sectors such as agriculture. “Preparedness, prevention, and response activities must not be the province of the health sector alone,” said Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, WHO Director-General. “Just as health emergencies have impacts across many sectors, so must our preparedness and response efforts span sectors, disciplines and pathogens. It is critical, too, that community engagement and equity are the centre of our efforts, especially for those populations that are marginalized and most at risk.” PRET is an evolution in WHO’s approach to pandemic preparedness through the application of a mode of transmission lens, rather than a focus on specific diseases.
translations.hypothesis: As the COVID-19 pandemic and other health emergencies have demonstrated, countries must be prepared to take practical steps to prevent infectious diseases, including having well-defined preparedness plans and fostering better coordination and collaboration with other sectors, such as agriculture. "Preparedness, prevention, and response efforts should not be the sole responsibility of the health sector," said Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Director-General of the World Health Organization. "Just as health emergencies impact various sectors, our preparedness and response efforts must also be integrated across different sectors, disciplines, and disease areas. Community engagement and equity must be at the heart of our efforts, particularly for vulnerable and marginalized populations." The Pandemic Preparedness and Response Technical Assistance (PRET) initiative represents a paradigm shift in pandemic preparedness, moving away from a focus on specific diseases and embracing an approach that leverages the human-to-human transmission pathway.

translations.source: የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት እንደአስፈላጊነቱ በተወሰኑ በሽታዎች ላይ መመሪያዎችን ማዘጋጀቱን እና ማሰራጨቱን ይቀጥላል። የዝግጁነት እና የተቋቋሚነት ለአደጋ ስጋት የድርጊት የመጀመሪያ እርምጃ (PRET) ይፋዊ ጅማሬ በአባል ሀገራት፣ አጋሮች እና የአለም ጤና ድርጅት ፅህፈት ቤት ከተወከሉ ከ120 በላይ ግለሰቦች ጋር በተካሄደው የመጨረሻ ቀን ስብሰባ ላይ በመካሄድ ላይ ነው፣ የዝግጁነት እና የተቋቋሚነት ለአደጋ ስጋት የድርጊት የመጀመሪያ እርምጃ (PRET) ወደፊት የሚወስደውን መንገድ ለመወሰን፣ የመተንፈሻ አካላት በሽታ አምጪ ተህዋስያንን የወረርሽኝ ዝግጁነት ዓለም አቀፍ የትግበራ ፍኖተ ካርታ ማዘጋጀትን ጨምሮ ይወሰናል። የ የዝግጁነት እና የተቋቋሚነት ለአደጋ ስጋት የድርጊት የመጀመሪያ እርምጃ (PRET) የመጀመሪያ የጥናት ርዕስ ጉንፋንን፣ ኮሮና ቫይረስን፣እና የመተንፈሻ ሲንሲቲያል ቫይረስን ጨምሮ፣ የመተንፈሻ አካል በሽታ አምጪ ተህዋስያንን ላይ ያተኩራል። እየቀጠለ ያለውን የ ኮቪድ-19 ወርሽን እና የአቪያን ጉንፋን(influenza) ያስከትላል ተብሎ የሚጠበቀውን ጉዳት በግምት ውስጥ በማስገባት ፣ ይህ ሞጁል አገሮች የተግባር እና የክምችት አቅም መኖራቸውን ለማረጋገጥ፣ የአተነፋፈስ ወረርሽኝ እቅዳቸውን በከፍተኛ ሁኔታ እንዲገመግሙ፣ እንዲሞክሩ እና እንዲያሻሽሉ ያስችላቸዋል። በዚህ የድርጊት የመጀመሪያ እርምጃ የሚነሱትን፣ እንደ አርቦቫይረስ የመሳሰሉ በሽታ አምጪ ተህዋሲያን ቡድን የመለየት ሂደትም በመካሄድ ላይ ነው።
translations.reference: WHO will continue to develop and disseminate guidance on specific diseases as needed. The launch of the initiative is taking place on the last day of meetings with over 120 individuals representing Member States, partners, and the WHO secretariat, to define the path forward for PRET, including the development of a global implementation roadmap for respiratory pathogen pandemic preparedness. The PRET Initiative’s first module focuses on respiratory pathogens, including influenza, coronaviruses, and respiratory syncytial virus. Given the ongoing COVID-19 pandemic and the possible threat of avian influenza, this module will enable countries to critically review, test, and update their respiratory pandemic planning efforts to ensure they have the functional capacities and capabilities in place. A process is underway to identify the next group of pathogens, such as arboviruses, to be addressed under this initiative.
translations.hypothesis: The World Health Organization (WHO) continues to develop and disseminate guidelines on specific diseases as needed. The "Preparedness and Response to Emerging Infectious Disease Threats: A Framework for Action" (PRET) was finalized at a concluding meeting held with over 120 individuals representing member states, partners, and the WHO Secretariat. The meeting determined the future direction of the PRET, including the development of a global roadmap for preparedness against respiratory disease pathogens. The initial focus of the Preparedness and Response to Emerging Infectious Disease Threats (PRET) framework is on respiratory pathogens, including influenza, coronavirus, and respiratory syncytial virus. Taking into account the ongoing COVID-19 pandemic and the potential impact of avian influenza, this module aims to enable countries to rigorously assess, test, and improve their respiratory disease preparedness plans to ensure they have the capacity and resources to respond effectively. The framework also includes a process for identifying emerging pathogens, such as arenaviruses.

translations.source: ይህንንም ተከትሎ ለጤና ድንገተኛ አደጋ ዝግጁነት፣ ምላሽ እና መቋቋም (HEPR) ዓለም አቀፋዊ መዋቅራዊ ንድፍን ለማጠናከር ተለይተው ከተቀመጡ ቅድሚያ የሚሰጣቸውን ከአሥሩ የውሳኔ እቅድ ጉዳዮች ይቀርባሉ። የየዝግጁነት እና የተቋቋሚነት ለአደጋ ስጋት የድርጊት የመጀመሪያ እርምጃ (PRET) አዲስ ወረርሽኞችን ለመከላከል አዲስ ዘመንን ያመጣል እናም የሁሉንም አባል ሀገራት የጤና ድንገተኛ ዝግጁነት፣ የመከላከል እና የምላሽ አቅሞችን እና ብቃቶችን ለመደገፍ የሚያደርገውን የአለም ጤና ድርጅት ዋና ተግባራት ዝግመተ ለውጥን ይወክላል። ይህ ተነሳሽነት በአሁኑ ጊዜ በአለም ጤና ድርጅት አባል ሀገራት እየተደራደረ ያለውን የወረርሽኝ ድንጋጌዎችን ራዕዮች እና አላማዎችን ተግባራዊ ለማድረግ ያስችላል። በስብሰባ ተሳታፊዎች መካከል የሚገኙ ግብአቶችን እና ውይይቶችን ተከትሎ፣ የአለም ጤና ድርጅት ባለድርሻ አካላትን የመተንፈሻ አካላት በሽታ አምጪ ተዋህስያን ወረርሺኝ መከላከልን ለማጎልበት ወደዚህ አለም አቀፍ ጥሪ አቅርቧል። የድርጊት ጥሪ
translations.reference: This will follow priorities identified through the ten proposals to strengthen the global architecture for health emergency preparedness, response, and resilience (HEPR). The PRET Initiative ushers in a new era for pandemic preparedness and represents an evolution of WHO’s core activities to support all Member States in strengthening health emergency preparedness, prevention, and response capacities and capabilities. The initiative can also serve to operationalize the objectives and provisions of the Pandemic Accord, which is currently being negotiated by WHO Member States. Following inputs and discussions among meeting participants, WHO invites stakeholders to this global call to action to advance respiratory pathogen pandemic preparedness. Call to Action
translations.hypothesis: Following this, the ten priority areas for decision-making, specifically focused on strengthening the global framework for health emergency preparedness, response, and resilience (HEPR), are outlined. The "Preparedness and Resilience for Emerging Threats" (PRET) initiative marks a new era in preventing new outbreaks and represents a significant shift in the World Health Organization's (WHO) core functions, aimed at supporting all member states in strengthening their health emergency preparedness, prevention, and response capacities. This initiative will enable WHO member states to implement the vision and objectives of the International Health Regulations. Following contributions and discussions among participants at the meeting, the WHO has issued this global call to action to enhance the prevention of respiratory pathogen outbreaks. Call to action.

translations.source: የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት፣ አባል ሀገራት እና አጋሮች በጋራ ቅድሚያ የሚሰጣቸውን ተግባራት በመለየት ተግባራዊ በማድረግ ሀገራዊ፣ ክልላዊ እና አለም አቀፋዊ ወረርሽኞች እና ታዳጊ ተላላፊ በሽታ ስጋቶችን ዝግጁነት ለማጠናከር እየሰሩ ነው። በወረርሽኝ መከላከል እና ምላሽ ላይ በጣም የተለመደ የሆነውን የሽብር እና የቸልተኝነት ዑደት ለማስወገድ የማህበረሰቡ እና የሁሉም መስኮች ሚና እና የተቀናጀ እና ቀጣይነት ያለው መነሳሳት እንደሚያስፈልግ እንገነዘባለን። ከቀደምት አለምአቀፋዊ ወረርሽኞች እና ክልላዊ ወረርሽኞች በተለይም በመተንፈሻ አካላት በሽታ አምጪ ተህዋሲያን ምክንያት የተወሰዱ ትምህርቶችን እናስታውሳለን። ወረርሽኞች መከላከል እና የጤና እንክብካቤ ለሁሉም ሰዎች እንዳይደርስ በመገኘት፣ እነዚህ በሽታ አምጪ ተህዋሲያን የበሽታ እና የሞት አደጋን የማስከተል፣ የጤና ስርአቶችን የማጨናነቅ፣ የአለም ኢኮኖሚን የማዛባት እና ኢፍትሃዊነትን የሚያባብሱ ትልቅ አለም አቀፍ ስጋትን የማስከተል አቅም አላቸው እና ለወደፊትም ይኖራቸዋል። ይህ የተግባር ጥሪ በአለም አቀፍ ደረጃ ለወረርሽኞች እና ለሚከሰቱ ስጋቶች ዝግጁነትን ለማፋጠን ነው።
translations.reference: WHO, Member States, and partners are working together to identify and implement priority actions to strengthen national, regional, and global preparedness for pandemics and emerging infectious disease threats. We recognize the role of communities and all sectors, and the need for harmonized and sustained momentum to end the cycle of panic and neglect that is all too common in pandemic preparedness and response. We recall lessons from previous epidemics and pandemics, especially those caused by respiratory pathogens. These pathogens have and will continue to pose a significant global threat with the potential to cause tremendous morbidity and mortality, overwhelm health systems, de-stabilize the global economy, and exacerbate inequities, which exist in the access to both the tools to prevent pandemics and health care for all people. This is a Call to Action to accelerate preparedness for pandemics and emerging threats globally.
translations.hypothesis: The World Health Organization, along with member states and partners, is working to strengthen national, regional, and global preparedness for outbreaks and emerging infectious disease threats by identifying and implementing priority actions. We recognize the need for a coordinated and sustained effort, involving the community and all sectors, to break the cycle of panic and neglect that often characterizes responses to outbreaks. We remember the lessons learned from past global and regional outbreaks, particularly those caused by respiratory pathogens. These pathogens have the potential to cause significant global risks, leading to increased morbidity and mortality, straining health systems, disrupting the global economy, and exacerbating inequalities. This call to action aims to accelerate global preparedness for outbreaks and emerging threats.

translations.source: ውጤታማ ዝግጁነት በጠንካራ እቅድ እና በተቀናጀ ተግባር ላይ የተመሰረተ ነው። በሚከተሉት ተግባራት ላይ የምናሳየው የጋራ ቁርጠኝነት እ.ኤ.አ በታህሳስ 2025 መሻሻልን በክትትል ማዕቀፉ (ለአደጋ ስጋት ዝግጁነት እና ተቋቋሚነት (PRET) እንደተገለጸው እንድናይ ያስችላል፡- ቅድሚያ የሚሰጣቸውን ተግባራት ተከዋኝነትን የሚያረጋግጡ እና ካለፉት ክስተቶች የተማሩትን ያገናዘቡ የዘመኑ የዝግጁነት እቅዶችን ማውጣት ። በመተንፈሻ አካላት በሽታ አምጪ ተህዋሲያን ሊያስከትሉ የሚችሉትን አደጋ በመገንዘብ፣ በየዝግጁነት እና የተቋቋሚነት ለአደጋ ስጋት የድርጊት የመጀመሪያ እርምጃ (PRET) ሞጁል #1 ውስጥ በተገለጹት ጭብጦች ላይ በመመርኮዝ የመተንፈሻ አካልን በሽታ አምጪ ወረርሽኝ ለመከላከል ማቀድ፡ የመተንፈሻ በሽታ አምጪ ተህዋስያንን ወረርሽኝን ለመከላከል ማቀድ ቅድሚያ የሚሰጠው ጉዳይ ነው።
translations.reference: Effective preparedness relies on robust planning and coordinated action. A collective commitment on the following actions will see progress achieved by December 2025 as will be described in the (Preparedness and Resilience for Emerging Threats (PRET) monitoring framework: Update preparedness plans that affirm priority actions and that have considered learnings from past events. Recognizing the risk posed by respiratory pathogens, planning for a respiratory pathogen pandemic based on the themes identified in the PRET Module #1: Planning for Respiratory Pathogen Pandemics is a priority
translations.hypothesis:
Effective preparedness is based on a strong plan and coordinated action. The following activities demonstrate our commitment and will allow us to track progress towards improvement by December 2025, as defined by the Preparedness, Response, and Resilience to Hazards (PRET) framework:

*   Developing up-to-date preparedness plans that ensure resilience and incorporate lessons learned from past events.
*   Recognizing the potential risks posed by respiratory pathogens, we will prioritize planning to prevent and mitigate respiratory disease outbreaks, focusing on the topics outlined in PRET Action Module #1. Preventing respiratory disease outbreaks is a key priority.

translations.source: ስልታዊ ቅንጅት እና ትብብር በማድረግ በወረርሽኝ ዝግጁነት እቅድ ውስጥ በባለድርሻ አካላት መካከል ያለውን ግንኙነት ማሳደግ። ይህም ፍትሃዊ ስርዓቶችን መገንባትን; የጋራ ልምምድ ማካሄድ; እና ስለ ጥሩ ልምዶች፣ ተግዳሮቶች እና እድሎች መረጃን መለዋወጥን ይጨምራል። ያለፉትን በአለምአቀፋዊ ወረርሽኞች እና ክልላዊ ወረርሽኞች የታዩ ክፍተቶችን በመቅረፍ ቀጣይነት ያለው ኢንቨስትመንቶችን፣ የገንዘብ ድጋፍን እና የወረርሽኙን ቅድመ ዝግጅት መከታተል። የኮቪድ-19 ወረርሽኝ ምላሽ በፖለቲካ አቋም ቁርጠኝነት፣ በማህበረሰብ ተሳትፎ እና በገንዘብ ምን ሊገኝ እንደሚችል አሳይቷል።
translations.reference: Increase connectivity among stakeholders in pandemic preparedness planning through systematic coordination and cooperation. This includes building equitable systems; conducting joint exercises; and sharing information on good practices, challenges, and opportunities. Dedicate sustained investments, financing and monitoring of pandemic preparedness with a particular focus on addressing the gaps identified during past pandemics and epidemics. The COVID-19 pandemic response has demonstrated what can be achieved with political commitment, community engagement and funding.
translations.hypothesis: By fostering strategic partnerships and collaboration, we can strengthen the connections between stakeholders in pandemic preparedness planning. This includes building equitable systems, conducting joint exercises, and sharing information about best practices, challenges, and opportunities. Continuous investment, financial support, and monitoring of pandemic preparedness are essential to address gaps identified from past global and regional outbreaks. The COVID-19 response has demonstrated the importance of political commitment, community engagement, and adequate funding in effectively addressing a pandemic.

translations.source: የዚህ ሥራ ዋና ነገር ለቀጣዩ ወረርሽኝ በጋራ ለመዘጋጀት እኩልነትን ማረጋገጥ ነው። በዚህ የድርጊት ጥሪ ውስጥ የተገለፀውን እድገት ለማሳየት የህብረተሰቡ ሁሉ እርምጃ ያስፈልጋል። ትግበራው ስለዚህም፣ የማህበረሰቦችን የመቋቋም አቅም ማጠናከር አለበት; በመደበኛ ስርዓቶች ላይ መግባት, መቆየት እና መገንባት አለበት; እና ለአደጋ ጊዜ ዝግጁነት እና ምላሽ ሰፊ አቅሞችን መጠቀም አለበት። ይህ የድርጊት ጥሪ የተለቀቀው በጄኔቫ፣ ስዊዘርላንድ ውስጥ እ.ኤ.አ በ24-26 ሚያዝያ 2023 በተካሄደው አለምአቀፋዊ የሆነ፣ ለወደፊት ለሚመጡት የመተንፈሻ አካላት በሽታ አምጪ ወረርሽኞች ዝግጁነት እና ተቋቋሚነት (PRET) ላይ በተደረገ ስብሰባ ላይ ነው።
translations.reference: At the heart of this work is to ensure equity to be ready for the next pandemic together. Whole-of-society action is needed to make the progress outlined in this Call to Action. Implementation should therefore strengthen the resilience of communities; maintain, sustain, and build on routine systems; and leverage broader capacities for emergency preparedness and response. This Call to Action was released at the Preparedness and Resilience for Emerging Threats (PRET): Global Meeting for Future Respiratory Pathogen Pandemics held on 24-26 April 2023 in Geneva, Switzerland.
translations.hypothesis: The primary objective of this initiative is to ensure equity in preparing for the next pandemic. Achieving the progress outlined in this call to action requires collective action from all sectors of society. Therefore, the effort must focus on strengthening community resilience, integrating into existing systems, and building upon them, while also leveraging a wide range of resources for emergency preparedness and response. This call to action was released following a global meeting on Pandemic Preparedness and Resilience (PPR) held in Geneva, Switzerland, from May 24-26, 2023.

translations.source: ወባን ለመዋጋት የሚመከሩ መሳሪያዎችን ተግባራዊ ለማድረግ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት አሳስቧል የዓለም የወባ ቀንን በማስመልከት የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት (WHO) ከወባ በሽታ ህይወትን ለመታደግ አዳዲስ እና ነባር እርምጃዎችን ተግባራዊ ለማድረግ ጥሪ አቅርቧል። በጋና፣ኬንያ እና ማላዊ ለወባ ለከፍተኛ ለበሽታ እና ለሞት የተጋለጡ ወደ 1.5 ሚሊዮን የሚጠጉ ህጻናት አሁን በአለም ጤና ድርጅት (WHO) አስተባባሪነት የመጀመሪያውን የወባ ክትባት RTS,S/AS01 (RTS,S) አግኝተዋል ። እ.ኤ.አ በ 2019 የተጀመረው የመጀመሪያው የወባ ክትባት ዙር ፣ በጣም ተጋላጭ ለሆኑ ሰዎች የወባ መከላከልን ተደራሽነት ፍትሃዊነት በማሳደግ ህይወትን እየታደጉ ይገኛሉ። በሰፊው ተግባራዊ ከሆነም፣ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት የወባ ክትባቶቹ በየዓመቱ በአሥር ሺዎች የሚቆጠሩ የሕፃናትን ሕይወት ሊታደጉ እንደሚችሉ ይገምታል።
translations.reference: WHO urges increased implementation of recommended tools to combat malaria Marking World Malaria Day, the World Health Organization (WHO) is issuing a call for increased implementation of new and existing interventions to save lives from malaria. Nearly 1.5 million children at high risk of illness and death from malaria in Ghana, Kenya and Malawi have now received their first dose of the first malaria vaccine, RTS,S/AS01 (RTS,S), thanks to an ongoing pilot programme coordinated by WHO. The malaria vaccine pilots, launched in 2019, are increasing equity in access to malaria prevention for the most vulnerable and are saving lives. If implemented broadly, WHO estimates that malaria vaccines could save the lives of tens of thousands of children each year.
translations.hypothesis: In commemoration of World Malaria Day, the World Health Organization (WHO) has called for the implementation of recommended tools to combat malaria. The WHO has urged the implementation of new and existing measures to protect lives from malaria. In Ghana, Kenya, and Malawi, approximately 1.5 million children who are at high risk of contracting malaria have now received the first dose of the world's first malaria vaccine, RTS,S/AS01 (RTS,S), coordinated by the WHO. The first round of the malaria vaccine, which began in 2019, is helping to save lives by making malaria prevention more accessible to those most at risk. If implemented widely, the WHO estimates that malaria vaccines could save tens of thousands of children's lives each year.

translations.source: "ወባን ለማጥፋት የሚረዱ መሳሪያዎች፣ የአስተላላፊ ትንኝ ቁጥጥር፣ የመከላከያ መድሃኒቶች፣ ምርመራ እና ህክምናን ያካተቱ የክትትል ክምችቶች አሉን፣" ሲሉ ዶክተር ቴዎድሮስ አድሃኖም ገብረእየሱስ የአለም ጤና ድርጅት ዋና ዳይሬክተር ተናግረዋል። “እነዚህ፣ በየአመቱ በአስር ሺዎች የሚቆጠሩ ህጻናትን ሊያድኑ ከሚችሉ፣ ደህንነቱ የተጠበቀ እና ውጤታማ ክትባት ጋር ተቀላቅለው ይገኛሉ። በዘላቂ ኢንቨስትመንት እና በተጠናከረ ጥረት ለአደጋ ለተጋለጡትን በማዳረስ፣ በብዙ አገሮች አሁን ወባን ለማጥፋት ተደርሷል። """ የዓለም የወባ ቀን 2023 " አሁን ዜሮ ወባን የማዳረስ ጊዜ: መዋዕለ ንዋይ ማፍሰስ፣ አዲስ ግኝት መፍጠር፣ መተግበር( Time to deliver zero malaria:invest, inovate, implement" በሚል መሪ ቃል እየተከበረ ነው። በዚህ ጭብጥ ውስጥ፣ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት የወባ በሽታን ለመከላከል፣ ለመመርመር እና ለማከም፣ በተለይ በወባ ተጋላጭ በሆኑ ህብረተሰብ ዘንድ የበለጠ ውጤታማ በሆነ መንገድ እንዲተገበር የሚያስችሉ መሳሪያዎች እና ስልቶች እንዲመጡ እያነሳሳ ይገኛል።
translations.reference: “We have the tools to drive down malaria, a package of interventions that includes vector control, preventive medicines, testing, and treatment,” said Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Director-General of WHO. “These are joined by a safe and effective malaria vaccine, which could save the lives of tens of thousands children every year. With sustained investment and scaled-up efforts to reach those most at risk, malaria elimination in many countries is in reach. "World Malaria Day 2023 is being marked under the theme “Time to deliver zero malaria: invest, innovate, implement”. Within this theme, WHO is urging more effective implementation of available tools and strategies to prevent, diagnose and treat malaria, particularly among marginalized populations.
translations.hypothesis:
"We have the tools to eradicate malaria: vector control, diagnostic testing, treatment, and preventive medications," said Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Director-General of the World Health Organization. "When combined with safe and effective vaccines, these tools can save tens of thousands of children every year. With sustained investment and intensified efforts, we are now on the verge of eradicating malaria in many countries."

World Malaria Day 2023 is being observed under the theme "Time to deliver zero malaria: invest, innovate, implement." Within this framework, the World Health Organization is highlighting the need for new tools and strategies to prevent, diagnose, and treat malaria, particularly those that are more effective in communities at high risk.

translations.source: እ.ኤ.አ በታህሳስ 2022 በታተመው የመጨረሻው የአለም የወባ ሪዘገባ መሰረት፣ እ.ኤ.አ በ2021 ወደ 247 ሚሊዮን የሚጠጉ አዳዲስ የወባ በሽታ የተያዙ ሰዎች እንዳሉ ይገመታል። የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት የአፍሪካ ክልል የበሽታውን ከባድ ሸክም መሸከሙን ቀጥሏል -እ.ኤ.አ በ 2021 ከጠቅላላው በወባ በሽታ የተያዙ ሰዎች 95 በመቶ (234 ሚሊዮን) እና ከጠቅላላው ሞት 96 በመቶውን (593 000) ይሸፍናል ። በአፍሪካ ክልል 80 በመቶ የሚጠጋው የወባ ሞት ከአምስት አመት በታች የሆኑ ህጻናት ናቸው። አዳዲስ ስልቶች እና መሳሪያዎች አገሮች ለአብዛኛዎቹ ተጋላጭ ለሆኑ ህብረተሰቦች የወባ መከላከል አገልግሎት ተደራሽነትን በማስፋት ረገድ መጠነኛ መሻሻል አሳይተዋል።
translations.reference: According to the latest World malaria report, published in December 2022, there were an estimated 247 million new cases of malaria in 2021. The WHO African Region continues to shoulder the heaviest burden of the disease – accounting for an estimated 95% of all malaria cases (234 million) and 96% of all deaths (593 000) in 2021. Nearly 80% of malaria deaths in the African Region were among children under the age of five. New strategies and tools Countries have made some progress in expanding access to malaria services for most-at-risk populations.
translations.hypothesis: According to the latest global malaria report, published in December 2022, an estimated 247 million people were infected with malaria in 2021. The World Health Organization (WHO) reports that the African region continues to bear the brunt of the disease, accounting for 95% of all malaria cases (234 million) and 96% of all malaria deaths (593,000) globally in 2021. In the African region, approximately 80% of malaria deaths are among children under the age of five. New strategies and tools have shown some progress in expanding access to malaria prevention services for the most vulnerable populations in many countries.

translations.source: ምንም እንኳን መጠነኛ መሻሻል ቢኖረውም፣ ብዙ ለወባ የተጋለጡ ሰዎች አሁንም በሽታውን ለመከላከል፣ ለመለየት እና ለማከም የሚያስችል አገልግሎት አያገኙም። የወባ መከላከል አገልግሎት ተደራሽነትን በማስፋፋት ላይ ያሉ ተግዳሮቶች በተለይም ከሰሃራ በታች ባሉ የአፍሪካ ሀገራት በኮቪድ-19 ወረርሽኝ ፣በሰብአዊ ቀውሶች ፣ በተገደበ የገንዘብ ድጋፍ ፣ በደካማ የክትትል ስርዓቶች እና የወባ መከላከያ መሳሪያዎች ውጤታማነት ላይ ተዕጽኖ አድርጓል። እነዚህን ስጋቶች ለመቅረፍ እና ሀገራት የበለጠ የሚቋቋሙ የወባ መከላከል መርሀግብሮችን በመገንባት ለመደገፍ፣ የአለም ጤና ድርጅት አዲስ ስትራቴጂዎችን እና ማዕቀፎችን በቅርቡ አሳትሟል፣ ከእነዚህም መካከል፡- በአፍሪካ ውስጥ የፀረ ወባ መድሐኒት አለመቀበልን የሚያካትት አዲስ ስልት; በከተማ አካባቢ የአኖፌሌስ ስቴፈንሲ ስርጭትን ለማስቆም አዲስ ተነሳሽነት;
translations.reference: Despite some progress, many people at high risk of malaria still lack access to services that can prevent, detect and treat the disease. Challenges in expanding access to malaria services have been compounded, particularly in sub-Saharan Africa, by the COVID-19 pandemic, converging humanitarian crises, restricted funding, weak surveillance systems, and declines in the effectiveness of core malaria-fighting tools. To address these threats and support countries in building more resilient malaria programmes, WHO recently published new strategies and frameworks, including: a new strategy to contain antimalarial drug resistance in Africa; a new initiative to stop the spread of Anopheles stephensi in urban environments;
translations.hypothesis: While there has been some progress, many people at risk of malaria still do not have access to the services they need to prevent, detect, and treat the disease. Challenges to expanding access to malaria control services, particularly in sub-Saharan Africa, have been exacerbated by the COVID-19 pandemic, humanitarian crises, limited funding, weak surveillance systems, and the effectiveness of malaria control tools. To address these challenges and support countries in building more resilient malaria control programs, the World Health Organization has recently released new strategies and frameworks, including: a new strategy to address the issue of substandard and falsified antimalarial medicines in Africa; and a new initiative to eliminate *Anopheles stephensi* mosquitoes in urban areas.

translations.source: በከተማዎች፣ ወባ መከላከል ላይ የከተማ መሪዎችን ለመምራት በ አለም ጤና ድርጅት(WHO) እና በ ዩኤን-ሀቢታት(UN-Habitat) በጋራ የተዘጋጀ አዲስ ማዕቀፍ። ሀገራት የወባ ክትትል ስርዓታቸውን ለመገምገም እና የኢንቨስትመንት ቦታዎችን ለመለየት የሚረዳ አዲስ መሳሪያ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት የወባ መከላከል ምክሮቹን ግልጽነት፣ ተለዋዋጭነት እና ተደራሽነትን ጨምሯል። የተቀናጀው የአለም ጤና ድርጅት የወባ መመሪያዎች አሁን በሁለት ዲጂታል መድረኮች ይገኛሉ፡ 'MAGICapp' እና "Malaria Toolkit" መተግበሪያ። የአለም ጤና ድርጅት ከፍተኛ ተጽእኖ ያለው ውጤት እንዲያመጡ ሀገራት አቅጣጫዎቻቸውን ከአካባቢያቸው ሁኔታ ጋር በማጣጣም እንዲያስተካክሉ ይመክራል።
translations.reference: a new framework, developed jointly by WHO and UN-Habitat, to guide city leaders in urban malaria control. a new toolkit to help countries assess their malaria surveillance systems and identify areas for investment WHO has also increased the transparency, flexibility and access to its malaria recommendations. The consolidated WHO Guidelines for malaria are now available through two digital platforms: MAGICapp and the “Malaria Toolkit” app. WHO encourages countries to tailor the recommendations to local disease settings for maximum impact.
translations.hypothesis: In cities, a new framework developed jointly by the World Health Organization (WHO) and UN-Habitat aims to guide city leaders in malaria prevention. The WHO has introduced a new tool to help countries assess their malaria surveillance systems and identify areas for investment. This enhances the clarity, adaptability, and accessibility of the WHO's malaria prevention guidelines. The integrated WHO malaria guidelines are now available on two digital platforms: the 'MAGICapp' and the "Malaria Toolkit" application. The WHO recommends that countries tailor their strategies to local conditions to ensure the most effective results.

translations.source: ለአዳዲስ የግኝት ተስፋዎች እ.ኤ.አ የ2030 የአለም የወባ ግቦችን ለማሳካት፣ አዳዲስ የወባ ክትባቶችን እና የቀጣይ ትውልድ መሳሪያዎችን በማዘጋጀት እና በማሰማራት ላይ የሚደረግ ቀጣይ ኢንቨስትመንቶች ቁልፍ ሚና ይሆናሉ። ሁለተኛው የወባ ክትባት R21/Matrix-M (R21) ክትባት ከፀደቀ፣ በአቅርቦትና በፍላጎት መካከል ያለውን ከፍተኛ ልዩነት ለመዝጋት እና የህፃናትን ህመም እና የወባ ሞትን የበለጠ ለመቀነስ ያስችላል። በመካሄድ ላይ ያለው ከR21 ምዕራፍ 3 ሙከራ ተጨማሪ ቁልፍ የደህንነት እና የውጤታማነት መረጃ ከተገኘ እና ለአለም ጤና ድርጅት(WHO) ከቀረበ በኋላ የ R21 የወባ ክትባት የተሟላ እና ቀልጣፋ የባለሙያዎችን ግምገማ መቀጠል ለ ለአለም ጤና ድርጅት(WHO) ቅድሚያ የሚሰጠው ጉዳይ ነው። የ RTS,S የወባ ክትባት በአፍሪካ ውስጥ ባሉ ተጨማሪ አገሮች ውስጥ የደረጃ በደረጃ መግቢያ እ.ኤ.አ በ 2024 መጀመሪያ ላይ ይጀምራል ተብሎ ይጠበቃል።
translations.reference: Prospects for new interventions Continued investment in the development and deployment of new malaria vaccines and next-generation tools will be key to achieving the 2030 global malaria targets. A second malaria vaccine, the R21/Matrix-M (R21) vaccine, if approved, could help close the sizable gap between supply and demand and further reduce child illness and death from malaria. It is a priority for WHO to continue the thorough and efficient expert review of the R21 malaria vaccine once additional key safety and efficacy data from the ongoing R21 phase 3 trial are available and provided to WHO. Phased introductions of the RTS,S malaria vaccine in additional countries in Africa are expected to begin in early 2024.
translations.hypothesis: To achieve the global malaria targets for 2030, continued investments in the development and deployment of new malaria vaccines and next-generation tools will be crucial. If the second malaria vaccine, R21/Matrix-M (R21), is approved, it will help to close the gap between supply and demand and further reduce the burden of malaria in children. The ongoing Phase 3 trial of R21 is expected to provide further key safety and efficacy data, which will be submitted to the World Health Organization (WHO). A comprehensive and timely expert review of the R21 malaria vaccine remains a high priority for the WHO. The phased rollout of the RTS,S malaria vaccine in additional African countries is expected to begin in early 2024.

translations.source: በአስተላላፊ ትንኝ መቆጣጠሪያ ቦታ ውስጥ፣ በ R & D ቧንቧ ውስጥ 28 አዳዲስ ምርቶች አሉ። በመሞከር ላይ ካሉት መንገዶች ለምሳሌ በጸረነፍሳት የታከሙ አዳዲስ አይነት መረቦች፣ የወባ ትንኞችን ለመሳብ የታለሙ ማጥመጃዎች፣ የግርግዳ ላይ መከላከያዎች፣ ገዳይ የቤት ማባበያዎች (የእንጨት ቱቦዎች) እና የወባ ትንኞች የዘረመል ምህንድስና ያካትታሉ። ተመራማሪዎች በከፊል የአርቴሚሲኒን መቋቋም መከሰትን እና መስፋፋትን ምክንያት አርገው፣ በፀረ-ወባ መድሐኒቶች መስክ ከኤሲቲ-ውጭ (non-ACT) የሆኑ ሕክምናዎች እድገት ቅድሚያ እየሰጡ ነው። "እንደ ""ሶስት ድርብ ኤሲቲዎች (triple ACTs)" ያሉ የቀጣይ ትውልድ መድኃኒቶችን በማዘጋጀት የአርቴሚሲን እና የሁለት አጋር መድኃኒቶች ጥምረት በመጠቀም የመድኃኒት የመቋቋም አደጋን ለመቀነስ በመካሄድ ላይ ነው። ለአርታዒው ማስታወሻ፡ ስለ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት የወባ ቀን ዘመቻ ለበለጠ መረጃ፡ ይጎብኙ፡ https://www.who.int/campaigns/world-malaria-day/2023
translations.reference: In the vector control space, there are 28 new products in the R&D pipeline. Tools under evaluation include, for example, new types of insecticide-treated nets, targeted baits that attract mosquitoes, spatial repellents, lethal house lures (eaves tubes) and genetic engineering of mosquitoes. Researchers are prioritizing the development of non-ACT treatments in the field of antimalarial medicines due to the emergence and spread of partial artemisinin resistance. The development of next-generation medicines, such as "triple ACTs," which use a combination of artemisinin and two partner drugs, is underway to reduce the risk of drug resistance. Notes to the editor:For more information on the WHO World Malaria Day campaign, visit: https://www.who.int/campaigns/world-malaria-day/2023
translations.hypothesis:
Within the global malaria control landscape, the R&D pipeline boasts 28 new products. These include innovative approaches such as insecticide-treated nets, attractants designed to lure mosquitoes, protective barriers, indoor residual sprays (for wooden structures), and genetically modified mosquitoes. Researchers are investigating the emergence and spread of partial artemisinin resistance, and are prioritizing the development of non-artemisinin-based combination therapies (ACTs) in the fight against malaria. "We are working on developing next-generation drugs, such as 'triple ACTs,' which combine artemisinin with two partner drugs, to reduce the risk of drug resistance," the statement reads.

Editor's note: For more information about the World Health Organization's World Malaria Day campaign, please visit: https://www.who.int/campaigns/world-malaria-day/2023.

translations.source: የ ኤም.አር.ኤን.ኤ(mRNA) ቴክኖሎጂ ሽግግር ፕሮግራም ወደ ቀጣዩ የእድገት ደረጃ ተሸጋግሯል በአፍሪገን፣ ደቡብ አፍሪካ ኤም.አር.ኤን.ኤ(mRNA) ቴክኖሎጂ ማዕከል ምርቃት። አጋሮች በኬፕ ታውን ስለ ኤም.አር.ኤን.ኤ(mRNA) ክትባት ልማት እና የእውቀት መጋራት ቀጣዩን ምዕራፍ ለመወያየት ተሰብስበዋል። የ ኤም.አር.ኤን.ኤ(mRNA) ቴክኖሎጂ ሽግግር ፕሮግራም ላይ የሚሰሩ ከ 200 በላይ አለምአቀፍ ተሳታፊዎች፣ በዚህ ሳምንት በኬፕ ታውን ለመጀመሪያ ጊዜ ፊት ለፊት ተገናኝተዋል። የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ዋና ዳይሬክተር ዶ/ር ቴዎድሮስ አድሃኖም ገብረእየሱስ፣ የጤና ጥበቃ ሚኒስትር እና የንግድ እና ኢንዱስትሪ ሚኒስትር ኢብራሂም ፓቴል፣ የደቡብ አፍሪካ ፓቴል ዶ/ር ጆ ፋህላ ፤ ከገንዘብ ድጋፍ ሰጪ ሀገራት ከፍተኛ ባለስልጣናት ጋር በመቀላቀል የመሩት፣ ይህ ልዩ ስብሰባ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት እና የመድኃኒት ፓተንት ፑል (MPP) እ.ኤ.አ በሰኔ 2021 ከጀመሩበት ጊዜ ጀምሮ ያለውን ሂደት ገምግሟል።
translations.reference: mRNA Technology Transfer Programme moves to the next phase of its development Inauguration of the mRNA technology Hub facility at Afrigen, South Africa. Partners gather in Cape Town to discuss the next phase of the mRNA vaccine development and knowledge sharing. Over 200 international participants working with the mRNA Technology Transfer Programme met in Cape Town this week for their first face-to-face meeting. Joined by World Health Organization (WHO) Director General, Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus and Dr. Joe Phaahla, Minister of Health and Minister of Trade and Industry, Mr. Ebrahim Patel of South Africa together with high-level officials from funding countries, this unique meeting reviewed the progress since WHO and Medicines Patent Pool (MPP) launched it in June 2021.
translations.hypothesis: The mRNA Technology Transfer Program has moved to the next stage of growth, according to the African mRNA Manufacturing Hub in South Africa. Partners gathered in Cape Town to discuss the next steps in mRNA vaccine development and knowledge sharing. More than 200 international participants involved in the mRNA Technology Transfer Program met in person for the first time this week in Cape Town. The meeting, led by World Health Organization Director-General Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, along with the Minister of Health and the Minister of Trade and Industry of South Africa, Dr. Joe Phaahla, and senior officials from donor countries, reviewed the progress made since the World Health Organization and the Medicines Patent Pool (MPP) launched the initiative in June 2021.

translations.source: "ዝቅተኛ እና መካከለኛ ገቢ ላላቸው ሀገራት የክትባት እና የሌሎች ህይወት አድን የጤና ምርቶችን ፍትሃዊ ተደራሽነት ለመስጠት ኤም.አር.ኤን.ኤ(mRNA) ቴክኖሎጂ ሽግግር ዘላቂ ሞዴል ለመደገፍ ከአጋሮቻችን ጋር በኬፕ ታውን በመገኘቴ ደስተኛ ነኝ" ሲሉ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ዋና ዳይሬክተር ዶክተር ቴድሮስ አድሃኖም ገብረእየሱስ ገልፀዋል ። "በዚህ ፕሮጀክት ውስጥ በተሳተፉት ሁሉ ስኬት በጣም ኮርቻለሁ; ሁለት ዓመት ባልሞላ ጊዜ ውስጥ ተባብረን ስንሠራ በጋራ እንደሚሳካልንም አሳይተናል። የስብሰባው ተሳታፊዎች በፕሮግራሙ ውስጥ ከ 15 አገሮች የተውጣጡ የባዮማኑፋክቸሪንግ አጋሮችን፣ ዋና ባለሙያዎችን፣ ኢንዱስትሪዎችን፣ የሲቪል ማህበረሰብ ተወካዮች እና የገንዘብ ሰጪዎችን ያካትታሉ። በአምስት ቀናቱ ስብሰባ ውስጥ፣ ተሳታፊዎች ያሳዩትን እድገት ያጋራሉ እና ለፕሮግራሙ ዘላቂነት እንደ የአእምሮአዊ ንብረት ጉዳዮች እና የቁጥጥር ጉዳዮች እንዲሁም ስለ ኤም.አር.ኤን.ኤ(mRNA) ቴክኖሎጂዎች ሳይንስ እና በሌሎች የበሽታ አካባቢዎች ከኤል.ኤም.አይ.ሲዎች(LMICs) ጋር ተዛማጅነት ያላቸውን እንደ ኤች አይ ቪ እና ቲዩበርክሎዝስ ቁልፍ መተግበሪያዎች ላይ ይወያያሉ ። የኮቪድ-19 ወረርሽኝ፣ በጤና ምርቶች በተለይም በክትባቶች ተደራሽነት ላይ ከፍተኛ ኢፍትሃዊነት እንዳለ አመልክቷል።
translations.reference: “I am delighted to be here in Cape Town with our partners to support a sustainable model for mRNA technology transfer to give low- and middle-income countries equitable access to vaccines and other lifesaving health products,” said WHO Director-General Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus. “I am immensely proud of the achievement of all those involved in this project; in less than two years we have shown that when we work collaboratively, we succeed collectively.” The meeting participants include biomanufacturing partners from 15 countries in the Programme, leading experts, industry, civil society representatives, and funders. During the five-day meeting, participants will share progress and discuss critical enablers for the sustainability of the Programme such as intellectual property issues and regulatory aspects, as well as the science of mRNA technologies and key applications relevant to LMICs in other disease areas such as HIV and tuberculosis. The COVID-19 pandemic has underscored that gross inequity exists in access to health products, especially vaccines.
translations.hypothesis: "I am pleased to be in Cape Town with our partners to support a sustainable model for the transfer of mRNA technology, with the aim of ensuring equitable access to vaccines and other life-saving health products for low- and middle-income countries," said Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Director-General of the World Health Organization. "I am very proud of the success achieved by everyone involved in this project; in less than two years, we have demonstrated that working together, we can achieve great things. The meeting participants include biomanufacturing partners, key experts, industry representatives, civil society organizations, and funders from 15 countries. Over the five-day meeting, participants will share progress and discuss key topics for the program's sustainability, including intellectual property issues and regulatory matters, as well as the science of mRNA technologies and their applications in other areas of disease, such as HIV and tuberculosis, which are relevant to low- and middle-income countries. The COVID-19 pandemic has highlighted the significant inequities in access to health products, particularly vaccines."

translations.source: እ.ኤ.አ ከመጋቢት 2023 ጀምሮ፣ የአለም ጤና ድርጅት ኮቪድ-19ን የአለም አቀፍ ስጋት የህዝብ ጤና ድንገተኛ አደጋ (PHEIC) ካወጀ ከሶስት አመታት በኋላ፣ 69.7 በመቶ የሚሆነው የአለም ህዝብ ቢያንስ አንድ የ ኮቪድ-19 ክትባት ወስደዋል። በተለይም ይህ መጠን አሁንም ዝቅተኛ ገቢ ባላቸው አገሮች (LICs) ከ 30 በመቶ በታች እንደሆነ ነው። ይህ ፕሮግራም፣ ከኤል.ኤም.አይ.ሲዎች(LMICs) ዘላቂ የማምረት አቅም ስርጭትን በማሳደግ፣ ክልላዊ እና ክልላዊ ትብብሮችን በማጎልበት እና በተበጀ እና ሁሉን አቀፍ ስልጠና እና የባለሙያዎች ድጋፍ የሀገር ውስጥ የሰው ሃይል በማዳበር እና በማብቃት ኤም.አር.ኤን.ኤ(mRNA) ክትባቶችን ፍትሃዊ ተጠቃሚ ለማድረግ የበኩሉን አስተዋፅኦ ማድረግ ነው። በ ኤም.አር.ኤን.ኤ(mRNA) ቴክኖሎጂ ማስተላለፊያ ሞዴል ውስጥ ልዩ የሆነው ቴክኖሎጂዎችን ለብዙ ተቀባዮች ማጋራት የሚፈቅደው በአገር ውስጥ እና በክልላዊ ምርት እርዳታ የሚያስፈልጋቸውን በፍጥነት እንዲደርሱ የሚያስችል ሁለገብ ሂደት ነው። የደቡብ አፍሪካ የጤና ጥበቃ ሚኒስትር ዶ/ር ፋህላ “ዛሬ ታሪካዊ ወቅት ነው፤ ይህ መርሃ ግብር ዝቅተኛ እና መካከለኛ ገቢ ያላቸዎን ሃገራት በአለምአቀፍ የትብብር ትስስር ለማጎልበት ያለመ ነው” ብለዋል።
translations.reference: As of March 2023, more than three years after WHO declared COVID-19 a Public Health Emergency of International Concern (PHEIC), 69.7% of the global population had received at least one dose of a COVID-19 vaccine. Notably, this proportion still remains below 30% in low-income countries (LICs) . This Programme aims to contribute to equitable access to mRNA vaccines by increasing the distribution of sustainable manufacturing capacity across LMICs, enhancing regional and inter-regional collaborations, and developing and empowering a local workforce through tailored and inclusive training and expert support. What is unique in the mRNA Technology Transfer model is the multilateral process that allows sharing of technologies to multiple recipients so that through local and regional production those in need can be reached rapidly. Dr Phaahla, Minister of Health South Africa, said: “What we see here today, is a moment in history, a Programme that is aimed at empowering LMICs through a global collaborative network.
translations.hypothesis: Since March 2023, more than 69.7 percent of the world's population has received at least one dose of a COVID-19 vaccine, three years after the World Health Organization declared COVID-19 a Public Health Emergency of International Concern (PHEIC). However, this figure remains below 30 percent in low-income countries (LICs). This program aims to contribute to equitable access to mRNA vaccines by increasing the sustainable manufacturing capacity of low- and middle-income countries (LMICs), strengthening regional and local partnerships, and developing and empowering a domestic workforce through tailored and comprehensive training and expert support. The mRNA technology transfer model is unique in that it allows for the sharing of technologies with multiple recipients, enabling a streamlined process to quickly provide assistance to countries that need it, both domestically and regionally. South African Minister of Health, Dr. Phaahla, stated, "Today is a historic moment; this program is designed to strengthen international cooperation among low- and middle-income countries."

translations.source: በአንፃራዊ ሁኔታ በአጭር ጊዜ ውስጥ የተገኘውን እድገት በማየቴ በጣም ደስተኛ ነኝ እና - እንደ ደቡብ አፍሪካ ያሉ ጠንካራ የባዮማኑፋክቸሪንግ አቅም ያላቸው እና በጋራ ለመስራት ፣ አንዱ ከሌላው ለመማር እና ለማካፈል ፈቃደኛ የሆኑ፣ ከተለያዩ ሀገራት የሚደረገውን ድጋፍ በደስታ ተቀብያለሁ።" የከፍተኛ ትምህርት እና ሳይንስ ሚኒስትር ብሌድ ንዚማንዴ ፣ "እኛ የያዝነው ከኮቪድ-19 ባለፈ ኤም.አር.ኤን.ኤ(mRNA) ቴክኖሎጂ ማስተላለፊያ ማዕከል ራዕይ ነው። የምንገነባው ችሎታዎች ሌሎች ወደፊት ወረርሽኞችን ለመቋቋም የሚያስችል ኃይልን ለመስጠት ያለመ ሲሆን፣ ክትባታቸውም በተመሳሳይ ኤም.አር.ኤን.ኤ(mRNA)ን የቴክኖሎጂ መድረክ ሊጠቀም ይችላል። በአካባቢያችን እየተስፋፉ ያሉ እንደ ወባ፣ ሳንባ ነቀርሳ፣ ኤችአይቪ እና ኤድስ ባሉ በሽታዎች ላይም ዓይኑን ያሳረፈ ራዕይ ነው። ይህ አጋጣሚ ዶ/ር ቴድሮስ እና ዶ/ር ማትሺዲሶ ሞኢቲ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት እና የአፍሪካ ክልላዊ ዳይሬክተር ቻርለስ ጎሬ የኤምፒፒ ዋና ዳይሬክተር እና በአፍሪገን ሪባን የመቁረጥ ሥነ ሥርዓት ላይ የተሳተፉት የተከበሩ ሚኒስትሮች በተገኙበት አፍሪገን የሚገኘውን ኤም.አር.ኤን.ኤ(mRNA) ቴክኖሎጂ ሃብ ተቋም በኬፕ ታውን መከፈቱ ለፕሮግራሙ ጠቃሚ ምዕራፍ ነው፣ ።
translations.reference: I am thrilled to see the progress made in such a relatively short time and welcome the support from so many different countries - countries like South Africa that have a strong vibrant biomanufacturing capacity and that are willing to work together, learn from and share with each other.” Minister Blade Nzimande of Higher Education and Science, said: “Ours is the vision of the mRNA Technology Transfer Hub beyond just COVID-19. The capabilities we are building are looking to empower us to deal with other future pandemics whose vaccines could use the same mRNA technology platform. It is a vision that has set its eyes on diseases that are prevalent in our environments such as Malaria, Tuberculosis and HIV and AIDS.” This occasion also marks an important milestone for the Programme with the inauguration of the mRNA technology Hub facility at Afrigen in Cape Town in the presence of Dr. Tedros and Dr. Matshidiso Moeti WHO Regional Director for Africa, Charles Gore Executive Director of MPP and the Honorable Ministers who took part in a ribbon cutting ceremony at Afrigen.
translations.hypothesis: "I am incredibly pleased to see the progress made in such a short time. I am also delighted to receive the support from various countries, including South Africa, which has a strong biomanufacturing capacity and is willing to collaborate, learn from each other, and share knowledge." said Minister of Higher Education, Science and Technology, Blade Nzimande. "Our vision is to establish an mRNA technology transfer hub, not just for responding to COVID-19, but also to build capacity that will enable other countries to respond to future pandemics, and to use the same mRNA technology platform for vaccines. This vision also addresses the growing burden of diseases in our region, such as malaria, tuberculosis, HIV, and AIDS." This milestone was marked by the opening of the mRNA technology hub in Cape Town, South Africa, in the presence of Dr. Tedros, Dr. Matshidiso Moeti, Director-General of the World Health Organization and Regional Director for Africa, and Mr. Charles Gore, CEO of Medicines Patent Pool, along with other distinguished ministers who participated in the ribbon-cutting ceremony.

translations.source: የአፍሪገን ዋና ዳይሬክተር ፕሮፌሰር ፔትሮ ቴርባንቼ እንዳሉት፡ “መላው የአፍሪጀን ቡድን ኤም.አር.ኤን.ኤ(mRNA) ቴክኖሎጅ መድረክ ተጠናቆ ወደዚህ ወሳኝ ምዕራፍ በመድረሳቸው በጣም ተደስተዋል። ይህ መድረክ ከጫፍ እስከ ጫፍ ባለው ኤም.አር.ኤን.ኤ(mRNA) ክትባት ልማት እና የማምረቻ ተቋም ውስጥ የሚገኝ ሲሆን ኤም.አር.ኤን.ኤ(mRNA) ሀብ ኮቪድ 19 ክትባት እጩ አፍሪቫክ 2121 በአሁኑ ጊዜ በማሳደግ ደረጃ ላይ ይገኛል። ባለፉት 18 ወራት ውስጥ አፍሪገን በዚህ ፕሮግራም በነቃ የአጋሮች እና የአማካሪዎች ኔትወርክ ድጋፍ የማይታመን ለውጥ አድርጓል። የ ኤም.አር.ኤን.ኤ(mRNA) ክትባቶችን ለማምረት በ ከኤል.ኤም.አይ.ሲዎች(LMICs) ውስጥ ዘላቂ አቅም የመገንባት ዓላማን በማገልገል ከፍተኛ የጥራት ደረጃዎችን ለማሟላት አቅማችንን እና ብቃታቸወንን አሳድገናል። አፍሪገን የኮቪድ-19 ክትባት ማምረቻ ሂደትን በላብራቶሪ ደረጃ በተሳካ ሁኔታ አቋቁሞ በአሁኑ ጊዜ ያን ሂደት በደረጃ I/2 ክሊኒካዊ ሙከራዎች ወደ ጂኤምፒ(GMP) ደረጃ ለመጠቀም የክትባት ስብስቦችን ለማምረት ተስማሚ ደረጃ ላይ ይገኛል።
translations.reference: Professor Petro Terblanche, Executive Director of Afrigen said: “The entire Afrigen team are thrilled to reach this important milestone with the completion of the mRNA technology platform. This platform is housed within the end-to-end mRNA vaccine development and production facility where the mRNA Hub Covid 19 vaccine candidate AfriVac 2121 is currently in scale-up phase. Over the last 18 months, Afrigen has undergone an incredible transformation with the support of a network of partners and mentors enabled by this Programme. We have grown our capability and capacity to meet the highest quality standards of mRNA vaccine development, serving the objective to build sustainable capacity in LMICs to produce mRNA vaccines”. Afrigen has successfully established a COVID-19 vaccine manufacturing process at a laboratory scale and is currently scaling up that process to a level suitable for manufacturing vaccine batches to be used in Phase I/ II clinical trials to GMP standards.
translations.hypothesis: According to Professor Petro Terbance, CEO of African Union, "The entire African Union team is thrilled to have reached this crucial milestone in the mRNA technology platform. This platform, which covers the entire mRNA vaccine development and manufacturing process, is currently scaling up the mRNA-based COVID-19 vaccine candidate, Afrigen 2121. Over the past 18 months, African Union, with the support of its partners and collaborators, has made remarkable progress in this program. We have strengthened our capacity and capabilities to meet the highest quality standards, with the aim of building a sustainable mRNA vaccine manufacturing capacity within LMICs (Low and Middle-Income Countries). African Union has successfully established the COVID-19 vaccine manufacturing process at the laboratory level and is now ready to move it to GMP (Good Manufacturing Practice) standards for Phase I/2 clinical trials, enabling the production of vaccine batches."

translations.source: በትይዩ ሂደት፣ አፍሪገን የስልጠና እና የቴክኖሎጂ ሽግግርን ለኔትወርክ አጋሮች ማከናወኑን ይቀጥላል። የገንዘብ አቅራቢዎቹ ፕሮግራሙን በጠቅላላ በ 117 ሚሊዮን ዩኤስ ዶላር በመደገፍ ወሳኝ ሚና ይጫወታሉ እናም ፈረንሳይ ኤም.አር.ኤን.ኤ(mRNA) ቴክኖሎጂ ሽግግር ስራ የመጀመሪያዋ ነች። በስብሰባው ላይ የአውሮፓ ኮሚሽን፣ ቤልጂየም፣ ጀርመን፣ እንዲሁም ኖርዌይ፣ ካናዳ፣ አፍሪካ ህብረት፣ ደቡብ አፍሪካ እና የኤልማ ፋውንዴሽን ተወካዮች ተገኝተዋል። የግሎባል አፌርስ የካናዳ የደቡብ እና የምስራቅ አፍሪካ ዋና ዳይሬክተር ካሮላይን ዴላኒ፡ "ካናዳ ኤም.አር.ኤን.ኤ(mRNA) የቴክኖሎጂ ሽግግር መርሃ ግብር ቀጣይነት ያለው ድጋፍ እንዳረጋገጠች እና ዛሬ በስብሰባው ላይ የ 15 ሚሊዮን የካናዳ ዶላር ተጨማሪ መዋጮ ማድረጓን በደስታ እንገልፃለን። ይህ የካናዳ አጠቃላይ መዋጮ ለፕሮግራሙ እስከ 45 ሚሊዮን የካናዳ ዶላር ያደርሰዋል።
translations.reference: In a parallel process, Afrigen will continue to carry out training and technology transfer to the network partners. The funders play a crucial role in supporting the Programme with total funding to date at US$ 117 million and France being the first to fund the mRNA technology transfer work. Present at the meeting were representatives from the European Commission, Belgium, Germany, as well as Norway, Canada, African Union, South Africa, and the ELMA Foundation. Caroline Delany, GlobalAffairs Canada’s Director General for Southern and Eastern Africa said: “Canada reaffirms its continued support of the mRNA Technology Transfer Programme and we are delighted to announce a further contribution of 15 million Canadian dollars at the meeting today. This brings Canada’s total contribution to the Programme up to 45 million Canadian dollars.
translations.hypothesis: Throughout the process, African institutions continue to implement training programs and technology transfer initiatives with their network partners. Financial partners play a crucial role, providing a total of $117 million to support the program, with France being the first to initiate mRNA technology transfer efforts. The meeting was attended by representatives from the European Commission, Belgium, Germany, as well as Norway, Canada, the African Union, South Africa, and the Elma Foundation. Caroline Delaney, Global Affairs Canada's Director General for South and East Africa, stated: "Canada is pleased to reaffirm its ongoing support for the mRNA technology transfer initiative and to announce today an additional contribution of $15 million from Canada. This brings Canada's total contribution to the program to $45 million."

translations.source: እነዚህ ገንዘቦች የ ሀቡን ሥራ በደቡብ አፍሪካ እና በዓለም ዙሪያ ያሉ የኔትወርክ አጋሮችን ይደግፋሉ። በአከባቢ እና በክልል ደረጃ የአቅም ግንባታ አስፈላጊነትን አጥብቀንም እናምናለን"፣ሲሉ የአውሮፓ ኮሚሽን ምክትል ዋና ዳይሬክተር ሚስተር ማርቲን ሲሼል ተናግረዋል። "ኢሲ(EC) ቀደም ሲል የኤም.አር.ኤን.ኤ(mRNA) የቴክኖሎጂ ሽግግር ማዕከልን ለማቋቋም 40 ሚሊዮን ዩሮ ያበረከተ ሲሆን በቅርቡ ደግሞ የክትባትን የማምረት አቅምን ለማሳለጥ የ15,5 ሚሊዮን ዩሮ ሌላ የገንዘብ ድጋፍ ከኢ.አይ.ቢ(EIB) ጋር ተፈራርሟል። ይህ በአውሮፓ ህብረት የጤና ምርቶች ተነሳሽነት ላይ ከ1 ቢሊዮን ዩሮ በላይ ከተሰበሰበበት በአውሮፓ ህብረት ግሎባል የመግቢያ በር ስትራቴጂ ስር ያለው አጠቃላይ ኢንቨስትመንት አካል ነው። በሁሉም የአለም ክልሎች ከፍተኛ ደረጃ ያላቸውን ክትባቶች ማምረት ብቻ ሳይሆን ፈጣን እና ፍትሃዊ የሆነ ክትባቶችን ተደራሽ ለማድረግ አሁን ሊከሰቱ የሚችሉ የቁጥጥር እና የፍላጎት ማነቆዎችን መፍታት አስፈላጊ ነው ።"
translations.reference: These funds support the running of the Hub in South Africa as well as network partners around the world. We firmly believe in the importance of building capacity at local and regional level”. Mr Martin Seychell, Deputy Executive Director European Commission, said. “The EC has already contributed €40 million to the establishment of the mRNA technology transfer Hub and has recently signed another grant with the EIB for €15,5 million to facilitate the expansion of vaccines manufacturing capacity. This is part of the overall investment under the EU Global Gateway strategy, where more than €1 Billion have been mobilised under the Team Europe Initiative on Manufacturing health products. It is now particularly important to also address possible regulatory and demand bottlenecks, to ensure not only production of vaccines of the highest standard in all regions of the world, but also rapid and equitable access to those vaccines.”
translations.hypothesis: "These funds will support Habu's work with network partners in South Africa and around the world. We strongly believe in the importance of building capacity at both the local and regional levels," said Mr. Martin Scheel, Deputy Director-General of the European Commission. "The EC previously contributed €40 million to establish an mRNA technology transfer center, and recently, a further €15.5 million in funding was agreed with the EIB to enhance vaccine manufacturing capacity. This is part of a broader investment of over €1 billion within the European Union's Global Gateway strategy, focused on health products. It is essential not only to produce high-quality vaccines in all regions of the world, but also to address potential bottlenecks in supply and demand to ensure rapid and equitable access to vaccines."

translations.source: የአለም ጤና ድርጅት ስትራቴጂካዊ አቅጣጫዎችን እና ተነሳሽነቶችን ለመምራት ቁልፍ የአመራር ሹመቶች የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት (WHO) በጄኔቫ በሚገኘው ዋና መሥሪያ ቤቱ አመራር ቡድን ውስጥ አምስት አዳዲስ ከፍተኛ ሰዎችን ሾሟል። አዲሶቹ ሹመቶች ዶ/ር ቴዎድሮስ አድሃኖም ገብረእየሱስን ለሁለተኛ አምስት ዓመታት በዋና ዳይሬክተርነት መሾማቸውን ተከትሎ ነው። አጠቃላይ የአመራር ቡድኑ ለቀጣዮቹ አምስት አመታት ከድርጅቱ ቅድሚያ ከሚሰጣቸው ጉዳዮች ጋር እንዲጣጣም የተጠናከረ ሲሆን እነዚህን ቅድሚያ የሚሰጣቸውን ጉዳዮች እና የአለም ጤና ድርጅትን ትልቅ የለውጥ አጀንዳ ለማስቀጠል ከዋና ዳይሬክተር ጋር በቅርበት ይሰራሉ። የአለም ጤና ድርጅት 13ኛ አጠቃላይ የስራ መርሃ ግብር (GPW13) አተገባበር እና የ"ሶስትዮሽ ቢሊየን" ግቦችን እና ከጤና ጋር የተያያዙ የዘላቂ ልማት ግቦችን ለማሳካት እድገትን ለማፋጠን ለሚቀጥሉት አምስት አመታት የአለም ጤና ድርጅት ስራ በአምስት ቅድሚያዎች ላይ ያተኮረ ነው።
translations.reference: Key leadership appointments made to drive WHO strategic direction and initiatives The World Health Organization (WHO) has appointed five new senior figures to its headquarters leadership team in Geneva. The new appointments follow the reappointment of Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus to his second five-year term as Director-General. The overall leadership team has been consolidated to align with the Organization’s priorities for the next five years and will work closely with the Director-General to drive forward these priorities and WHO’s ambitious transformation agenda. To accelerate progress on implementation of WHO’s 13th General Programme of Work (GPW13), and achievement of its “triple billion” targets and the health-related Sustainable Development Goals, WHO’s work for the coming five years is focused on five priorities:
translations.hypothesis: The World Health Organization (WHO) has appointed five new senior officials to its leadership team at its headquarters in Geneva. These appointments follow the re-election of Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus as Director-General for a second five-year term. The strengthened leadership team will work closely with the Director-General to align with the organization's priorities for the next five years and to advance the WHO's ambitious agenda for change. The WHO's 13th General Programme of Work (GPW13) focuses on five priorities for the next five years, aiming to accelerate progress towards the "triple billion" goals and other health-related Sustainable Development Goals.

translations.source: ጤናን ወደ ማጎልበት እና የበሽታን መንስዔዎችን በመቅረፍ ማዕቀፍ ለውጥ ማድረግ; የጤና ስርዓቶችን ወደ የመጀመሪያ ደረጃ ጤና አጠባበቅ ማስተካከል; ለጤና ድንገተኛ አደጋ ዝግጁነት እና ምላሽ ስርዓቶችን ማጠናከር; የጤና ጥበቃን ወደፊት ለማራመድ የሳይንስ፣ ፈጠራ፣ መረጃ እና ቴክኖሎጂዎች ኃይልን መጠቀም; እና የዓለም ጤና ድርጅትን እንደ መሪ ባለስልጣን ማጠናከር እና በአገሮች ላይ ተጽእኖ ማሳደር።
translations.reference: making a paradigm shift towards promoting health and preventing disease by addressing its root causes; radically reorienting health systems towards primary health care; strengthening the systems for health emergency preparedness and response; harnessing the power of science, innovation, data and technologies to advance health; and strengthening WHO as the leading authority on global health and to achieve impact in countries.
translations.hypothesis: Promoting health and addressing the root causes of disease through policy changes; strengthening health systems to prioritize primary healthcare; enhancing preparedness and response systems for health emergencies; leveraging the power of science, innovation, data, and technology to advance healthcare; and strengthening the World Health Organization's leadership role and influence on countries.

translations.source: እኩል ቁጥር ያላቸውን የሴቶች እና የወንዶች ቁጥር ያቀፈው የዋናው መሥሪያ ቤት አመራር ቡድን ከዓለም ጤና ድርጅት የክልል እና የሀገር ውስጥ ቢሮዎች ጋር በጋራ ይሰራል። የዋናው መሥሪያ ቤት አመራር ቡድን አዲስ አባላት፡ ዶ/ር ጄረሚ ፋራራ እ.ኤ.አ ከግንቦት 8 ቀን 2023 ጀምሮ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ዋና ተመራማሪ ይሆናሉ። የዶ/ር ፋራር ሹመት ቀደም ብሎ እ.ኤ.አ በታኅሣሥ 2022 ይፋ ነበር። በዚህ ተግባር ዶ/ር ፋራር የሳይንስ ክፍልን ይቆጣጠራሉ፣ በዓለም ዙሪያ ያሉ ምርጥ የሳይንስ እና ፈጠራ ባለሙያዎችን እና ኔትወርኮችን በማሰባሰብ ከፍተኛ ጥራት ያላቸውን የጤና ፖሊሲዎች እና አገልግሎቶችን በጣም ለሚያስፈልጋቸው ሰዎች እንዲመሩ፣ እንዲያዘጋጁ እና እንዲያቀርቡ ያደርጋል። ወደ አለም ጤና ድርጀት (WHO) ከመቀላቀላቸው በፊት ዶ/ር ፋራር የዌልኮም ትረስት ዳይሬክተር ነበሩ።
translations.reference: The headquarters leadership team, comprising equal numbers of women and men, will work jointly with WHO regional and country offices. New members of the headquarters leadership team: Dr Jeremy Farrar will become WHO’s Chief Scientist as of 8 May 2023. The appointment of Dr Farrar was previously announced in December 2022. In this role Dr Farrar will oversee the Science Division, bringing together the best experts and networks in science and innovation from around the world to guide, develop and deliver high quality health policies and services to the people who need them most. Prior to joining WHO Dr Farrar was Director of the Wellcome Trust.
translations.hypothesis: The central management team, which includes an equal number of men and women, works closely with the World Health Organization's regional and country offices. The central management team has announced new appointments: Dr. Jeremy Farrar will become the Chief Scientist of the World Health Organization, effective May 8, 2023. Dr. Farrar's appointment was previously announced in December 2022. In this role, Dr. Farrar will lead the science division, bringing together leading scientists and networks from around the world to develop and deliver high-quality health policies and services to those who need them most. Prior to joining the World Health Organization (WHO), Dr. Farrar was the Director of the Wellcome Trust.

translations.source: እ.ኤ.አ በ2013 ዌልኮምን ከመቀላቀሉ በፊት ዶ/ር ፋራር በቬትናም በሚገኘው የሀሩር ክልል በሽታዎች ሆስፒታል የክሊኒካል ምርምር ክፍል ዳይሬክተር በመሆን 17 ዓመታትን አሳልፈዋል፣ የምርምር ፍላጎቶቹ በሚከሰቱ ተላላፊ በሽታዎች ላይ በማተኮር በዓለም አቀፍ ጤና ላይም ያተኮሩ ነበሩ። ዶ/ር ፋራራ የህክምና ዲግሪ እና ፒኤችዲ ያለው ክሊኒካዊ ተመራማሪ ነው። ዶ/ር አይላን ሊ እ.ኤ.አ ከግንቦት 8 ቀን 2023 ጀምሮ ሁለንተናዊ የጤና ሽፋን፣ ጤናማ ህዝቦች ረዳት ዋና ዳይሬክተር ይሆናሉ። በዚህ ሚና ዶ / ር ሊ የአየር ንብረት ለውጥን ፣ የትምባሆ ቁጥጥርን ፣ የኬሚካል ደህንነትን ፣ የመንገድ ደህንነትን ፣ የምግብ ስርዓቶችን ጨምሮ ከአካባቢ ፣ ከማህበራዊ እና ኢኮኖሚያዊ የጤና ጉዳዮች ጋር በተያያዙ ጣልቃገብነቶች የተሻለ ጤና እና ደህንነትን ለማስተዋወቅ እና የተመጣጠነ ምግብ፣ የአካል ብቃት እንቅስቃሴ፣ የአየር ብክለት እና ጨረሮች በአንድ ጤና አቀራረብ የሚያረገውን የድርጅቱን ጥረት ይቆጣጠራል። ዶ/ር ሊ እ.ኤ.አ ከሀምሌ 15 ቀን 2019 ጀምሮ በካምቦዲያ መንግሥት የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ተወካይ ሆነው እያገለገሉ ነው።
translations.reference: Before joining Wellcome in 2013 Dr Farrar spent 17 years as Director of the Clinical Research Unit at the Hospital for Tropical Diseases in Vietnam where his research interests were in global health with a focus on emerging infectious diseases. Dr Farrar is a clinician researcher with a medical degree and a PhD. Dr Ailan Li will become Assistant Director-General for Universal Health Coverage, Healthier Populations as of 8 May 2023. In this role Dr Li will oversee the Organization’s efforts to promote better health and well-being through interventions relating to the environmental, social, and economic determinants of health, including climate change, tobacco control, chemical safety, road safety, food systems and nutrition, physical activity, air pollution and radiation, through a One Health approach. Dr Li has been serving as the WHO Representative to the Kingdom of Cambodia since 15 July 2019.
translations.hypothesis: Prior to joining Wellcome in 2013, Dr. Farrar spent 17 years as the Director of the Clinical Research Department at the Hospital for Tropical Diseases in Hanoi, Vietnam. His research focused on global health, with a particular emphasis on infectious diseases. Dr. Farrar is a clinical researcher with a medical degree and a PhD. Dr. Ailan Li will become the Deputy Director of the Department of Health and Well-being, effective May 8, 2023. In this role, Dr. Li will oversee the organization's efforts to promote better health and safety through interventions related to environmental, social, and economic health issues, including climate change, tobacco control, chemical safety, and road safety, as well as food systems. She will also lead the organization's "One Health" approach, which addresses issues such as nutrition, physical activity, air pollution, and radiation. Dr. Li has been serving as the World Health Organization Representative to the Government of Cambodia since July 15, 2019.

translations.source: ከዚህ ሚና በፊት ዶ / ር ሊ በምዕራብ ፓስፊክ ውስጥ በአለም ጤና ድርጅት ክልላዊ ቢሮ ውስጥ ለ አለም ጤና ድርጅት የጤና ድንገተኛ አደጋዎች መርሃ ግብር የክልል ድንገተኛ አደጋ ዳይሬክተር ሆነው አገልግለዋል። ዶ/ር ሊ በጤና ማህበራዊ ሳይንስ ዲግሪ እና የህክምና ዲግሪ አላቸው። ዶ/ር ዩኪኮ ናካታኒ እ.ኤ.አ ከግንቦት 2 ቀን 2023 ጀምሮ የመድኃኒት እና የጤና ምርቶች ተደራሽነት ረዳት ዋና ዳይሬክተር ይሆናሉ። በዚህ ሚና ዶ/ር ናካታኒ የ አለም ጤና ድርጅት ደንቦች እና ፖሊሲዎች ልማት እና አተገባበር ይቆጣጠራሉ ፣ ይህም ወረርሽኞችን ለመከላከል እና ምላሽ ለመስጠትን ጨምሮ በሁሉም ቦታ ለሚገኙ ሁሉም ህዝቦች ጥራት ያለው መድሃኒት ፣ ክትባቶች እና ምርመራዎች ፍትሃዊ ተደራሽነትን ለማረጋገጥ ነው። ዶ/ር ናካታኒ የጃፓን የጤና፣ የሰራተኛ እና ደህንነት ጥበቃ ሚኒስቴር የካንሰር እና የበሽታ መቆጣጠሪያ ክፍል ዳይሬክተር እና በጃፓን ለኮቪድ-19 የክትባት ግብረ ኃይል ሚኒስትር ዴኤታ የቴክኒክ አማካሪ ሆነው ሲያገለግሉ ቆይተዋል።
translations.reference: Prior to this role, Dr Li served as Regional Emergency Director for the WHO Health Emergencies Programme in the WHO Regional Office for the Western Pacific. Dr Li holds a medical degree and degree in health social sciences. Dr Yukiko Nakatani will become Assistant Director-General for Access to Medicines and Health Products as of 2 May 2023. In this role Dr Nakatani will oversee the development and implementation of WHO’s norms and policies to ensure equitable access to quality medicines, vaccines and diagnostics for all populations everywhere, including for preventing and responding to epidemics. Dr Nakatani has been serving as the Director of the Cancer and Disease Control Division of the Japanese Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare and as the Technical Advisor to the Minister of State for the COVID-19 Vaccination Task Force in Japan.
translations.hypothesis: Prior to this role, Dr. Li served as the Regional Director for the World Health Organization's Health Emergencies Programme at the organization's regional office in the Western Pacific. Dr. Li holds a degree in health sciences and a medical degree. Dr. Yukiko Nakatani will become the Assistant Director-General for Access to Medicines and Health Products, effective May 2, 2023. In this role, Dr. Nakatani will oversee the development and implementation of WHO policies and regulations, ensuring equitable access to quality medicines, vaccines, and diagnostics for all people everywhere, including in the prevention and response to outbreaks. Dr. Nakatani previously served as the Director of the Cancer and Infectious Disease Division at the Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare of Japan, and as a technical advisor to the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare for the COVID-19 vaccination task force in Japan.

translations.source: ዶ/ር ናካታኒ የሕክምና ዲግሪ እና በሕዝብ ጤና ፒኤችዲ አግኝተዋል። ዶ/ር ራዝያ ፔንድሴ እ.ኤ.አ ከግንቦት 4 ቀን 2023 ጀምሮ የሼፍ ዲ ካቢኔ ሆና ተሹማለች። በዚህ ተግባር ዶ/ር ፔንድሴ የድርጅቱን ቅድሚያ የሚሰጣቸውን ተግባራት እና ተነሳሽነቶችን በማገዝ የጄኔራል ዳይሬክተሩን ጽህፈት ቤት ይመራሉ እና በአለም ጤና ድርጅት አመራር ቡድን እና በአለም ጤና ድርጅት ሶስት እርከኖች መካከል ያለውን አሰላለፍ ታረጋግጣለች። ዶ/ር ፔንድሴ በጤናማ ህዝብ እና ተላላፊ ያልሆኑ በሽታዎች ዳይሬክተር ሆነው በአለም ጤና ድርጅት ክልላዊ ቢሮ ለደቡብ-ምስራቅ እስያ ሲያገለግሉ የቆዩ ሲሆን ቀደም ሲል የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት በስሪላንካ ተወካይ ነበሩ። ዶ/ር ፔንደሴ በሕዝብ ጤና ሁለተኛ ዲግሪ እና የሕክምና ዲግሪ አላቸው።
translations.reference: Dr Nakatani holds a medical degree and a PhD in public health. Dr Razia Pendse will become the Chef de Cabinet as of 4 May 2023. In this role Dr Pendse will head the Director-General’s Office, helping to drive the Organization’s priorities and initiatives, and will ensure alignment within the WHO leadership team and across the three levels of WHO. Dr Pendse has been serving as the Director of Healthier Populations and Noncommunicable Diseases in the WHO Regional Office for South-East Asia and was previously the WHO Representative to Sri Lanka. Dr Pendse holds a medical degree and master's degree in public health.
translations.hypothesis: Dr. Nakatani holds a medical degree and a PhD in public health. Dr. Razia Pendse was appointed as the Director-General of the World Health Organization (WHO) effective May 4, 2023. In this role, Dr. Pendse will lead the Director-General's office, supporting the organization's priorities and initiatives, and will ensure coordination between the WHO's leadership team and its three levels of operation. Dr. Pendse previously served as the Director of the Regional Office for South-East Asia at the WHO, where she was responsible for the Department of Health and Noncommunicable Diseases. She also served as the WHO Representative to Sri Lanka. Dr. Pendse holds a second degree in public health and a medical degree.

translations.source: ዶ/ር ጄሮም ሰሎሞን እ.ኤ.አ ከመጋቢት 17 ቀን 2023 ጀምሮ ለአለም አቀፍ የጤና ሽፋን፣ ተላላፊ እና ተላላፊ ያልሆኑ በሽታዎች ረዳት ዋና ዳይሬክተር ሆነው ተሹመዋል። በዚህ ተግባር ዶ/ር ሰሎሞን ኤችአይቪ፣ ሄፓታይተስ መባዛትን፣ በግብረ ሥጋ ግንኙነት የሚተላለፉ ኢንፌክሽኖች፣ ሳንባ ነቀርሳ፣ ወባ፣ ችላ የተባሉ የሞቃታማ ክልል በሽታዎች፣ የአዕምሮ ጤና፣ሥር የሰደደ የመተንፈሻ አካላት በሽታዎች እና ካንሰርን፣ የአደንዛዥ እጾች መዛባት እና ተላላፊ ያልሆኑ በሽታዎችን እንደ የልብና የደም ቧንቧ በሽታ፣ የስኳር በሽታ የመሳሰሉ ሰፋ ያሉ የቴክኒክ ፕሮግራሞችን ይቆጣጠራሉ። ዶ/ር ሰሎሞን በፈረንሳይ ጤና ጥበቃ ሚኒስቴር የጤና ጥበቃ ዋና ዳይሬክተር ሆነው ሲያገለግሉ ቆይተዋል። ከመሾሙ በፊት የአለም ጤና ድርጅት ስራ አስፈፃሚ ቦርድ አባል የነበሩ እና በጤና ስርአት አያያዝ፣ ተላላፊ በሽታዎች እና አለም አቀፍ የህብረተሰብ ጤና ላይ ሰፊ ልምድ ያላቸው ናቸው። ዶ/ር ሰሎሞን የሕክምና ዲግሪ፣ በሕዝብ ጤና ማስተርስ ዲግሪ እና በኤፒዲሚዮሎጂ ፒኤችዲ አግኝተዋል።
translations.reference: Dr Jérôme Salomon will become Assistant Director-General for Universal Health Coverage, Communicable and Noncommunicable Diseases as of 17 April 2023. In this role Dr Salomon will oversee a broad portfolio of technical programmes covering HIV, viral hepatitis, sexually-transmitted infections, tuberculosis, malaria, neglected tropical diseases, mental health, substance use disorders, and noncommunicable diseases such as cardiovascular disease, diabetes, chronic respiratory diseases and cancer. Dr Salomon has been serving as the Director-General for Health at the Ministry of Health and Prevention of France. He was a Member of the WHO Executive Board prior to his appointment and has extensive experience in health systems management, communicable diseases and international public health. Dr Salomon holds a medical degree, a master’s degree in public health and a PhD in epidemiology.
translations.hypothesis: Dr. Jerome Solomon was appointed as the Assistant Director-General for Universal Health Coverage, Communicable and Noncommunicable Diseases at the World Health Organization, effective March 17, 2023. In this role, Dr. Solomon will oversee a wide range of technical programs, including HIV, hepatitis, sexually transmitted infections, tuberculosis, malaria, neglected tropical diseases, mental health, chronic respiratory diseases, and cancer, as well as substance use disorders and noncommunicable diseases such as cardiovascular diseases and diabetes. Prior to this appointment, Dr. Solomon served as the Director-General of Health at the French Ministry of Health. Before that, he was a member of the Executive Board of the World Health Organization and has extensive experience in health systems, communicable diseases, and global public health. Dr. Solomon holds a medical degree, a Master's degree in Public Health, and a PhD in Epidemiology.

translations.source: የዋና መሥሪያ ቤቱ አመራር ቡድን ነባር አባላት የስልጣንና የልምድ ደረጃ፡- ዶ/ር ሰሚራ አስማ የመረጃ፣ ትንታኔ እና ለተፅእኖ አቅርቦት ረዳት ዋና ዳይሬክተር ሆነው ይቀጥላሉ። የዶ/ር አስማ የስራ ሀላፊነት ከተባለው በላይ ሲሰፋ፣ የጤና መረጃዎች አስተማማኝ እና ተደራሽ መሆናቸውን በማረጋግጥ እና በዓለም ዙሪያ የጤና ውጤቶችን ለማሻሻል ጥቅም ላይ የሚውሉ የሶስት ቢሊዮን ግቦችን መከታተል እና ማፋጠን እና ከጤና ጋር የተገናኙ የዘላቂ ልማት ግቦች ይቆጣጠራሉ። ዶ/ር አስማ እ.ኤ.አ በ2018 የአለም ጤና ድርጅትን ከመቀላቀላቸው በፊት በዩኤስ የበሽታ መቆጣጠሪያ እና መከላከያ ማእከል (CDC) ውስጥ ከሁለት አስርት አመታት በላይ የመሪነት ቦታዎችን ይዘዋል። በጥርስ ቀዶ ጥገና የዶክትሬት ዲግሪ፣ በሕዝብ ጤና ሁለተኛ ዲግሪንም አግኝታለች። ዶ/ር ብሩስ አይልዋርድ እ.ኤ.አ ከግንቦት 4 ቀን 2023 ጀምሮ የአለም አቀፍ የጤና ሽፋን የህይወት ኮርስ ክፍል ረዳት ዋና ዳይሬክተር ሆነው ተሹመዋል።
translations.reference: The portfolios of existing members of the headquarters leadership team: Dr Samira Asma will continue as the Assistant Director-General for Data, Analytics and Delivery for Impact. Dr Asma oversees a portfolio which ensures that health data are reliable and accessible, and are used to improve health outcomes worldwide, including tracking and accelerating progress towards the triple billion targets and the health-related Sustainable Development Goals. Prior to joining WHO in 2018, Dr Asma held leadership positions at the U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) for over two decades. She holds a doctorate degree in dental surgery and a master's degree in public health. Dr Bruce Aylward has been appointed Assistant Director-General of the Universal Health Coverage, Life Course Division as of 4 May 2023.
translations.hypothesis: The leadership team of the organization has announced the following key appointments: Dr. Semira Asma will continue as Deputy Director for Data, Analytics, and Impact. In her expanded role, Dr. Asma will ensure the accuracy and accessibility of health data, oversee the tracking and acceleration of the organization's three billion-dollar goals to improve health outcomes globally, and manage health-related sustainable development goals. Prior to joining the organization in 2018, Dr. Asma held leadership positions at the U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) for over two decades. She holds a doctorate in oral surgery and a master's degree in public health. Dr. Bruce Aylward has been appointed Deputy Director of the Global Health Coverage Life Course Department, effective May 4, 2023.

translations.source: ዶ/ር አይልዋርድ የአለም ጤና ድርጅት የለውጥ አጀንዳን እና የድርጅቱን የባለብዙ ኤጀንሲ የኮቪድ-19 መሳሪያዎች አፋጣኝ (ACT-A) ሀብን በመምራት ላይ ናቸው። ዶ/ር አይልዋርድ በአዲሱ የሥራ ድርሻው የድርጅቱን አጀንዳ በመንዳት የመጀመሪያ ደረጃ የጤና አገልግሎትን እንደ ማዕከላዊ ወደ ሁለንተናዊ የጤና ሽፋን ለመቀየር እንዲሁም የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት በጤና ስርዓቶች፣ በክትባት እና በስነ-ተዋልዶ፣ በእናቶች እና ህጻናት ጤና ላይ የሚሰራውን ስራ ይቆጣጠራል። ዶ/ር አይልዋርድ የሕክምና ዲግሪ እና የማስተርስ በሕዝብ ጤና ዲግሪ አላቸው። ዶክተር ሃናን ባልኪ የፀረ-ተባይ መከላከያ ረዳት ዋና ዳይሬክተር ሆነው ይቀጥላሉ ዶ/ር ባልኪ ከምግብ እና ግብርና ድርጅት፣ ከዓለም የእንስሳት ጤና ድርጅት እና ከተባበሩት መንግስታት የአካባቢ ጥበቃ ፕሮግራም ጋር ባለ ብዙ ዘርፍ ትብብርን ጨምሮ የመድኃኒት የመቋቋም የጤና እና ኢኮኖሚያዊ ሸክሞችን ለመግታት ቴክኒካል እና ፖለቲካዊ አመራር ይሰጣል። ዶ/ር ባልኪ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅትን ከመቀላቀላቸው በፊት በሳዑዲ አረቢያ ብሔራዊ ጥበቃ ሚኒስቴር የኢንፌክሽን መከላከልና መቆጣጠር ዋና ዳይሬክተር ነበሩ።
translations.reference: Dr Aylward has been leading WHO’s Transformation Agenda and the Organization’s work on the multi-agency Access to COVID-19 Tools Accelerator (ACT-A) Hub. In his new role, Dr Aylward will drive the Organization’s agenda to transform primary health care as central to universal health coverage, as well as overseeing WHO’s work on health systems, immunization and reproductive, maternal and child health. Dr Aylward holds a medical degree and a master’s degree in public health.Dr Hanan Balkhy will continue as Assistant Director-General for Antimicrobial Resistance. Dr Balkhy provides technical and political leadership to curb the health and economic burdens of drug resistance, including through multi-sectoral collaboration with the Food and Agriculture Organization, the World Organization for Animal Health, and the United Nations Environment Programme. Prior to joining WHO, Dr Balkhy was Executive Director for Infection Prevention and Control at Saudi Arabia’s Ministry of National Guard.
translations.hypothesis: Dr. Iulwaerd is leading the World Health Organization's (WHO) transformation agenda and overseeing the organization's work on the Access to COVID-19 Tools (ACT-A) initiative. In her new role, Dr. Iulwaerd will focus on driving the organization's agenda, with a primary emphasis on transforming primary healthcare into a central pillar of universal health coverage. She will also oversee the WHO's work in health systems, immunization, and reproductive, maternal, newborn, and child health. Dr. Iulwaerd holds a medical degree and a Master's degree in public health. Dr. Hanan Balki will continue as the Assistant Director-General for Antimicrobial Resistance. Dr. Balki provides technical and political leadership to address the health and economic burden of antimicrobial resistance, including through multi-sectoral collaboration with the Food and Agriculture Organization, the World Animal Health Organization, and the United Nations Environment Programme. Prior to joining the WHO, Dr. Balki was the Director-General for Infection Prevention and Control at the Ministry of National Guard in Saudi Arabia.

translations.source: ዶ/ር ባልኪ በልጆች ተላላፊ በሽታዎች ላይ ልዩ ሙያ ያለው የሕክምና ዲግሪ አለው። ዶ/ር ካታሪና ቦህሜ እ.ኤ.አ ከግንቦት 4 ቀን 2023 ጀምሮ ረዳት ዋና ዳይሬክተር፣ የውጭ ግንኙነት እና አስተዳደር ይሆናሉ። በዚህ ሚና ዶ/ር ቦህሜ የአለም ጤና ድርጅትን ስትራቴጂክ ተሳትፎን በአስተዳደር ፣በሃብት ማሰባሰብ እና የአጋር ግንኙነቶችን ይመራል። የእርሷ የስራ መደብ፣ ወሳኝ የአባል ሀገራት ሂደቶችን ማጠናቀርን ያጠቃልላል፣ ለምሳሌ ስለ ወረርሽኙ ስምምነት ድርድር፣ የአለም ጤና ድርጅት ማሻሻያ እና በዘላቂ ፋይናንስ ላይ ምክሮችን መተግበር ነው። ዶ/ር ቦህሜ የድርጅቱን ስትራቴጂካዊ ራዕይ በመንዳት የድርጅቱ ዋና ዳይሬክተር ጄኔራል ሼፍ ዴ ካቢኔ በመሆን በማገልገል ላይ ትገኝ ነበር።
translations.reference: Dr Balkhy holds a medical degree with specialization in paediatric infectious diseases. Dr Catharina Boehme will become Assistant Director-General, External Relations and Governance as of 4 May 2023. In this role Dr Boehme will lead WHO’s strategic engagement in the areas of governance, resource mobilization and partner relations. Her portfolio will include critical Member States processes, such as their negotiation of a pandemic accord, the reform of WHO’s governance and the implementation of recommendations on sustainable financing. Dr Boehme has been serving as the Director-General’s Chef de Cabinet during which time she drove the leadership’s strategic vision, ensuring alignment across the Organization and with Member States and partners.
translations.hypothesis: Dr. Balki holds a medical degree with a specialization in childhood infectious diseases. Dr. Katarina Bohme will assume the role of Deputy Director-General for External Relations and Administration, effective May 4, 2023. In this role, Dr. Bohme will lead the World Health Organization's strategic engagement in areas such as governance, resource mobilization, and partnerships. Her responsibilities will include finalizing key processes for member states, such as negotiating agreements on pandemic preparedness, implementing WHO reforms, and providing advice on sustainable financing. Dr. Bohme previously served as Director-General of the organization, driving its strategic vision.

translations.source: እ.ኤ.አ በ2021 የአለም ጤና ድርጅትን ከመቀላቀሏ በፊት፣የ ኤፍ.አይ.ኤን.ዲ(FIND) ዋና ስራ አስፈፃሚ በመሆን፣የአለም አቀፍ ጥምረት መርማሪ ነበረች። ዶ/ር ቦህሜ የህክምና ዲግሪ እና በህዝብ ጤና እና አስተዳደር ዲፕሎማ አግኝተዋል። ዶ/ር ቺክዌ ኢኽክዌዙ በድንገተኛ አደጋ ፕሮግራም ውስጥ የጤና ድንገተኛ መረጃ እና ክትትል ስርዓቶች ክፍል ረዳት ዋና ዳይሬክተር ሆነው ይቀጥላሉ። እንደ ስራ ድርሻው አካልም፣ ዶ/ር ኢክክዋዙ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅትን አለምአቀፋዊ ወረርሽኝ እና ክልላዊ ወረርሽኝ ኢንተለጀንስ ይመራሉ፣ አጋሮችን በማሰባሰብ ወደፊት ስለ ወረርሽኙን እና የወረርሽኙን አደጋዎች በተሻለ የመረጃ ተደራሽነት፣ የትንታኔ አቅም እና የውሳኔ ሰጭ መሳሪያዎች ላይ ይሰራሉ። ዶ/ር ኢሂክዋዙ እ.ኤ.አ ከ2016 እስከ 2021 ድረስ የአለም ጤና ድርጅትን ከመቀላቀላቸው በፊት የናይጄሪያ የበሽታ መቆጣጠሪያ ማዕከል (NCDC) የመጀመሪያው ዋና ዳይሬክተር ነበሩ።
translations.reference: She joined WHO in 2021, having been the Chief Executive Officer of FIND, the international alliance for diagnostics. Dr Boehme holds a medical degree and diplomas in public health and management. Dr Chikwe Ihekweazu will continue as Assistant Director-General for the Division of Health Emergency Intelligence and Surveillance Systems in the Emergencies Programme. As part of his portfolio, Dr Ihekweazu leads the WHO Hub for Pandemic and Epidemic Intelligence, bringing together partners to address future pandemic and epidemic risks with better access to data, analytical capacities, and tools for decision-making. Prior to joining WHO, Dr Ihekweazu was the first Director General of the Nigeria Centre for Disease Control (NCDC), from 2016 to 2021.
translations.hypothesis: Prior to joining the World Health Organization (WHO) in 2021, Dr. Boheme served as the Chief Executive Officer of FIND (a global alliance for diagnostics) and was a leading international expert. Dr. Chikwe Ihekweazu will continue as the Assistant Director-General for Health Emergency Intelligence, working within the Health Emergencies Programme. In this role, Dr. Ihekweazu will lead the WHO's global and regional emergency intelligence functions, working with partners to improve access to information, analytical capacity, and decision-making tools related to outbreaks and their risks. Before joining the WHO, Dr. Ihekweazu served as the first Director-General of the Nigeria Centre for Disease Control (NCDC) from 2016 to 2021.

translations.source: ዶ/ር ኢሂክዋዙ የህክምና ዲግሪ አላቸው፣ በልዩ ባለሙያነት እንደ ተላላፊ በሽታ ኤፒዲሚዮሎጂስት እና በሕዝብ ጤና ማስተርስ ዲግሪ አላቸው። ዶ/ር ማይክል ራያን የአለም ጤና ድርጅት(WHO's) የጤና ድንገተኛ አደጋ መርሀ ግብር ዋና ዳይሬክተር ሆነው ይቀጥላሉ። ዶ/ር ራያን በአለም ጤና ድርጅት ውስጥ ላለፉት 25 አመታት የጤና ድንገተኛ አደጋዎችን ሲመሩ የቆዩ ሲሆን በቅርቡም የአለም ጤና ድርጅት ለኮቪድ-19 ወረርሽኝ የድንገተኛ ጊዜ ምላሽን መርተዋል። ለበሽታ ወረርሽኝ፣ ለሰብአዊ ቀውሶች እና ለሌሎች የህዝብ ጤና ድንገተኛ አደጋዎች የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ምላሽንም ይመራል። ዶ/ር ራያን በአለም ዙሪያ በመቶዎች ለሚቆጠሩ የበሽታ ወረርሽኞች ምላሽ የረዳው የአለም አቀፍ ወረርሽኝ ማስጠንቀቂያ እና ምላሽ መረብ (GOARN) መስራች አባል ነው።
translations.reference: Dr Ihekweazu holds a medical degree, with specialization as an infectious disease epidemiologist, and a master’s degree in public health. Dr Michael Ryan will continue as Executive Director of WHO’s Health Emergencies Programme. Dr Ryan has been managing health emergencies in WHO for the past 25 years and most recently led WHO’s emergency response to the COVID-19 pandemic. He leads WHO’s response to disease outbreaks, humanitarian crises and other public health emergencies. Dr Ryan is a founding member of the Global Outbreak Alert and Response Network (GOARN), which has aided the response to hundreds of disease outbreaks around the world.
translations.hypothesis: Dr. Ihekweazu holds a medical degree and is a specialist in infectious disease epidemiology, as well as a Master's degree in public health. Dr. Michael Ryan will continue as the Director of the Health Emergencies Programme at the World Health Organization (WHO). Dr. Ryan has led the WHO's work on health emergencies for the past 25 years and recently oversaw the organization's response to the COVID-19 pandemic. He also leads the WHO's response to outbreaks, humanitarian crises, and other public health emergencies. Dr. Ryan is a founding member of the Global Outbreak Alert and Response Network (GOARN), which has helped to respond to hundreds of outbreaks around the world.

translations.source: ዶ/ር ራያን በህክምና ዲግሪን እና በህዝብ ጤና ሁለተኛ ዲግሪያቸውን አግኝተዋል። ሚስተር ራውል ቶማስ የንግድ ስራዎች ረዳት ዋና ዳይሬክተር ሆነው ይቀጥላሉ። ሚስተር ቶማስ ለ አለም ጤና ድርጅት የንግድ ተግባራት ባጀት፣ ፋይናንስ፣ የሰው ሃይል፣ አስተዳደር፣ ኦዲት፣ የአደጋ አስተዳደር፣ ተጠያቂነት እና ተገዢነት እና አጠቃላይ አስተዳደርን ጨምሮ አጠቃላይ ሃላፊነት አለበት። በድርጅቱ የለውጥ አጀንዳ ውስጥ ያሉትን ብዙ ተነሳሽነቶችን በመተግበር እና የተከበረ፣ ሁሉን አቀፍ የስራ ቦታ የመፍጠር ሃላፊነት አለበት። አቶ ቶማስ ከአለም ጤና ድርጅት ጋር ባሳለፉት 20 አመታት በአፍሪካ፣ በአሜሪካ፣ በምስራቅ ሜዲትራኒያን እና በምዕራብ ፓስፊክ እንዲሁም በአለም አቀፍ የካንሰር ምርምር ኤጀንሲ (IARC) አገልግለዋል።
translations.reference: Dr Ryan holds a degree in medicine and a master’s degree in public health. Mr Raul Thomas will continue as Assistant Director-General for Business Operations. Mr Thomas has overall responsibility for WHO’s business functions, including budget, finance, human resources, administration, audit, risk management, accountability and compliance, and general management. He is responsible for implementing many of the initiatives under the Organization’s transformation agenda and creating a respectful, inclusive workplace.
